13658.title: ACM MM 2026 Grand Challenge: Machine-oriented Visual Media Quality Assessment
13658.description: This competition evaluates the visual robustness of Vision-Language Models (VLMs) in embodied scenarios. In the Preliminary Phase, participants predict performance degradation on 12,400 distorted images (IQA). The Final Phase will escalate to real-world robot data. Join us to build more reliable eyes for embodied agents!
13658.description_zh: 这项竞赛评估视觉语言模型(VLMs)在具身场景中的视觉鲁棒性。在初赛阶段，参赛者需要对12,400张失真图像(IQA)的性能下降进行预测。决赛阶段将升级为真实机器人数据。加入我们，为具身智能体构建更可靠的视觉系统！

pages.title: Overview
pages.content:
# Machine-oriented Visual Media Quality Assessment
Welcome to the **Machine-oriented Visual Media Quality Assessment**！ This competition is dedicated to exploring and enhancing the visual robustness of Vision-Language Models (VLMs) within complex, real-world physical environments. ## Background With the development of Embodied AI, machines have replaced humans as the main consumers of visual media, yet existing Image Quality Assessment (IQA) metrics remain focused on human and overlooked machine task utility. To address this gap, we introduce the Machine-oriented Image Quality Assessment (MoIQA) Challenge, emphasizing simulation comprehension and Real-world exectution. This challenge aims to advance reliable image understanding for machine preference and support robust Embodied AI applications. --- ## Associated Workshop & Challenge This competition is a core track of the **ACM MM 2026 Grand Challenge**. We invite researchers and practitioners to tackle the robustness challenges of Vision-Language Models in embodied AI. * **Official Grand Challenge Site**: [ACM MM 2026 Grand Challenges](https://2026.acmmm.org/site/grand-challenges.html) * **Challenge Homepage**: [M-VQA Challenge](https://m-vqa-challenge.github.io/M-VQA/) *** ## Competition Structure & Core Tasks The competition is organized into three distinct tracks, spanning from synthetic robustness evaluation to real-world robotic deployment. ### Track 1: Absolute Performance Prediction (In-Silico) Participants must predict the absolute accuracy (0.0 to 1.0) of VLM models on a large-scale dataset containing 12,400 images with controlled noise. The goal is to accurately estimate the model's task-solving capability under various distortions. ### Track 2: Relative Degradation Assessment (In-Silico) Participants are tasked with predicting the relative performance drop of VLM models compared to their baseline on original, clean images. This track focuses on the sensitivity of metrics to increasing levels of image degradation. ### Track 3: Real-World Robot Deployment (Real-Robot) Starting May 1, 2026, top-performing models will undergo rigorous evaluation on physical robot platforms. Specifically, we will conduct systematic assessments on data collected from 100 robotic arms operating within a standardized experimental setup. * **Eligibility**: Only the top 10 teams from the combined rankings of Track 1 and Track 2 are eligible to participate. * **Evaluation Protocol**: Direct testing on real-world sensor data collected from laboratory robot platforms, with no prior training phase. Performance will be measured by assessing the consistency between predicted quality scores and binary success/failure labels (0/1) derived from task execution outcomes. --- ## Phase Details ### Preliminary Round: Development & Challenge * **Status**: Open. * **Objective**: Evaluation for Track 1 and Track 2 based on the released Training and Validation datasets. Participants must submit their predictions to the CodaBench platform for ranking. ### Final Round: Sim-to-Real * **Status**: Invitation Only. * **Objective**: Evaluation for Track 3. Qualified teams will deploy their algorithms on private, real-world robotic data to test generalization in physical environments. --- ## Awards & Publications * **Awards**: Prizes will be awarded to the Top 3 winners of each individual track (Track 1, Track 2, and Track 3). * **Publications**: The final evaluation for paper acceptance and official competition reports will be based on the **comprehensive performance across all three tracks**. --- ## Evaluation Metrics For the **Preliminary Round**, submissions are evaluated based on their correlation with Ground Truth scores derived from 11 state-of-the-art VLMs. * **Absolute Score**: Predicting the mean performance level (Mean Opinion Score) of models on a specific distorted image. * **Relative Score**: Measuring the ratio of performance on a distorted image compared to its original, clean counterpart (Ratio of Means). The final ranking depends on the consistency (PLCC and SRCC) between the predicted scores and the ground truth. --- ## Contact For any inquiries, please reach out to: * **Email**: lcysyzxdxc@sjtu.edu.cn
pages.content_zh:
# 面向机器的视觉媒体质量评估
欢迎来到**面向机器的视觉媒体质量评估**竞赛！本竞赛致力于探索和增强视觉语言模型（VLMs）在复杂真实物理环境中的视觉鲁棒性。 ## 背景 随着具身人工智能的发展，机器已取代人类成为视觉媒体的主要消费者，然而现有的图像质量评估（IQA）指标仍然专注于人类视觉，忽视了机器任务效用。为了弥补这一空白，我们推出了面向机器的图像质量评估（MoIQA）挑战赛，强调仿真理解和真实世界执行。本竞赛旨在推进机器偏好的可靠图像理解，支持强大的具身人工智能应用。 --- ## 相关研讨会与挑战赛 本竞赛是**ACM MM 2026大奖赛**的核心赛道。我们邀请研究人员和从业者应对具身人工智能中视觉语言模型的鲁棒性挑战。 * **官方大奖赛网站**: [ACM MM 2026 Grand Challenges](https://2026.acmmm.org/site/grand-challenges.html) * **挑战赛主页**: [M-VQA Challenge](https://m-vqa-challenge.github.io/M-VQA/) *** ## 竞赛结构与核心任务 竞赛分为三个不同的赛道，涵盖从合成鲁棒性评估到真实机器人部署的全过程。 ### 赛道1：绝对性能预测（计算机模拟） 参赛者需要预测视觉语言模型在包含12,400张受控噪声图像的大规模数据集上的绝对准确度（0.0到1.0）。目标是准确估计模型在各种失真条件下的任务求解能力。 ### 赛道2：相对性能衰减评估（计算机模拟） 参赛者需要预测视觉语言模型与原始清晰图像基线相比的相对性能下降。本赛道重点关注指标对图像降质增加水平的敏感性。 ### 赛道3：真实机器人部署（实体机器人） 自2026年5月1日起，表现最佳的模型将在物理机器人平台上接受严格评估。具体而言，我们将对在标准化实验设置中运行的100个机械臂收集的数据进行系统评估。 * **参赛资格**: 仅限赛道1和赛道2综合排名前10的团队参与。 * **评估协议**: 直接在实验室机器人平台收集的真实世界传感器数据上进行测试，无先前训练阶段。性能将通过评估预测质量分数与从任务执行结果衍生的二元成功/失败标签（0/1）之间的一致性来衡量。 --- ## 阶段详情 ### 初赛：开发与挑战 * **状态**: 开放。 * **目标**: 基于发布的训练和验证数据集，对赛道1和赛道2进行评估。参赛者必须向CodaBench平台提交其预测结果以获得排名。 ### 决赛：仿真到真实 * **状态**: 邀请制。 * **目标**: 赛道3的评估。合格团队将在私有真实机器人数据上部署其算法，以测试在物理环境中的泛化能力。 --- ## 奖项与论文发表 * **奖项**: 每个独立赛道（赛道1、赛道2和赛道3）的前3名获胜者将获得奖励。 * **论文发表**: 论文接收的最终评估和官方竞赛报告将基于**所有三个赛道的综合性能**。 --- ## 评估指标 对于th

pages.title: Dataset
pages.content:
## 1. Download Data ### **Part A: Training Dataset** * **Content**: `public_train.tsv` * **Link**: [Primary Link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/111D1HAxacArbDAxjaJ56bPza4V9j2_T1/view?usp=drive_link) or [Backup Link](https://www.codabench.org/datasets/575587/) ### **Part B: Validation Dataset** * **Content**: `public_val.tsv` * **Link**: [Primary Link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1StOa9abHPD1a8FMPBP4-aYNeJ-szlhKg/view?usp=drive_link) or [Backup Link](https://www.codabench.org/datasets/575724/) ### **Part C: Test Dataset** * **Content**: `public_test.tsv` * **Link**: [Link for Public Test Data](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1N_zGyzVpKG97XHtcZzJw1WXI8pXFsVSh/view?usp=sharing) > > **Test Phase Submission Format Example** > > Download the **[Test Submission Template](https://www.codabench.org/datasets/download/49e7804e-26da-46d9-b940-a4ea91452d28/)** to see a working example of the **test phase** submission structure and a random score generator. --- ## 2. Dataset Statistics The dataset consists of **12,400 images** in total, divided into three splits. Each original image is associated with 30 distorted variations.
Split Original Images Distorted Images Total Role
Train 280
(img_000 - img_279) 		8,400
(img_000_noise01 - img_279_noise30) 		8,680 Model Training & Fine-tuning
Val 40
(img_280 - img_319) 		1,200
(img_280_noise01 - img_319_noise30) 		1,240 Model Selection & Debugging
Test 80
(img_320 - img_399) 		2,400
(img_320_noise01 - img_399_noise30) 		2,480 Competition Evaluation
Total 400 12,000 12,400 -
   **Distortion Types**: 30 distinct types. **Severity Levels**: 5 levels (Level 4 to Level 8). --- ## 3. Data Format All data files are provided in **TSV** format. * **Encoding**: Images are encoded as **Base64 strings**. * **Naming Convention**: Original Image Index: `img_000` to `img_399`. Distorted Image Index: `img_XXX_noiseYY` (e.g., `img_320_noise01`). ### Field Definitions 1. Common Fields (Present in All Files) * `index`: Unique identifier for the image (String). * `image`: Base64 encoded image string. * `question`: The visual question associated with the image. 2. Training Fields (Available in `train`) * `answer`: Ground truth text answer to the visual question. * `noise_type`: Integer ID (01-30) representing the distortion type. * `noise_level`: Integer (4-8) representing the distortion severity. * **`absolute_score_gt`**: **[Target]** The ground truth Absolute Score. * **Definition**: Represents the overall performance or accuracy of Vision-Language Models (VLMs) on a specific image (whether clean or distorted). It reflects the absolute semantic understandability of the visual content. * **Range**: **[0.0, 1.0]**. A score of `1.0` indicates perfect performance, while `0.0` indicates complete failure. * **`relative_score_gt`**: **[Target]** The ground truth Relative Score. * **Definition**: Represents the performance degradation ratio caused by image distortions. * **Range**: **[0.0, 1.0]**. A score of `1.0` indicates **zero performance degradation** . Lower scores indicate more severe performance drops due to the distortion. 3. Test Fields (Available in `val`/`test`) * Only `index`, `image`, and `question` are provided. * Participants must predict the `absolute_score` and `relative_score`. ## 4. Additional Training Data * **Simulation Track:** [MPD Dataset](https://github.com/lcysyzxdxc/MPD) * **Real-Robot Track:** [EmbodiedIQA Dataset](https://github.com/aiben-ch/EmbodiedIQA) *If you use these datasets, please adhere to the following citation formats:* **Simulation Data Citation:** ```bibtex @inproceedings{simdata, author = {Li, Chunyi and Tian, Yuan and Ling, Xiaoyue and Zhang, Zicheng and Duan, Haodong and Wu, Haoning and Jia, Ziheng and Liu, Xiaohong and Min, Xiongkuo and Lu, Guo and Lin, Weisi and Zhai, Guangtao}, title = {Image Quality Assessment: From Human to Machine Preference}, booktitle = {Proceedings of the IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)}, month = {June}, year = {2025}, pages = {7570-7581} } ``` **Real-Robot Data Citation:** ```bibtex @inproceedings{realdata, title={Image Quality Assessment for Embodied {AI}}, author={Chunyi Li and Jiahao Xiao and Jianbo Zhang and Farong Wen and Zicheng Zhang and Yuan Tian and Xiangyang Zhu and Xiaohong Liu and Zhengxue Cheng and Weisi Lin and Guangtao Zhai}, booktitle={The Fourteenth International Conference on Learning Representations}, year={2026}, url={https://openreview.net/forum?id=azj53PLJRL} } ```

pages.title: Evaluation & Submission
pages.content:
## 1. Submission Format To participate in the Preliminary Round, you must submit a **ZIP file** containing a single TSV file named **`submission.tsv`**. The file must follow the specific formatting and column requirements. **File Structure:** * **Archive**: `submission.zip` * **Content**: `submission.tsv` **TSV Header Requirements:** The TSV file must include the following three columns: 1. **index**: The unique identifier for each image. 2. **abs_score**: Your predicted Absolute Score (floating-point value). 3. **rel_score**: Your predicted Relative Score (floating-point value). **Example TSV Content:**
index abs_score rel_score
img_320 0.9850 1.0000
img_320_noise01 0.7520 0.8264
img_320_noise02 0.4510 0.7612
... ... ...
> **Test Phase Submission Format Example** > > Download the **[Test Submission Template](https://www.codabench.org/datasets/download/49e7804e-26da-46d9-b940-a4ea91452d28/)** to see a working example of the **test phase** submission structure and a random score generator. --- ## 2. Evaluation Metrics Submissions are evaluated based on their correlation with the Ground Truth (GT) scores. We use two primary statistical metrics to assess the accuracy of your predictions: * **Pearson Linear Correlation Coefficient (PLCC)**: Measures the linear relationship between the predicted scores and the ground truth. * **Spearman Rank-Order Correlation Coefficient (SRCC)**: Measures the monotonic relationship (ranking consistency) between the predicted scores and the ground truth. --- ## 3. Leaderboard Ranking The competition features two parallel tracks. * **Track 1: Absolute Score (Robustness Level)** * Evaluated using the mean of PLCC and SRCC on the absolute score predictions. * **Track 2: Relative Score (Performance Degradation)** * Evaluated using the mean of PLCC and SRCC on the relative score predictions. --- ## 4. Important Notes * **Consistency**: Ensure the `index` column matches the Test Set exactly. Any missing rows or mismatched indices will result in a submission error. * **Encoding**: Do not include Base64 image data in your submission; only the predicted scores are required. * **Precision**: We recommend providing scores with at least 4 decimal places for better ranking granularity.
pages.content_zh:
## 1. 提交格式 要参加初赛，您必须提交一个**ZIP文件**，其中包含一个名为**`submission.tsv`**的TSV文件。该文件必须遵循特定的格式和列要求。 **文件结构：** * **压缩包**: `submission.zip` * **内容**: `submission.tsv` **TSV文件头要求：** TSV文件必须包含以下三列： 1. **index**: 每张图像的唯一标识符。 2. **abs_score**: 您预测的绝对分数（浮点数值）。 3. **rel_score**: 您预测的相对分数（浮点数值）。 **TSV文件内容示例：**
index abs_score rel_score
img_320 0.9850 1.0000
img_320_noise01 0.7520 0.8264
img_320_noise02 0.4510 0.7612
... ... ...
> **测试阶段提交格式示例** > > 下载**[测试提交模板](https://www.codabench.org/datasets/download/49e7804e-26da-46d9-b940-a4ea91452d28/)**以查看**测试阶段**提交结构的工作示例和随机分数生成器。 --- ## 2. 评估指标 提交内容根据与真实值(GT)分数的相关性进行评估。我们使用两个主要统计指标来评估您预测的准确性： * **皮尔逊线性相关系数(PLCC)**: 衡量预测分数与真实值之间的线性关系。 * **斯皮尔曼秩相关系数(SRCC)**: 衡量预测分数与真实值之间的单调关系（排序一致性）。 --- ## 3. 排行榜排名 竞赛包含两个平行赛道。 * **赛道1：绝对分数（鲁棒性等级）** * 使用绝对分数预测的PLCC和SRCC的平均值进行评估。 * **赛道2：相对分数（性能下降）** * 使用相对分数预测的PLCC和SRCC的平均值进行评估。 --- ## 4. 重要提示 * **一致性**: 确保`index`列与测试集完全匹配。任何缺失的行或不匹配的索引都将导致提交错误。 * **编码**: 不要在您的提交中包含Base64图像数据；仅需提供预测的分数。 * **精度**: 我们建议提供至少4位小数的分数，以获得更好的排名精确度。

15281.description_zh: 该网站用于研究。竞赛已结束。

pages.title: overview
pages.content:
NTIRE Workshop and Challenges @ CVPR 2026

 

The Second Challenge on Day and Night Raindrop Removal for Dual-Focused Images

Important dates

  • 2026.01.15 Release of train data (input and output)
  • 2026.01.15 Release of validation data (inputs only)
  • 2026.01.30 Validation server online
  • 2026.03.13 Final test data release (inputs only)
  • 2026.03.17 Test output results submission deadline
  • 2026.03.17 Fact sheets and code/executable submission deadline
  • 2026.03.19 Preliminary test results release to the participants
  • 2026.03.24 Paper submission deadline for entries from the challenge
  • 2026.06.x NTIRE workshop and challenges, results and award ceremony (CVPR 2026, USA)

 

Challenge overview

The 11th edition of NTIRE: New Trends in Image Restoration and Enhancement workshop will be held on June 17th, 2026 in conjunction with CVPR 2026.

Image manipulation is a key computer vision tasks, aiming at the restoration of degraded image content, the filling in of missing information, or the needed transformation and/or manipulation to achieve a desired target (with respect to perceptual quality, contents, or performance of apps working on such images). Recent years have witnessed an increased interest from the vision and graphics communities in these fundamental topics of research. Not only has there been a constantly growing flow of related papers, but also substantial progress has been achieved.

Each step forward eases the use of images by people or computers for the fulfillment of further tasks, as image manipulation serves as an important front end. Not surprisingly then, there is an ever-growing range of applications in fields such as surveillance, the automotive industry, electronics, remote sensing, or medical image analysis etc. The emergence and ubiquitous use of mobile and wearable devices offer another fertile ground for additional applications and faster methods.

This workshop aims to provide an overview of the new trends and advances in those areas. Moreover, it will offer an opportunity for academic and industrial attendees to interact and explore collaborations.

 

Motivation for this competition

Raindrops on camera lenses or windshields significantly reduce image visibility in human life, posing challenges for applications like surveillance and autonomous driving. Effective raindrop removal is crucial to ensuring reliable performance in these systems. However, most deraining datasets overlooked the complex environment in real life. In particular, there are few raindrop-focused datasets, and most existing datasets focus on capturing background scenes while the camera is focused on the background. Meanwhile, these datasets primarily target daytime scenarios, with limited attention to nighttime conditions.

To promote the development of deraining, we introduce the first large-scale real-world raindrop-clarity dataset, which includes raindrop-focused images and nighttime scenes. Based on this dataset, we have the first NTIRE Challenge on Day and Night Raindrop Removal for Dual-Focused Images, jointly with the NTIRE workshop.

The goal of this competition is to establish a new and applicable benchmark for the Day and Night Raindrop Removal for Dual-Focused Images. We are looking forward to the collaborative efforts of our participants, aiming to elevate the quality of training images. The final ranking will be made based on PSNR, SSIM, and LPIPS.

 

The top-ranked participants will be awarded and invited to follow the CVPR submission guide for workshops to describe their solution and to submit to the associated NTIRE workshop at CVPR 2026.

More details are found in the competition data section.

Competition

The training data has already been made available to the registered participants.

Provided Resources

  • Scripts: With the dataset the organizers will provide scripts to facilitate the reproducibility of the images and performance evaluation results after the validation server is online. More information is provided on the data page.
  • Contact: You can use the forum on the data description page (highly recommended!) or directly contact the challenge organizers by email (xin.li@ustc.edu.cn, jinyeying@u.nus.edu, jinxin@eitech.edu.cn, zongwei.wu@uni-wuerzburg.de, lbc31415926@mail.ustc.edu.cn and Radu.Timofte@uni-wuerzburg.de) if you have doubts or any questions.
pages.content_zh:
NTIRE研讨会和挑战赛 @ CVPR 2026

 

第二届双焦点图像日夜雨滴去除挑战赛

重要日期

  • 2026.01.15 发布训练数据（输入和输出）
  • 2026.01.15 发布验证数据（仅输入）
  • 2026.01.30 验证服务器上线
  • 2026.03.13 发布最终测试数据（仅输入）
  • 2026.03.17 测试输出结果提交截止日期
  • 2026.03.17 事实表和代码/可执行文件提交截止日期
  • 2026.03.19 向参赛者发布初步测试结果
  • 2026.03.24 来自挑战赛的论文提交截止日期
  • 2026.06.x NTIRE研讨会和挑战赛、结果和颁奖典礼（CVPR 2026，美国）

 

挑战赛概述

第11届NTIRE：图像复原与增强新趋势研讨会将于2026年6月17日与CVPR 2026联合举办。

图像处理是计算机视觉的关键任务，旨在恢复退化的图像内容、填补缺失信息或进行必要的变换和/或操作以实现所需的目标（涉及感知质量、内容或在此类图像上工作的应用程序性能）。近年来，视觉和图形社区对这些基础研究课题的兴趣不断增加。相关论文的发表数量持续增长，并取得了实质性进展。

每一步进展都为人们或计算机使用图像完成进一步任务提供了便利，因为图像处理是一个重要的前端。不足为奇的是，在监控、汽车工业、电子学、遥感或医学图像分析等领域存在着越来越多的应用。移动设备和可穿戴设备的出现和广泛使用为额外应用和更快方法提供了另一片沃土。

本研讨会旨在提供这些领域新趋势和进展的概览。此外，它将为学术界和工业界的与会者提供互动和探索合作的机会。

 

竞赛的动机

附着在摄像机镜头或挡风玻璃上的雨滴会显著降低人类生活中的图像可见性，对监控和自动驾驶等应用构成挑战。有效的雨滴去除对于确保这些系统的可靠性能至关重要。然而，大多数去雨数据集忽略了现实生活中的复杂环境。特别是，很少有针对雨滴的数据集，大多数现有数据集集中于捕捉背景场景，而摄像机对背景进行聚焦。同时，这些数据集主要针对白天场景，对夜间条件的关注有限。

为了促进去雨技术的发展，我们引入了首个大规模真实世界雨滴清晰度数据集，其中包括以雨滴为焦点的图像和夜间场景。基于该数据集，我们与NTIRE研讨会联合举办首届NTIRE双焦点图像日夜雨滴去除挑战赛。

本竞赛的目标是为双焦点图像日夜雨滴去除建立新的和实用的基准。我们期待参赛者的共同努力，旨在提高训练图像的质量。最终排名将基于PSNR、SSIM和LPIPS进行。

pages.title: data
pages.content:
NTIRE Workshop and Challenges @ CVPR 2026

 

The Second Challenge on Day and Night Raindrop Removal for Dual-Focused Images

 

Data overview

The dataset named RainDrop Clarity is contributed by Yeying Jin et al (NUS). The dataset includes both daytime and nighttime scenes for training and testing. (i) The daytime raindrop dataset contains a total of 5,442 paired or triplet images, with 4,713 pairs used for training and the remaining 729 pairs for testing. (ii) The nighttime raindrop dataset consists of 9,744 paired or triplet images, where 8,655 pairs are allocated to the training set, and the remaining 1,089 pairs are reserved for the validation and test set.

In this competition, the used dataset is composed of:

  • train data: A total of 14,139 day and night dual-focused raindrop images with blurry backgrounds and their corresponding clear backgrounds are provided.
  • validation data: A total of 240 day and night rainy images are provided. 
  • test data: A total of 731 day and night rainy images are provided.

 

Data access

By accessing the data the participants implicitly agree with the terms and conditions of the challenge.

 

    • Development phase / Learning:
      • Train Data, please download day rainy data and night rainy data with Google drive.
      • Validation Data (only input images), has been released
      • Test Data (only input images), has be released with Google drive "We have released the test input with google drive "https://drive.google.com/file/d/1E82JAS7bSt73pXA5cWoaoWyan0WKaZyp/view?usp=sharing" The" !!!

 

Final test phase submission guidelines (Factsheet can be downloaded from here)

The final submission should be emailed to xin.li@mail.ustc.edu.cn; yshustc@mail.ustc.edu.cn; jinyeying@u.nus.edu; Radu.Timofte@uni-wuerzburg.de

The title of the mail should be:  [COMPETITION_NAME] - [TEAM_NAME]

The body of the mail shall include the following:

      1. the challenge full name
      2. team name
      3. team leader and all members' names, email addresses and institutes.
      4. team members with NTIRE2026 sponsors
      5. team name and user names on NTIRE2026 Codabench competitions
      6. executable/source code attached or download links
      7. factsheet attached (you should use the updated factsheet of the competition!)
      8. download link(s) to the FULL results of ALL of the test frames (corresponding to the last entry in the online Codalab test server and provided codes/exe)

The executable/source code should include trained models or necessary parameters so that we could run it and reproduce results. There should be a README or descriptions that explains how to execute the executable/code. Factsheet must be a compiled pdf file and a zip with the corresponding .tex source files. Please provide a detailed explanation.

Note that you can still submit to the validation server in those competitions for which the validation ground truth images were not released. 

 

Final test phase - preliminary results [TBA]

Here are the preliminary results: <>

Note that changes are likely to happen, we are still checking the submissions with their results. If your entry is not correctly reported please contact asap the challenge organizers. Depending on the submitted factsheets/reproducibility and results many participants will be invited next week to co-author and edit the challenge report.

Note that all the participants with interesting or top ranked solutions are invited to contribute a paper to the NTIRE 2026 workshop. However, the paper will be double-blind peer reviewed for contributions (a top ranking solution is in itself a contribution) and scientific quality as any other CVPR or NTIRE paper. Submission deadline for challenge papers follows the NTIRE2026. Check NTIRE 2026 website for more info.
pages.content_zh:
NTIRE 2026研讨会和挑战赛 @ CVPR 2026

 

第二届日夜雨滴去除双焦点图像挑战赛

 

数据概览

数据集名称为RainDrop Clarity，由新加坡国立大学(NUS)的Yeying Jin等人贡献。该数据集包含训练和测试的日间和夜间场景。(i)日间雨滴数据集包含总共5,442对或三元组图像，其中4,713对用于训练，剩余729对用于测试。(ii)夜间雨滴数据集包含9,744对或三元组图像，其中8,655对分配给训练集，剩余1,089对保留用于验证和测试集。

在本次竞赛中，使用的数据集由以下部分组成：

  • 训练数据：总共14,139张日夜双焦点雨滴图像及其对应的清晰背景。
  • 验证数据：总共240张日夜雨天图像。
  • 测试数据：总共731张日夜雨天图像。

 

数据获取

pages.title: evaluation
pages.content:
NTIRE Workshop and Challenges @ CVPR 2026

 

The Second Challenge on Day and Night Raindrop Removal for Dual-Focused Images

 

Evaluation

The evaluation consists of the comparison of the restored images with the reference ground truth images. Please inference all images with the same model (do not distinguish day and night).

The evaluation consists of the comparison of PSNR, SSIM, and LPIPS.

For the final results, we will compute it by weighting the above metric as the initial results: final_score = PSNR (Y) + 10*SSIM (Y) - 5*LPIPS.

For submitting the results, you need to follow these steps:

  1. process the input images and keep the same name for the output image results as produced by your method (example: for an input file with the name "00063.png" the output file should be "00063.png") Note that the output images should have the size of the input images.  The submission example can be found in "https://drive.google.com/file/d/1LTGEuGxU0bXOv--pLcj0VJj-S2JoM2PK/view?usp=sharing". 
  2. create a ZIP archive containing the files and a readme.txt  Note that the archive should not include folders, all the files should be in the root of the archive.
  3. the readme.txt file should contain the following lines filled in with the runtime per image (in seconds) of the solution, 1 or 0 accordingly if employs CPU or GPU at runtime, and 1 or 0 if employs extra data for training the models or not.
    runtime per image [s] : 10.43 
    CPU[1] / GPU[0] : 1
    Extra Data [1] / No Extra Data [0] : 1
    Other description: Solution based on A+ of Timofte et al. ACCV 2014. We have a Matlab/C++ implementation, and report single core CPU runtime. The method was trained on Train 91 of Yang et al. and BSDS 200 of the Berkeley segmentation dataset. 
      The last part of the file can have any description you want about the code producing the provided results (dependencies, link, scripts, etc.)
    The provided information is very important both during the validation period when different teams can compare their results / solutions but also for establishing the final ranking of the teams and their methods.
pages.content_zh:
NTIRE 研讨会和挑战赛 @ CVPR 2026

 

第二届日夜雨滴去除双焦点图像挑战赛

 

评估

评估包括将恢复的图像与参考真值图像进行比较。请使用同一模型对所有图像进行推理（不区分日间和夜间）。

评估包括PSNR、SSIM和LPIPS的比较。

对于最终结果，我们将通过对上述指标进行加权来计算，作为初始结果：final_score = PSNR (Y) + 10*SSIM (Y) - 5*LPIPS。

提交结果时，需要按照以下步骤操作：

  1. 处理输入图像，并为方法生成的输出图像结果保持与输入相同的名称（示例：输入文件名为"00063.png"时，输出文件应为"00063.png"）。注意输出图像的尺寸应与输入图像相同。提交示例可在"https://drive.google.com/file/d/1LTGEuGxU0bXOv--pLcj0VJj-S2JoM2PK/view?usp=sharing"中找到。
  2. 创建包含文件和readme.txt的ZIP压缩包。注意压缩包不应包含文件夹，所有文件应位于压缩包的根目录中。
  3. readme.txt文件应包含以下行，并填入解决方案的每张图像运行时间（以秒为单位），根据运行时是否使用CPU或GPU分别填1或0，以及是否使用额外数据进行模型训练分别填1或0。
    runtime per image [s] : 10.43 
    CPU[1] / GPU[0] : 1
    Extra Data [1] / No Extra Data [0] : 1
    Other description: Solution based on A+ of Timofte et al. ACCV 2014. We have a Matlab/C++ implementation, and report single core CPU runtime. The method was trained on Train 91 of Yang et al. and BSDS 200 of the Berkeley segmentation dataset. 
      文件的最后部分可包含您想要的任何关于生成所提供结果的代码描述（依赖项、链接、脚本等）。
    所提供的信息非常重要，既可用于验证期间不同团队比较其结果/解决方案，也可用于确定团队及其方法的最终排名。

14618.description: Classification of YouTube video's title and description into 12 classes.
14618.description_zh: YouTube视频标题和描述分类到12个类别。

pages.title: Participation
pages.content:
# How to participate

You should submit an untrained model in a python file `model.py` which contains
your `class Model`, which will be imported, trained, and tested on Codalab.

The overall dataset is divided into training/validation/test as a 80/5/15 split.

You can download the input_data, which contains both the training and validation dataset. Although the competition will only run training and test dataset's, the validation dataset is provided for competitors to finetune their models on their end.

See the "Seed" page for the outline of a `Model` class, with the expected
function names.

See the "Timeline" page for additional information about the phases of this
competition
pages.content_zh:
# 如何参与

您应该在Python文件 `model.py` 中提交一个未训练的模型，该文件包含您的 `class Model`，将在Codalab上导入、训练和测试。

整体数据集按80/5/15的比例分为训练集/验证集/测试集。

您可以下载input_data，其中包含训练数据集和验证数据集。虽然竞赛仅在训练和测试数据集上运行，但验证数据集是为参赛者在本地微调模型而提供的。

有关 `Model` 类的概述和预期的函数名称，请查看"Seed"页面。

有关竞赛各个阶段的更多信息，请查看"Timeline"页面。

pages.title: Data
pages.content:
# Dataset and Ground Truth
Our dataset consists of YouTube video titles and descriptions.
The task is a supervised multinomial classification problem: given a title and description, the model predicts
the video’s primary category from 12 total categories.
* Food & Cooking
* Education
* News
* Video Games
* Shopping
* Health & Fitness
* Sports & Games
* Vehicles
* Business
* Music
* Lifestyle
* Digital Media

Altogether, the dataset consists of 1851 manually annotated data points, with the **ground truth** being the majority vote across the three annotators.

![Count of Each Category](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thaafei/YouTubeCategories-4NL3/master/Count%20of%20Each%20Category.png)

To summarize, **Education** is the most common category, with 25.3% of all data being labeled as it in our dataset. The classes are not balanced, with two categories, Education and Lifestyle, accounting for 46% of the total dataset.

This makes sense, as they are one of the biggest genres on YouTube itself, and the original, unlabeled dataset we based our annotated one off of consists of videos sourced from only 50 channels.

To see more details about the dataset, how our team labeled the data, and some sample data points, [see the progress report](https://github.com/thaafei/YouTubeCategories-4NL3/blob/master/Step1/step_1.pdf) .
pages.content_zh:
# 数据集和基准真值

我们的数据集由YouTube视频标题和描述组成。
该任务是一个监督多项分类问题：给定标题和描述，模型从总共12个类别中预测视频的主要类别。
* 食品与烹饪
* 教育
* 新闻
* 视频游戏
* 购物
* 健康与健身
* 运动与游戏
* 车辆
* 商业
* 音乐
* 生活方式
* 数字媒体

总共，该数据集包含1851个手工标注的数据点，**基准真值**为三位标注员的多数投票。

![各类别计数](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thaafei/YouTubeCategories-4NL3/master/Count%20of%20Each%20Category.png)

总结一下，**教育**是最常见的类别，占我们数据集中所有数据的25.3%。各类别分布不均衡，教育和生活方式两个类别占数据集总量的46%。

这是合理的，因为这两个类别是YouTube上最大的类型之一，而我们基于的原始未标注数据集仅由来自50个频道的视频组成。

有关数据集的更多详情、我们团队的标注方法和一些样本数据点，请[查看进度报告](https://github.com/thaafei/YouTubeCategories-4NL3/blob/master/Step1/step_1.pdf)。

14569.title: Diabetic Retinopathy Detection
14569.description: Multilabel classification of retinal images into 5 severity levels.
14569.description_zh: 视网膜图像的多标签分类，分为5个严重程度等级。

pages.title: Overview
pages.content:
Diabetic retinopathy detection challenge

Classify retinal fundus images into 5 severity levels of diabetic retinopathy using machine learning.

Objective

Diabetic retinopathy (DR) is a diabetes complication that damages the blood vessels of the retina. It is a leading cause of blindness worldwide, affecting over 100 million people. Early detection through automated screening of retinal images can drastically improve patient outcomes.

Your goal is to build a model that takes a retinal fundus photograph as input and predicts which severity level of DR it corresponds to.

Severity levels

Label Severity Description
0 No DR Healthy retina, no signs of diabetic retinopathy
1 Mild Microaneurysms only
2 Moderate More than just microaneurysms, but less than severe
3 Severe Extensive intraretinal hemorrhages, venous beading
4 Proliferative DR Neovascularization and/or vitreous hemorrhage

Dataset

The dataset consists of 510 retinal fundus images from the IDRiD (Indian Diabetic Retinopathy Image Dataset), available under the CC BY 4.0 license.

  • Training set: 407 images with labels
  • Public test set (Development Phase): ~50 images
  • Private test set (Final Phase): ~50 images

Labels are provided as one-hot encoded vectors of length 5 (columns 0 to 4). Each image belongs to exactly one severity class.

Evaluation metrics

Submissions are evaluated on the following metrics:

Metric Description Direction
F1 Macro Average F1 score across all classes (treats each class equally) ↑ Higher is better
F1 Micro Global F1 computed from total TP, FP, FN (favors majority classes) ↑ Higher is better
Hamming Loss Fraction of incorrectly predicted labels ↓ Lower is better
Runtime (s) Total execution time (training + inference) ↓ Lower is better

Predictions are thresholded at 0.5 to produce binary labels before computing F1 and Hamming Loss.

How to participate

  1. Download the starting kit notebook (starting_kit.ipynb) from the GitHub repository : here.
  2. Explore the data and understand the evaluation metrics.
  3. Implement your model in a submission.py file following the required interface:
    • A get_model() function that returns a model object
    • The model must have fit(X_train, y_train, train_img_dir) and predict(X_test, test_img_dir) methods
    • predict should return a numpy array of shape (n_samples, 5) with values in [0, 1]
  4. Zip your submission.py and upload it to CodaBench.

Rules

Up to 100 submissions total (10 per day).

References

  • IDRiD Dataset (Roboflow)
  • Scikit-learn F1 Score Documentation
pages.content_zh:
# 糖尿病视网膜病变检测挑战赛

使用机器学习将视网膜眼底图像分类为**5个严重程度级别**的糖尿病视网膜病变。

---

## 目标

**糖尿病视网膜病变 (DR)** 是一种糖尿病并发症，会损害视网膜的血管。它是全球失明的主要原因，影响超过**1亿人**。通过自动化视网膜图像筛查进行早期检测可以显著改善患者预后。

您的目标是构建一个模型，该模型以**视网膜眼底照片**作为输入，预测其对应的**严重程度级别**。

## 严重程度级别

| 标签 | 严重程度 | 描述 |
|:----:|---------|------|
| **0** | 无DR | 健康视网膜，无糖尿病视网膜病变迹象 |
| **1** | 轻度 | 仅微动脉瘤 |
| **2** | 中度 | 不仅仅是微动脉瘤，但不严重 |
| **3** | 严重 | 广泛的视网膜内出血、静脉串珠状 |
| **4** | 增殖性DR | 新血管生成和/或玻璃体出血 |

## 数据集

数据集包含来自 [IDRiD（印度糖尿病视网膜病变图像数据集）](https://universe.roboflow.com/officeworkspace/diabetic-retinopathy-dataset) 的 **510 张视网膜眼底图像**，在 **CC BY 4.0** 许可证下提供。

- **训练集**：407 张带标签图像
- **公共测试集**（开发阶段）：约 50 张图像
- **私有测试集**（最终阶段）：约 50 张图像

标签以**独热编码向量**形式提供，长度为 5（列 `0` 至 `4`）。每张图像恰好属于一个严重程度等级。

## 评估指标

提交内容按以下指标评估：

| 指标 | 描述 | 方向 |
|------|------|:----:|
| **F1 宏平均** | 所有严重程度等级的平均 F1 分数 | ↑ |

16306.title: ACM MM 2026 PCBA Standard-to-Real Grand Challenge
16306.description: ACM MM 2026 Grand Challenge on Cross-domain visual question answering for real-world PCBA manufacturing inspection.
16306.description_zh: ACM MM 2026 跨域视觉问答大挑战赛 - 真实世界PCBA制造检测

pages.title: Overview
pages.content:
## Overview

The **PCBA Standard-to-Real Grand Challenge** focuses on **cross-domain Visual Question Answering (VQA) for real-world manufacturing inspection**.

Printed Circuit Board Assembly (PCBA) inspection requires more than visual recognition. Practical factory decision-making depends on both **industrial standards** and **real production-line imagery**. A reliable inspection model must understand manufacturing rules, identify context-dependent defects, reason about possible causes, and provide actionable answers.

This challenge evaluates multimodal models on their ability to generalize from **standards-derived knowledge** to **dense, heterogeneous real-world PCBA images**. Participants are invited to develop VQA systems that can interpret standard documents, recognize components and defects, perform counting tasks, and reason under realistic visual variation.

Participants can register for the challenge on [HuggingFace](https://huggingface.co/datasets/aimmifm/PCBA_Standard-to-Real_Challenge) by applying for a Dataset and submit their prediction files through the official competition platform.

## Task

Participants are given PCBA-related visual questions and must submit predicted answers for each test sample. The benchmark covers four major VQA families:

1. **Standard-based Knowledge QA**
   Questions derived from industrial standards, covering standard interpretation, defect-cause analysis, and handling decisions.

2. **Factuality**
   Perception-centric questions, including component type recognition, mount-side identification, defect existence detection, and defect type recognition.

3. **Quantitative Reasoning**
   Numeric short-answer questions, including component counting and pin/lead counting.

4. **Attribute Reasoning**
   Questions about visual attributes such as the shape of main components.

## Dataset

The released dataset https://huggingface.co/datasets/aimmifm/PCBA_Standard-to-Real_Challenge
is built from two complementary sources:

* **Standards-derived VQA**
  Constructed from industrial packaging standards and converted into VQA items. This subset focuses on standards knowledge, defect-cause analysis, and decision-making.

* **Real-world VQA**
  Built from factory production-line data. This subset focuses on real manufacturing imagery and inspection-oriented reasoning under practical visual variation.

The public split contains:

| Split | Number of Questions |
| ----- | ------------------: |
| Train |               8,200 |
| Test  |               8,200 |
| Total |              16,400 |
pages.content_zh:
## 概述

**PCBA标准到真实大挑战赛**关注**跨域视觉问答(VQA)在真实制造检测中的应用**。

印制电路板组装(PCBA)检测不仅需要视觉识别。实际工厂决策取决于**工业标准**和**真实生产线图像**。可靠的检测模型必须理解制造规则、识别上下文相关的缺陷、推理可能的原因，并提供可操作的答案。

本挑战赛评估多模态模型从**标准衍生知识**泛化到**密集、异构真实世界PCBA图像**的能力。邀请参赛者开发VQA系统，能够解读标准文件、识别元器件和缺陷、执行计数任务，并在现实视觉变化下进行推理。

参赛者可在[HuggingFace](https://huggingface.co/datasets/aimmifm/PCBA_Standard-to-Real_Challenge)上申请数据集进行注册，并通过官方竞赛平台提交预测文件。

## 任务

参赛者获得PCBA相关视觉问题，必须为每个测试样本提交预测答案。基准测试涵盖四个主要VQA类别：

1. **基于标准的知识问答**
   源自工业标准的问题，涵盖标准解读、缺陷原因分析和处理决策。

2. **真实性**
   感知为中心的问题，包括元器件类型识别、贴装面识别、缺陷存在检测和缺陷类型识别。

3. **定量推理**
   数值短答案问题，包括元器件计数和引脚/导线计数。

4. **属性推理**
   关于主要元器件形状等视觉属性的问题。

## 数据集

发布的数据集 https://huggingface.co/datasets/aimmifm/PCBA_Standard-to-Real_Challenge 
由两个互补数据源构建：

* **标准衍生VQA**
  由工业包装标准构建并转换为VQA项目。该子集重点关注标准知识、缺陷原因分析和决策制定。

* **真实世界VQA**
  由工厂生产线数据构建。该子集重点关注真实制造图像和实际视觉变化下的检测导向推理。

公开分割包含：

| 分割 | 问题数量 |
| ---- | -------: |
| 训练集 | 8,200 |
| 测试集 | 8,200 |
| 总计 | 16,400 |

pages.title: Evaluation
pages.content:
## Evaluation

All submissions are evaluated automatically on the official Codabench server. Participants must submit one prediction file for the released public test questions. The public leaderboard reports a single **Overall Score**.

## Submission Format

For Codabench submission, participants must upload a single `.zip` file. The `.zip` archive must contain one CSV file named `submission.csv` at the root level:

```text
your_submission.zip
└── submission.csv
```

The `submission.csv` file must contain exactly two columns:

```csv
qid,answer
1,B
2,12
3,E
```

Submission rules:

* `qid` must exactly match the question identifier in `vqa_test_public.json`
* `answer` must contain only the final prediction
* For multiple-choice questions, submit the option key exactly as shown in the question, e.g. `A`, `B`, `C`, `D`, `E`, or `F`
* For counting questions, submit an integer as a plain string, e.g. `12`
* The file must contain one row for every test question
* Missing rows, duplicated `qid` values, or unknown `qid` values will result in an invalid submission
* Do not place `submission.csv` inside an extra subfolder in the submission archive

The public test JSON does not expose task-type annotations. Task grouping is handled internally by the official evaluation server.

## Official Metrics

Each test question belongs internally to one of the following task groups:

* `standard_knowledge`
* `component_type`
* `mount_side`
* `defect_existence`
* `defect_type`
* `count_component`
* `count_pin_lead`
* `attribute_reasoning`

Task-level metrics:

* **Accuracy** for `standard_knowledge`, `component_type`, `mount_side`, `defect_type`, and `attribute_reasoning`
* **F1-score** for `defect_existence`
* **Normalized MAE** for `count_component` and `count_pin_lead`

For `defect_existence`, the official score uses binary F1 on the anomaly-positive class.

For counting tasks, the official normalized score is:

```text
count_score_t = max(0, 1 - MAE_t / C_t)
```

where the fixed normalization constants are:

* `C_count_component = 138`
* `C_count_pin_lead = 260`

## Overall Score

Each task-level metric is first converted to the same `0-1` range. The final public leaderboard score is then computed as the weighted average across task groups, using the task proportions in the evaluation set:

```text
Overall Score = 100 * sum_t (w_t * s_t)
```

where:

* `s_t` is the task-level score for task `t`
* `w_t = n_t / N`
* `n_t` is the number of evaluation questions in task `t`
* `N` is the total number of evaluation questions

The leaderboard displays the final **Overall Score** on a `0-100` scale.

## Evaluation Notes

* The official Codabench server is the only valid source of scores
* Organizer-side task annotations are hidden from participants
* Invalid option labels for multiple-choice questions are counted as incorrect answers
* Non-integer answers for counting questions are treated as invalid counting predictions
* Organizers may additionally run the same scoring protocol on private evaluation data for verification or post-competition analysis
pages.content_zh:
## 评估

所有提交内容都在官方Codabench服务器上自动评估。参赛者必须为发布的公开测试题目提交一个预测文件。公开排行榜报告单一的**总体评分**。

## 提交格式

对于Codabench提交，参赛者必须上传单个`.zip`文件。`.zip`压缩包必须在根级别包含一个名为`submission.csv`的CSV文件：

```text
your_submission.zip
└── submission.csv
```

`submission.csv`文件必须包含恰好两列：

```csv
qid,answer
1,B
2,12
3,E
```

提交规则：

* `qid`必须与`vqa_test_public.json`中的问题标识符完全匹配
* `answer`必须仅包含最终预测
* 对于多选题，按照问题中显示的选项键提交，例如`A`、`B`、`C`、`D`、`E`或`F`
* 对于计数题，以纯字符串形式提交整数，例如`12`
* 文件必须为每个测试题目包含一行
* 缺少的行、重复的`qid`值或未知的`qid`值将导致提交无效
* 不要将`submission.csv`放在提交压缩包中的额外子文件夹内

公开测试JSON不公开任务类型注解。任务分组由官方评估服务器在内部处理。

## 官方指标

每个测试题目在内部属于以下任务组之一：

* `standard_knowledge`（标准知识）
* `component_type`（元件类型）
* `mount_side`（安装侧）
* `defect_existence`（缺陷存在性）
* `defect_type`（缺陷类型）
* `count_component`（元件计数）
* `count_pin_lead`（引脚/引线计数）
* `attribute_reasoning`（属性推理）

任务级指标：

* `standard_knowledge`、`component_type`、`mount_side`、`defect_type`和`attribute_reasoning`采用**准确率**
* `defect_existence`采用**F1值**
* `count_component`和`count_pin_lead`采用**规范化MAE**

对于`defect_existence`，官方评分使用异常阳性类的二元F1。

对于计数任务，官方规范化评分为：

```text
count_score_t = max(0, 1 - MAE_t / C_t)
```

其中固定的规范化常数为：

* `C_count_component = 138`
* `C_count_pin_lead = 260`

## 总体评分

每个任务级指标首先转换为相同的`0-1`范围。最终公开排行榜评分随后计算为跨任务组的加权平均值，使用评估集中的任务比例：

```text
Overall Score = 100 * sum_t (w_t * s_t)
```

其中：

* `s_t`是任务`t`的任务级评分
* `w_t = n_t / N`
* `n_t`是任务`t`的评估题目数量
* `N`是评估题目总数

排行榜显示`0-100`范围内的最终**总体评分**。

## 评估说明

* 官方Codabench服务器是唯一有效的评分来源
* 组织者侧的任务注解对参赛者隐藏
* 多选题的无效选项标签被计为错误答案
* 计数题的非整数答案被视为无效的计数预测
* 组织者可能在私有评估数据上运行相同的评分协议以进行验证或赛后分析

16299.title: ACM MM AffectiveArt 2026 - Track 1 Generation
16299.description_zh: 情感感知艺术图像生成

pages.title: Overview
pages.content:
# Track 1: Emotion-Aware Artistic Image Generation

## Overview

Given a multimodal prompt describing semantic content, artistic movement, and desired emotional state, the goal of Track 1 is to generate an image that fulfills all three conditions.

This track evaluates whether generative models can preserve the requested visual content while aligning with both the specified artistic movement and the target emotional state.

## Problem Statement

Participants are provided with a set of captions.

For each caption, participants must generate one artistic image that simultaneously satisfies the described semantic content, artistic style, and emotional condition.

## Scientific Challenge

Participants must develop models that can disentangle style from emotion. Successful models should learn to subtly manipulate visual attributes to achieve a hybrid state, such as a calm Expressionist painting.

This requires models to reason about both artistic style and emotional expression through visual attributes including composition, brushwork, color, line, and lighting.

## Evaluation Metrics

Track 1 submissions will be evaluated using:

- Frechet Inception Distance (FID), which evaluates image quality and distribution-level similarity to reference artwork images.
- Attribute Alignment Score (AAS), which evaluates prompt-image alignment in terms of content, style, and artistic attributes.

The final Track 1 score combines FID and AAS with equal weights.
pages.content_zh:
# 赛道 1：情感感知艺术图像生成

## 概述

给定描述语义内容、艺术运动和期望情感状态的多模态提示，赛道 1 的目标是生成同时满足这三个条件的图像。

该赛道评估生成模型是否能够保留请求的视觉内容，同时与指定的艺术运动和目标情感状态相一致。

## 问题陈述

参赛者获得一组标题/描述文本。

对于每个标题，参赛者必须生成一幅艺术图像，该图像应同时满足所描述的语义内容、艺术风格和情感条件。

## 科学挑战

参赛者必须开发能够区分风格与情感的模型。成功的模型应该学会微妙地操纵视觉属性以实现混合状态，例如一幅平静的表现主义绘画。

这要求模型通过包括构图、笔触、色彩、线条和光线在内的视觉属性来推理艺术风格和情感表达。

## 评估指标

赛道 1 的提交将使用以下方法进行评估：

- 弗雷歇初始距离（FID），评估图像质量和与参考艺术作品图像的分布级相似性。
- 属性对齐分数（AAS），评估提示与图像在内容、风格和艺术属性方面的对齐程度。

最终的赛道 1 得分以相等权重结合 FID 和 AAS。

pages.title: Data
pages.content:
# Data

## Test Set

The official test set has been distributed to registered participants by email.

Participants should use the emailed test set to prepare their final submissions. Please make sure that every submitted result corresponds to the official `sample_id` values provided in the test set.



## Data Use

The test set is intended only for participation in this challenge. Participants should not redistribute the test set or use it for purposes outside the competition without permission from the organizers.
pages.content_zh:
# 数据

参赛者应使用电子邮件发送的测试集来准备最终提交。请确保提交的每个结果都对应测试集中提供的官方 `sample_id` 值。



## 数据使用

测试集仅用于参加本竞赛。参赛者不应在未获得组织者许可的情况下重新分发测试集或将其用于竞赛范围之外的目的。

pages.title: Submission
pages.content:
# Submission Instructions

Participants must upload a `submission.zip` file following the required Track 1 format.

Do not include an extra parent directory inside the zip file. The required files should appear directly at the top level of `submission.zip`.

Participants must submit a `submission.zip` file containing:

```text
submission.zip
+-- submission.json
`-- images/
    +-- track1_0001.jpg
    +-- track1_0002.jpg
    `-- ...
```

The `submission.json` file must follow the format below:

```json
[
  {
    "sample_id": "track1_0001",
    "path": "images/track1_0001.jpg"
  },
  {
    "sample_id": "track1_0002",
    "path": "images/track1_0002.jpg"
  }
]
```

Each generated image filename must exactly match its corresponding `sample_id`.

## Upload Rules

- Upload only the required `submission.zip` file.
- Do not upload the full project folder or source code unless explicitly requested.
- Ensure that all `sample_id` values match the official test set.
- Ensure that all paths in `submission.json` match the files inside the zip archive.
- Invalid JSON, missing samples, missing files, or mismatched filenames may cause the submission to fail validation.
pages.content_zh:
# 提交说明

不要在 zip 文件内包含额外的父目录。所需文件应直接出现在 `submission.zip` 的顶层。

参赛者必须提交包含以下内容的 `submission.zip` 文件：

```text

    `-- ...
```

`submission.json` 文件必须遵循以下格式：

```json

  {

  },
```

pages.title: Evaluation
pages.content:
# Evaluation

## Track 1: Emotion-Aware Artistic Image Generation

For Track 1, submissions will be evaluated using Frechet Inception Distance (FID) and Attribute Alignment Score (AAS).

## FID

FID measures the distribution-level similarity between the generated image set and the reference artistic image set.

Lower FID indicates better performance.

## AAS

AAS evaluates the prompt-level alignment between each generated image and its corresponding caption. It consists of three aspects:

- Content Alignment: whether the generated image correctly reflects the main visual content described in the prompt.
- Style Alignment: whether the generated image reflects the required artistic style.
- Attribute Alignment: whether the generated image aligns with the artistic attributes specified in the caption, such as composition, brushwork, color, line, or lighting when applicable.

AAS will be assessed using a standardized LLM-assisted multimodal evaluation protocol. Each generated image will be evaluated together with its corresponding prompt under fixed evaluation prompts, predefined scoring rubrics, and consistent inference settings. The final AAS will be computed by aggregating the scores across the three alignment aspects and all test samples.

The final Track 1 score will be computed by combining FID and AAS with equal weights: 50% FID and 50% AAS. Since lower FID indicates better performance, FID will be normalized or converted into a score before being combined with AAS.

## Invalid Content

Any attempt to manipulate the evaluator, such as including instructions directed at the evaluator rather than valid task responses, will be treated as invalid content and may lead to score deduction or disqualification.
pages.content_zh:
# 评估

## 赛道1：情感感知艺术图像生成

赛道1将使用Frechet Inception Distance（FID）和属性对齐分数（AAS）进行评估。

## FID

FID衡量生成图像集与参考艺术图像集之间的分布级相似性。

FID越低表示性能越好。

## AAS

AAS评估每个生成图像与其对应标题之间的提示级对齐度。它包括三个方面：

- 内容对齐：生成的图像是否正确反映了提示中描述的主要视觉内容。
- 风格对齐：生成的图像是否反映了所需的艺术风格。
- 属性对齐：生成的图像是否符合标题中指定的艺术属性，如构图、笔触、色彩、线条或光影（如适用）。

AAS将使用标准化的大语言模型辅助多模态评估协议进行评估。每个生成的图像将与其对应的提示一起，在固定的评估提示、预定义的评分标准和一致的推理设置下进行评估。最终的AAS将通过汇总三个对齐方面和所有测试样本的分数来计算。

赛道1的最终得分将通过以相等权重（50% FID和50% AAS）组合FID和AAS来计算。由于FID越低表示性能越好，FID在与AAS组合前将被归一化或转换为得分。

## 无效内容

任何试图操纵评估器的行为，如包含针对评估器而非有效任务响应的指令，将被视为无效内容，可能导致扣分或取消资格。

pages.title: Rules
pages.content:
# Rules

Participants may use their own models and methods to complete the challenge tasks, provided that all submissions follow the official submission format and are based on the official test set distributed by the organizers.

## General Rules

- Each submitted result must correspond to a valid official `sample_id`.
- Submissions must follow the required JSON schema for the selected track.
- Participants are responsible for ensuring that submitted files are complete, readable, and correctly packaged.
- The test set distributed by the organizers must not be redistributed without permission.
- Participants should not submit content that is unrelated to the task or intended to interfere with the evaluation process.

## Invalid Submissions

A submission may be rejected, penalized, or disqualified if it:

- contains missing, duplicated, or invalid `sample_id` values,
- contains unreadable files or invalid JSON,
- does not follow the required zip structure,
- includes instructions directed at the evaluator instead of valid task responses,
- attempts to manipulate the scoring system or evaluation protocol.
pages.content_zh:
# 规则

参赛者可以使用自己的模型和方法完成挑战任务，前提是所有提交都遵循官方提交格式并基于组织者分发的官方测试集。

## 总体规则

- 每个提交的结果必须对应一个有效的官方`sample_id`。
- 提交必须遵循所选赛道的必需JSON模式。
- 参赛者负责确保提交的文件完整、可读且包装正确。
- 组织者分发的测试集不得在未经许可的情况下重新分发。
- 参赛者不应提交与任务无关的内容或意图干扰评估过程的内容。

## 无效提交

如果提交存在以下情况，可能会被拒绝、处罚或取消资格：

- 包含缺失、重复或无效的`sample_id`值，
- 包含不可读的文件或无效的JSON，
- 不遵循所需的zip结构，
- 包含针对评估者的指令而非有效任务响应，
- 试图操纵评分系统或评估协议。

16445.title: NeuroMM-2026 Challenge — Track 3 NMM-Source-IED
16445.description: Track 3 (NMM-Source-IED): 5-class localization of the epileptogenic region — combine pre-extracted synchronized visual context features with physiological signals to localize the seizure focus. Result-only submission: upload predictions over the released candidate set; only the hidden official Track-3 samples are scored. Primary metric: Weighted-F1.
16445.description_zh: Track 3 (NMM-Source-IED)：5分类癫痫灶区域定位——结合预提取的同步视觉上下文特征与生理信号来定位癫痫灶。仅结果提交：上传对已发布候选集的预测；只有隐藏的官方Track-3样本被评分。主要指标：加权F1。

pages.title: Overview
pages.content:
# NeuroMM-2026 Challenge — Track 3: NMM-Source-IED

Welcome to **Track 3 (NMM-Source-IED)** of the NeuroMM-2026 multimodal
neurophysiological intelligence challenge.

### NMM-Source-IED
- Primary metric: Weighted-F1
- Focus: 5-class localization of epileptogenic regions

Combine visual context features with physiological signals to localize the
seizure focus.

## Task

**5-class classification**: for each sample, predict the epileptogenic-region
class (an integer in **{1, 2, 3, 4, 5}**). Track 3 **encourages teams to
combine the pre-extracted synchronized visual context features with the
physiological signals (EEG/EMG/ECG)** to localize the seizure focus.

## Data

Apply for access and download the dataset from:

> <https://huggingface.co/datasets/NeuroMM/NeuroMM-2026>

The dataset contains the **training set**, the **validation set**, and the
**candidate set** (which contains the Track-3 test samples mixed with
distractors). The candidate set is intentionally larger than the official
Track-3 set and **you are not told which samples are scored** — predict for
*every* id in the released candidate set and submit all of them; the scorer
automatically keeps only the official hidden Track-3 samples and ignores the
rest.

## Registration Rules

- One person may participate in **only one team**.
- A team may enter multiple tracks, but for **each track only one team
  member's account** may make submissions.
- The email used to register on **Codabench must match the email** filled in
  the HuggingFace dataset-access request form; otherwise the registration
  **will not be approved**. All registrations are manually reviewed by the
  organizers.

## Submission

This competition accepts **prediction results only**. You upload a single
prediction file (packaged in a ZIP) — no code or model submission is
required. See the **Evaluation** page for the exact format.

## Ranking

Submissions are ranked by **Weighted-F1** (the support-weighted average of
the per-class F1 over the 5 classes). This is the **only** metric — no other
metric is computed, reported, or shown.
pages.content_zh:
# NeuroMM-2026 挑战赛 — 赛道 3: NMM-Source-IED

欢迎参加 NeuroMM-2026 多模态神经生理智能挑战赛的 **赛道 3 (NMM-Source-IED)**。

### NMM-Source-IED
- 主要指标：加权-F1
- 重点：癫痫灶区域的 5 分类定位

结合视觉上下文特征和生理信号来定位癫痫焦点。

## 任务

**5 分类**：对于每个样本，预测癫痫灶区域类别（整数范围在 **{1, 2, 3, 4, 5}**）。赛道 3 **鼓励参赛队伍将预提取的同步视觉上下文特征与生理信号（EEG/EMG/ECG）相结合**来定位癫痫焦点。

## 数据

申请访问权限并从以下网址下载数据集：

> <https://huggingface.co/datasets/NeuroMM/NeuroMM-2026>

数据集包含**训练集**、**验证集**和**候选集**（候选集包含赛道 3 测试样本与干扰项的混合）。候选集故意设置得大于官方赛道 3 集合，**您不会被告知哪些样本被计分** — 请对发布的候选集中的*每个* id 进行预测并提交全部结果；评分系统会自动仅保留官方隐藏的赛道 3 样本并忽略其余样本。

## 注册规则

- 一个人只能参加**一个团队**。
- 一个团队可以参加多个赛道，但**每个赛道仅一名团队成员账户**可以提交。
- 在 **Codabench 上注册的电子邮件必须与**填写的 HuggingFace 数据集访问申请表中的电子邮件一致；否则注册**将不被批准**。所有注册均由组织者手动审核。

## 提交

本竞赛仅接受**预测结果**。您上传单个预测文件（打包为 ZIP 格式）— 无需提交代码或模型。有关确切格式，请参阅**评估**页面。

## 排名

提交按 **加权-F1**（5 个类别的各类 F1 的支持加权平均）排名。这是**唯一**的指标 — 不计算、报告或显示其他指标。

pages.title: Evaluation
pages.content:
# Evaluation

## Metric

**Weighted-F1** — the support-weighted average of the per-class F1 over the
5 classes (`scikit-learn` `f1_score(average='weighted')`). This is the
**only** metric; no other metric is produced, reported, or shown. Higher is
better.

## Submission format

Submit a **ZIP** file containing exactly one file named **`submission.csv`**:

```
sample_submission.zip
└── submission.csv
```

> Only the file name `submission.csv` is accepted. Any other name
> (e.g. `prediction.csv`) will cause the submission to fail.

`submission.csv` has two columns:

```csv
sample_id,prediction
NMM26-XXXXXXXX,3
NMM26-YYYYYYYY,1
NMM26-ZZZZZZZZ,5
```

- `sample_id` — the id exactly as given in the released candidate set.
- `prediction` — the predicted **class as an integer in {1, 2, 3, 4, 5}**
  (the epileptogenic-region class). One class per sample.

### Important

- Predict for **every** id in the released candidate set and include them all.
  You are **not** told which ids are the official Track-3 samples.
- The scorer keeps only the hidden official Track-3 ids and **silently
  ignores every other row** (distractors / other tracks). Extra rows are
  fine.
- A submission **fails** if any official Track-3 sample has no prediction, if
  predictions are non-numeric, if `sample_id` is duplicated, or if the file
  is not named `submission.csv`.
pages.content_zh:
# 评估

## 指标

**加权F1** — 5个类别的逐类F1的支持度加权平均值（`scikit-learn` `f1_score(average='weighted')`）。这是**唯一**的指标；不产生、报告或显示任何其他指标。数值越高越好。

## 提交格式

提交一个**ZIP**文件，其中恰好包含一个名为**`submission.csv`**的文件：

```
sample_submission.zip
└── submission.csv
```

> 仅接受文件名`submission.csv`。任何其他名称（例如`prediction.csv`）都会导致提交失败。

`submission.csv`包含两列：

```csv
sample_id,prediction
NMM26-XXXXXXXX,3
NMM26-YYYYYYYY,1
NMM26-ZZZZZZZZ,5
```

- `sample_id` — 发布的候选集中给定的样本ID。
- `prediction` — 预测的**类别，为整数{1, 2, 3, 4, 5}**（痫源区类别）。每个样本一个类别。

### 重要事项

- 为发布的候选集中的**每个**ID进行预测，并将其全部包含在内。**不会告知**您哪些ID是官方Track-3样本。
- 评分器仅保留隐藏的官方Track-3 ID，**自动忽略所有其他行**（干扰项/其他赛道）。额外的行没有问题。
- 如果任何官方Track-3样本没有预测、预测非数字、`sample_id`重复，或文件未命名为`submission.csv`，提交将**失败**。

pages.title: Terms
pages.content:
# Terms and Conditions

By participating in the NeuroMM-2026 Challenge (Track 3: NMM-Source-IED) you agree to:

- Use the provided data **only** for research and participation in this
  challenge. Redistribution of the data, or any commercial / unauthorized use,
  is prohibited.
- Make predictions with automated machine-learning methods. Manual labeling of
  samples by human inspection of the physiological signals is **not** an
  acceptable methodology and violates the spirit of the challenge.
- Not attempt to de-anonymize samples, identify which candidate ids are the
  scored official samples, or otherwise reverse-engineer the hidden labels.
- Make a reasonable number of submissions (a daily cap is enforced). Abusing
  the evaluation service to probe hidden labels is prohibited.

The organizers may update these rules and the competition schedule when
necessary, and reserve the right to disqualify submissions that violate these
terms.

The dataset is provided under the NeuroMM-2026 data usage policy
(academic research and challenge participation only).
pages.content_zh:
# 条款和条件

参与NeuroMM-2026挑战赛（赛道3：NMM-Source-IED）即表示您同意：

- **仅**将提供的数据用于研究和本挑战赛的参与。禁止数据的再分发、任何商业使用或未授权使用。
- 采用自动化机器学习方法进行预测。通过人工检查生理信号对样本进行手动标注**不是**可接受的方法，违反了挑战赛的宗旨。
- 不尝试对样本进行去匿名化处理、识别哪些候选ID是评分的官方样本，或以其他方式对隐藏标签进行逆向工程。
- 进行合理数量的提交（执行每日限制）。禁止滥用评估服务来探测隐藏标签。

组织者可在必要时更新这些规则和竞赛日程，并保留对违反这些条款的提交进行取消资格的权利。

该数据集根据NeuroMM-2026数据使用政策提供（仅用于学术研究和挑战赛参与）。

pages.title: Overview
pages.content:
# NeuroMM-2026 Challenge — Track 2: NMM-Context-IED

Welcome to **Track 2 (NMM-Context-IED)** of the NeuroMM-2026 multimodal
neurophysiological intelligence challenge.

### NMM-Context-IED
- Primary metric: AUPRC
- Focus: artifact suppression with synchronized visual context features

Vision-enhanced detection with synchronized contextual features.

## Task

Binary classification: for each sample, predict the probability that it
contains an epileptic **spike** (positive, label `1`) versus **non-spike**
(negative, label `0`). Track 2 **encourages teams to combine the
pre-extracted synchronized visual context features with the physiological
signals (EEG/EMG/ECG)** to suppress artifacts and improve robustness over
signal-only detection.

## Data

Apply for access and download the dataset from:

> <https://huggingface.co/datasets/NeuroMM/NeuroMM-2026>

The dataset contains the **training set**, the **validation set**, and the
**candidate set** (which contains the Track-2 test samples mixed with
distractors). The candidate set is intentionally larger than the official
Track-2 set and **you are not told which samples are scored** — predict for
*every* id in the released candidate set and submit all of them; the scorer
automatically keeps only the official hidden Track-2 samples and ignores the
rest.

## Registration Rules

- One person may participate in **only one team**.
- A team may enter multiple tracks, but for **each track only one team
  member's account** may make submissions.
- The email used to register on **Codabench must match the email** filled in
  the HuggingFace dataset-access request form; otherwise the registration
  **will not be approved**. All registrations are manually reviewed by the
  organizers.

## Submission

This competition accepts **prediction results only**. You upload a single
prediction file (packaged in a ZIP) — no code or model submission is
required. See the **Evaluation** page for the exact format.

## Ranking

Submissions are ranked by **AUPRC** (area under the precision–recall curve).
This is the **only** metric — no other metric is computed, reported, or
shown.
pages.content_zh:
# NeuroMM-2026 挑战赛 — 赛道2：NMM-Context-IED

欢迎来到 **NeuroMM-2026 多模态神经生理信息智能挑战赛的赛道2（NMM-Context-IED）**。

### NMM-Context-IED
- 主要评估指标：AUPRC
- 重点：通过同步视觉上下文特征进行伪影抑制

基于视觉增强的检测与同步上下文特征。

## 任务

二分类：对于每个样本，预测其包含癫痫**尖波**（正样本，标签 `1`）与**非尖波**（负样本，标签 `0`）的概率。赛道2 **鼓励团队将预先提取的同步视觉上下文特征与生理信号（EEG/EMG/ECG）相结合**，以抑制伪影并提高相对于仅用信号检测的鲁棒性。

## 数据

从以下地址申请访问和下载数据集：

> <https://huggingface.co/datasets/NeuroMM/NeuroMM-2026>

数据集包含**训练集**、**验证集**和**候选集**（包含赛道2的测试样本与干扰项混合）。候选集故意大于官方赛道2集合，**参赛者不被告知哪些样本会被计分** — 对发布的候选集中的*所有*id进行预测并提交所有结果；评分系统将自动只保留官方隐藏的赛道2样本并忽略其余样本。

## 注册规则

- 一个人只能参加**一个团队**。
- 一个团队可参加多个赛道，但**每个赛道只有一名团队成员账号**可进行提交。
- 在 **Codabench 上注册所用的电子邮箱必须与** HuggingFace 数据集访问申请表中填写的电子邮箱**相匹配**；否则注册**将不被批准**。所有注册都由组织者进行人工审核。

## 提交

本竞赛仅接受**预测结果**。您上传单个预测文件（打包为 ZIP 格式） — 不需要提交代码或模型。具体格式见**评估**页面。

## 排名

提交结果按 **AUPRC**（精准率-召回率曲线下面积）排名。这是**唯一的**评估指标 — 不计算、报告或显示其他指标。

pages.title: Evaluation
pages.content:
# Evaluation

## Metric

**AUPRC** — area under the precision–recall curve (a.k.a. average precision),
computed with `label = 1` (spike) as the positive class. This is the **only**
metric; no other metric is produced, reported, or shown. Higher is better.

## Submission format

Submit a **ZIP** file containing exactly one file named **`submission.csv`**:

```
sample_submission.zip
└── submission.csv
```

> Only the file name `submission.csv` is accepted. Any other name
> (e.g. `prediction.csv`) will cause the submission to fail.

`submission.csv` has two columns:

```csv
sample_id,prediction
NMM26-XXXXXXXX,0.0123
NMM26-YYYYYYYY,0.9871
NMM26-ZZZZZZZZ,0.5040
```

- `sample_id` — the id exactly as given in the released candidate set.
- `prediction` — a real-valued **probability/score that the sample is a
  spike** (positive class). Any monotone score works for AUPRC; values are
  not required to be calibrated (`[0, 1]` is recommended).

### Important

- Predict for **every** id in the released candidate set and include them all.
  You are **not** told which ids are the official Track-2 samples.
- The scorer keeps only the hidden official Track-2 ids and **silently
  ignores every other row** (distractors / other tracks). Extra rows are
  fine.
- A submission **fails** if any official Track-2 sample has no prediction, if
  predictions are non-numeric, if `sample_id` is duplicated, or if the file
  is not named `submission.csv`.
pages.content_zh:
# 评估

## 指标

**AUPRC** — 精确率-召回率曲线下的面积（又称平均精确率），以 `label = 1`（尖峰）作为正类计算。这是**唯一**的指标；不生成、报告或显示其他指标。数值越高越好。

## 提交格式

提交一个**ZIP**文件，其中包含恰好一个名为**`submission.csv`**的文件：

```
sample_submission.zip
└── submission.csv
```

> 仅接受文件名 `submission.csv`。任何其他名称（例如 `prediction.csv`）都会导致提交失败。

`submission.csv` 包含两列：

```csv
sample_id,prediction
NMM26-XXXXXXXX,0.0123
NMM26-YYYYYYYY,0.9871
NMM26-ZZZZZZZZ,0.5040
```

- `sample_id` — 与发布的候选集中提供的id完全相同。
- `prediction` — 一个实数值，表示**样本为尖峰的概率/分数**（正类）。任何单调分数都适用于AUPRC；不要求值经过校准（推荐使用 `[0, 1]` 范围）。

### 重要事项

- 对已发布候选集中的**每个**id进行预测，并包含所有这些id。**不会**告知哪些id是官方Track-2样本。
- 评分器仅保留隐藏的官方Track-2 id，**默认忽略所有其他行**（干扰项/其他赛道）。额外的行没有问题。
- 如果以下情况发生，提交**失败**：任何官方Track-2样本缺少预测、预测不是数值、`sample_id` 重复，或文件名不是 `submission.csv`。

The organizers may update these rules and the competition schedule when

15769.title: The Challenge on General AIGC Audio–Video Detection
15769.description: AIGC Audio-Video Deepfake Classification Challenge using the MVAD dataset.
15769.description_zh: AIGC音视频深度伪造分类挑战赛（采用MVAD数据集）

pages.title: Overview
pages.content:
# General AIGC Audio-Video Detection

This competition focuses on **General AIGC Audio-Video Detection**. To counter the growing threat of advanced AI-generated video forgery (e.g., Sora2, Veo3.1), we propose a large-scale multimodal detection challenge based on the **MVAD dataset** (released as DDL‑GAV), which contains more than **200,000 real‑forgery audio‑video samples**. It covers both realistic and anime visual styles, four content categories (humans, animals, objects, and scenes), three typical cross‑modal forgery patterns, and more than **23 AI generation techniques**, including text‑to‑video, image‑to‑video, video‑to‑video, and audio‑to‑video synthesis. The competition aims to promote the research and application of multimodal deepfake detection and support the sustainable development of **AI for Good**.

Competition link: https://www.codabench.org/competitions/15769/

If any participants have any questions, please send an email to: 17318508682@163.com

#### Note: The Test Set has been officially released on the GitHub repository as of May 15, 2026.

---

## Evaluation Metrics
This challenge includes **two hierarchical detection tasks**: binary forgery detection and four‑class modality classification.
### Task Definitions
- Real‑Real (RR): Both audio and video are authentic.
- Fake‑Real (FR): Video is forged, audio is real.
- Real‑Fake (RF): Video is real, audio is forged.
- Fake‑Fake (FF): Both audio and video are forged.

### Evaluation Metrics
Detection performance is evaluated using **AUC, ACC, F1, and AP**.

### Final Score Calculation
- Binary Classification Score = 0.2×AUC + 0.3×ACC + 0.3×AP + 0.2×F1
- Four‑Class Classification Score = 0.2×AUC + 0.3×ACC + 0.3×AP + 0.2×F1
- **Overall Final Score = 0.4 × Binary Classification Score + 0.6 × Four‑Class Classification Score**

## Submission Format

Participants must submit **two prediction files** packaged together in **a single `.zip` archive**.

### Packaging Rules

- The zip archive can have **any name** (`submission.zip`, `result.zip`, etc.).
- It must contain exactly two files, named **precisely** `binary.txt` and `four_class.txt` (case-sensitive — do not rename).
- The two files may sit at the zip root or inside a subfolder — both are accepted.
- Submit prediction results only — **do not** submit model code.

### 1. `binary.txt` — Binary Classification (Real / Fake)

Each line: `video_path  0_pred  1_pred`

- `0_pred` = probability of **Real**
- `1_pred` = probability of **Fake**
- An optional header line `video_path,0_pred,1_pred` is allowed and will be skipped.

```
video_path,0_pred,1_pred
000bfd940cf44f998b1b4803d48d7d05.mp4 0.5066 0.4934
0016150625f846bf8feebc6e5c0831b8.mp4 0.5100 0.4900
001eb668e3ba4ecd89417be16ca7b613.mp4 0.6434 0.3566
```

### 2. `four_class.txt` — Four-Class Classification

Each line: `video_path  0_pred  1_pred  2_pred  3_pred`

- Class indices: `0 = Real-Real (RR)`, `1 = Fake-Fake (FF)`, `2 = Fake-Real (FR)`, `3 = Real-Fake (RF)`
- An optional header line is allowed and will be skipped.

```
video_path,0_pred,1_pred,2_pred,3_pred
000bfd940cf44f998b1b4803d48d7d05.mp4 0.1292 0.0075 0.3836 0.4796
0016150625f846bf8feebc6e5c0831b8.mp4 0.2880 0.0590 0.2999 0.3531
001eb668e3ba4ecd89417be16ca7b613.mp4 0.3144 0.0230 0.3682 0.2944
```

### Important Notes

- **`video_path` must exactly match the test-set filenames** (the hashed `.mp4` names in the released test data). Mismatched names are treated as missing and scored with a default uniform probability.
- Within a line, use **either spaces or commas** as the separator consistently — do **not** mix `comma + space`.
- Probabilities need not sum exactly to 1 — the scorer normalizes each row automatically.
- **Every test-set sample should appear in both files.** Missing rows are filled with default probabilities and will lower your score.

## Awards and Recognition
- Outstanding solutions will be invited for presentation at IJCAI.
- **Top 1–5 teams** will receive official Ranking Certificates.
- **Top 6–10 teams** will receive Excellence Awards.
- Submitted papers for **Track 2** will have the opportunity to be selected for the Best Paper Award.

## Dataset
This competition is built on the **MVAD dataset** (released as DDL‑GAV), the first large‑scale benchmark for multimodal AI‑generated audio‑video detection. It contains more than **205,000 video‑audio samples**, covering two visual domains (realistic and anime), four content categories (humans, animals, objects, scenes), three cross‑modal forgery patterns, and over **23 AI generation methods**. The dataset provides comprehensive and diverse support for advancing research in generalizable AIGC audio‑video detection.

GitHub repository: https://github.com/HuMengXue0104/MVAD

## Competition Rules
1. Model Submission Requirements
Each track permits only one model submission to address the designated tasks.
All pre-trained backbone models used must be open-source. Teams that develop proprietary backbone models during the competition are required to publicly release their model specifications and training protocols under an open-source license (e.g., MIT, Apache 2.0) during the competition period.
Winning solutions must open-source their complete implementation, including:
    - Training pipelines and hyperparameter configurations
    - Evaluation code and reproducibility documentation
    - Final model weights in standard formats
2. Dataset Usage Requirements
Only organizer-recommended, publicly released datasets are permitted for training and evaluation.
Participants may use extended samples generated from these recommended datasets via data augmentation or deepfake tools for training, but all generation tools used must be submitted to the organizers to ensure reproducibility.
The use of any additional external datasets is strictly prohibited.

3. Violations and Sanctions
Violations of the above rules will result in disqualification. The organizing committee reserves the final authority over all competition-related matters.
pages.content_zh:
# 通用AIGC音视频检测

本竞赛聚焦于**通用AIGC音视频检测**。为应对先进AI生成视频伪造技术（如Sora2、Veo3.1）日益增长的威胁，我们基于**MVAD数据集**（作为DDL‑GAV发布）提出了一项大规模多模态检测挑战，该数据集包含超过**200,000个真实-伪造音视频样本**。涵盖真实和动画两种视觉风格，四个内容类别（人物、动物、物体和场景），三种典型的跨模态伪造模式，以及超过**23种AI生成技术**，包括文本生成视频、图像生成视频、视频生成视频和音频生成视频合成。本竞赛旨在促进多模态深度伪造检测的研究与应用，并支持**向善AI**的可持续发展。

竞赛链接：https://www.codabench.org/competitions/15769/

如有任何问题，请发送邮件至：17318508682@163.com

#### 说明：测试集已于2026年5月15日在GitHub仓库正式发布。

---

## 评估指标
本挑战包含**两项分层检测任务**：二分类伪造检测和四分类模态分类。
### 任务定义
- 真实-真实（RR）：音频和视频都是真实的。
- 伪造-真实（FR）：视频被伪造，音频是真实的。
- 真实-伪造（RF）：视频是真实的，音频被伪造。
- 伪造-伪造（FF）：音频和视频都被伪造。

### 评估指标
检测性能通过**AUC、ACC、F1和AP**进行评估。

### 最终分数计算
- 二分类分数 = 0.2×AUC + 0.3×ACC + 0.3×AP + 0.2×F1
- 四分类分数 = 0.2×AUC + 0.3×ACC + 0.3×AP + 0.2×F1
- **总体最终分数 = 0.4 × 二分类分数 + 0.6 × 四分类分数**

## 提交格式

参与者必须提交**两个预测文件**，统一打包在**一个`.zip`压缩包**中。

### 打包规则

- zip压缩包可以**任意命名**（`submission.zip`、`result.zip`等）。
- 必须恰好包含两个文件，名称**必须精确**为`binary.txt`和`four_class.txt`（区分大小写——不要重命名）。
- 两个文件可以位于zip根目录或子文件夹内——两种方式都可接受。
- 仅提交预测结果——**不要**提交模型代码。

### 1. `binary.txt` — 二分类（真实/伪造）

每行格式：`video_path  0_pred  1_pred`

- `0_pred` = **真实**的概率
- `1_pred` = **伪造**的概率
- 可选的标题行`video_path,0_pred,1_pred`允许但将被跳过。

```
video_path,0_pred,1_pred
000bfd940cf44f998b1b4803d48d7d05.mp4 0.5066 0.4934
0016150625f846bf8feebc6e5c0831b8.mp4 0.5100 0.4900
001eb668e3ba4ecd89417be16ca7b613.mp4 0.6434 0.3566
```

### 2. `four_class.txt` — 四分类

每行格式：`video_path  0_pred  1_pred  2_pred  3_pred`

- 类别索引：`0 = 真实-真实（RR）`、`1 = 伪造-伪造（FF）`、`2 = 伪造-真实（FR）`、`3 = 真实-伪造（RF）`
- 可选的标题行允许但将被跳过。

```
video_path,0_pred,1_pred,2_pred,3_pred
000bfd940cf44f998b1b4803d48d7d05.mp4 0.1292 0.0075 0.3836 0.4796
0016150625f846bf8feebc6e5c0831b8.mp4 0.2880 0.0590 0.2999 0.3531
001eb668e3ba4ecd89417be16ca7b613.mp4 0.3144 0.0230 0.3682 0.2944
```

### 重要说明

- **`video_path`必须精确匹配测试集文件名**（发布的测试数据中的哈希`.mp4`名称）。名称不匹配的样本视为缺失，将使用默认均匀概率计分。
- 行内分隔符**统一使用空格或逗号**——**不要**混用`逗号+空格`。
- 概率无需精确求和为1——评分器将自动对每行进行归一化。
- **每个测试集样本应出现在两个文件中。**缺失的行将填充默认概率，并降低您的分数。

## 奖项与荣誉
- 杰出解决方案将被邀请在IJCAI进行展示。
- **前1-5名团队**将获得官方排名证书。
