pages.title: Overview
pages.content:
# Track 3: Seephys Pro - Visual Physics Reasoning

A Multimodal Physics Reasoning Challenge with Diagrams, Expressions, and Explanations

Seephys Pro evaluates whether AI systems can solve physics problems that require visual perception, mathematical reasoning, and faithful explanation. The benchmark combines structured physics problems, diagram-grounded questions, and a curated image-only extension. To rigorously evaluate the systems' generalization capabilities across unseen environments, the benchmark incorporates a specialized Out-of-Distribution (OOD) test set captured via photography, which is represented by the Level 5 dataset. The Level 5 dataset is provided by Transsion, a leading smart device and mobile internet company focused on emerging markets. It consists of anonymized service data from Transsion’s AI photo-based problem-solving service. This level evaluates models in realistic education scenarios, including diverse question formats and natural image quality variations. Participants submit both final answers and reasoning; submissions are scored by an OpenAI-based judge against held reference answers.

Physics reasoning is a core scientific capability. It requires a system to identify quantities, interpret diagrams, connect visual evidence with equations, and produce a defensible conclusion. Recent multimodal models have made rapid progress, but physics questions with diagrams and formal expressions still expose a difficult gap between seeing, reading, and reasoning. The central question of Seephys Pro is: > **How can we build AI systems that reliably integrate visual information, physical laws, and mathematical derivations to solve real physics problems?** This challenge is designed as a compact but demanding public benchmark for that question. ## Challenge at a Glance | Item | Details | |---|---:| | Challenge name | Seephys Pro | | Public leaderboard questions | 830 | | Private leaderboard questions | 3320 | | Required submission fields | `question_id`, `prediction`, `reasoning` | | Scoring | OpenAI API semantic judge | | Overall score | Question-count weighted accuracy | ## Dataset The datasets are hosted on Hugging Face: https://huggingface.co/datasets/Kun-Xiang/SeePhysPro ## What to Submit Participants must submit a `.zip` file containing both: - `prediction.csv` - `prediction_private.csv` Each file must include `question_id`, `prediction`, and `reasoning`. `prediction.csv` is used for the Codabench public leaderboard. `prediction_private.csv` is used for the offline private leaderboard after the deadline. Submissions without `prediction_private.csv` will not be eligible for prizes. ## Notes The public leaderboard is intended for fast iteration. Private evaluation is not automatically run by this Codabench bundle. ## Citation 
@article{xiang2026seephyspro,
  title={SeePhys Pro: Diagnosing Modality Transfer and Blind-Training Effects in Multimodal RLVR for Physics Reasoning},
  author={Xiang, Kun and Zhang, Terry Jingchen and Liu, Zirong and Zhou, Bokai and Tang, Yueling and Yu, Junjie and Lu, Jiacong and Huang, Shangrui and Li, Heng and Zhang, Likui and Liu, Kunkun and Zhang, Changzheng and Fang, Yangle and Guo, Boqiang and Zhen, Hui-Ling and Tu, Dandan and Huang, Yinya and Liang, Xiaodan},
  journal={arXiv preprint arXiv:2605.09266},
  year={2026}
}
pages.content_zh:
# 赛道 3：Seephys Pro - 视觉物理推理

一项包含图表、表达式和解释的多模态物理推理挑战赛

Seephys Pro 评估人工智能系统是否能够解决需要视觉感知、数学推理和忠实解释的物理问题。该基准结合了结构化物理问题、基于图表的问题和精选的仅图像扩展。为了严格评估系统在未见环境中的泛化能力，该基准纳入了通过摄影捕获的专门分布外（OOD）测试集，由第 5 级数据集表示。第 5 级数据集由传音控股提供，传音控股是一家专注于新兴市场的领先智能设备和移动互联网公司。它由传音的基于 AI 的照片问题解决服务的匿名服务数据组成。该级别在现实教育场景中评估模型，包括多种问题格式和自然图像质量变化。参与者提交最终答案和推理过程；提交内容由基于 OpenAI 的评判员根据参考答案进行评分。

物理推理是一项核心科学能力。它要求系统识别物理量、解释图表、将视觉证据与方程联系起来，并得出可防守的结论。最近的多模态模型取得了快速进展，但包含图表和形式表达式的物理问题仍然暴露了观看、阅读和推理之间的一个困难差距。 Seephys Pro 的核心问题是： > **我们如何构建能够可靠地整合视觉信息、物理定律和数学推导来解决真实物理问题的人工智能系统？** 本挑战赛被设计为针对这一问题的紧凑但具有挑战性的公开基准。 ## 挑战赛概览 | 项目 | 详情 | |---|---:| | 挑战赛名称 | Seephys Pro | | 公开排行榜问题数 | 830 | | 私密排行榜问题数 | 3320 | | 必需提交字段 | `question_id`、`prediction`、`reasoning` | | 评分方式 | OpenAI API 语义评判员 | | 总体得分 | 问题计数加权准确率 | ## 数据集 数据集托管在 Hugging Face： https://huggingface.co/datasets/Kun-Xiang/SeePhysPro ## 提交内容 参与者必须提交包含以下两项内容的 `.zip` 文件： - `prediction.csv` - `prediction_private.csv` 每个文件必须包含 `question_id`、`prediction` 和 `reasoning`。 `prediction.csv` 用于 Codabench 公开排行榜。`prediction_private.csv` 用于截止日期后的离线私密排行榜。不包含 `prediction_private.csv` 的提交将没有资格获得奖项。 ## 注意事项 公开排行榜旨在进行快速迭代。私密评估不会由此 Codabench 捆绑包自动运行。 ## 引用 
@article{xiang2026seephyspro,
  title={SeePhys Pro: Diagnosing Modality Transfer and Blind-Training Effects in Multimodal RLVR for Physics Reasoning},
  author={Xiang, Kun and Zhang, Terry Jingchen and Liu, Zirong and Zhou, Bokai and Tang, Yueling and Yu, Junjie and Lu, Jiacong and Huang, Shangrui and Li, Heng and Zhang, Likui and Liu, Kunkun and Zhang, Changzheng and Fang, Yangle and Guo, Boqiang and Zhen, Hui-Ling and Tu, Dandan and Huang, Yinya and Liang, Xiaodan},
  journal={arXiv preprint arXiv:2605.09266},
  year={2026}
}

pages.title: Data
pages.content:
# Seephys Pro Data
Benchmark Data:
https://huggingface.co/datasets/Kun-Xiang/SeePhysPro

Project Page:
https://seephyspro.github.io/

Paper:
https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.09266
## Public Leaderboard Splits Use the following splits for public leaderboard submissions: | Split | Questions | Public leaderboard | |---|---|---| | `level1_testmini` | 200 | Yes | | `level2_testmini` | 200 | Yes | | `level3_testmini` | 200 | Yes | | `level4_testmini` | 200 | Yes | | `level5_testmini` | 30 | Yes | Total public leaderboard size: **830 questions**. ## Private Leaderboard Splits The Hugging Face repository also contains private data: | Split | Questions | Private leaderboard | |---|---|---| | `level1` | 800 | Yes | | `level2` | 800 | Yes | | `level3` | 800 | Yes | | `level4` | 800 | Yes | | `level5` | 120 | Yes | Total private leaderboard size: **3320 questions**. The final rankings are determined by the Overall Accuracy on the **private leaderboard.** ## Schema Each row includes a stable `question_id` used for submission alignment. Common columns: | Column | Description | |---|---| | `question_id` | Stable ID used in submissions | | `row_id` | Row index within the split | | `idx` | Original source index | | `problem` | Problem text, when available | | `images` | Image list in Hugging Face image format | ## No-Answer Release This prevents answer leakage. Reference answers are kept only in the Codabench reference bundle used by the scoring program. ## Loading Example Load the Testmini dataset: ```python from datasets import load_dataset testmini = load_dataset("Kun-Xiang/Track3-SeePhysPro-Testmini") testmini_level1 = load_dataset("Kun-Xiang/Track3-SeePhysPro-Testmini", split="level1_testmini") ``` Load the Test dataset: ```python from datasets import load_dataset level1_test = load_dataset("Kun-Xiang/SeePhysPro", "level1", split="test") level1_testmini = load_dataset("Kun-Xiang/SeePhysPro", "level1", split="testmini") ``` If the repository is private, authenticate with a Hugging Face token before loading the dataset.
pages.content_zh:
# Seephys Pro 数据
基准数据：
https://huggingface.co/datasets/Kun-Xiang/SeePhysPro

项目主页：
https://seephyspro.github.io/

论文：
https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.09266
## 公开排行榜数据集 使用以下数据集用于公开排行榜提交： | 数据集 | 问题数 | 公开排行榜 | |---|---|---| | `level1_testmini` | 200 | 是 | | `level2_testmini` | 200 | 是 | | `level3_testmini` | 200 | 是 | | `level4_testmini` | 200 | 是 | | `level5_testmini` | 30 | 是 | 公开排行榜总规模：**830道问题**。 ## 私有排行榜数据集 Hugging Face 仓库还包含私有数据： | 数据集 | 问题数 | 私有排行榜 | |---|---|---| | `level1` | 800 | 是 | | `level2` | 800 | 是 | | `level3` | 800 | 是 | | `level4` | 800 | 是 | | `level5` | 120 | 是 | 私有排行榜总规模：**3320道问题**。 最终排名由**私有排行榜**上的总体准确率决定。 ## 数据结构 每一行都包含用于提交对齐的稳定 `question_id`。 通用列： | 列 | 描述 | |---|---| | `question_id` | 提交中使用的稳定ID | | `row_id` | 数据集中的行索引 | | `idx` | 原始来源索引 | | `problem` | 问题文本（如果可用） | | `images` | Hugging Face 图像格式的图像列表 | ## 无答案发布 这可以防止答案泄露。参考答案仅保存在评分程序使用的 Codabench 参考包中。 ## 加载示例 加载 Testmini 数据集： ```python from datasets import load_dataset testmini = load_dataset("Kun-Xiang/Track3-SeePhysPro-Testmini") testmini_level1 = load_dataset("Kun-Xiang/Track3-SeePhysPro-Testmini", split="level1_testmini") ``` 加载 Test 数据集： ```python from datasets import load_dataset level1_test = load_dataset("Kun-Xiang/SeePhysPro", "level1", split="test") level1_testmini = load_dataset("Kun-Xiang/SeePhysPro", "level1", split="testmini") ``` 如果仓库是私有的，在加载数据集之前使用 Hugging Face token 进行身份验证。

pages.title: Evaluation
pages.content:
# SeePhys Pro Evaluation
Important: each submission must provide both a final answer and a supporting explanation. Responses that contain only a final answer without reasoning will not be considered complete.
## Submission Format Please submit a `.zip` file containing both public and private prediction files at the root of the archive. Your submission should contain: ```text submission.zip ├── prediction.csv └── prediction_private.csv ``` ### Required Files #### 1. `prediction.csv` This file is used for the Codabench public leaderboard. #### 2. `prediction_private.csv` This file is reserved for the private leaderboard. The private leaderboard will **not** be evaluated automatically on Codabench. After the competition deadline, the organizers will download the final submissions and evaluate `prediction_private.csv` offline using the private reference answers. ### Required Fields Both `prediction.csv` and `prediction_private.csv` must contain the following fields: ```text question_id prediction reasoning ``` ### Important Notes The public leaderboard score shown on Codabench is computed only from `prediction.csv`. The private leaderboard score is computed offline only from `prediction_private.csv`. If `prediction_private.csv` is missing, the submission will not receive a valid private leaderboard score. ## CSV Example ```csv question_id,prediction,reasoning level1_testmini_000000,BD,"The two circuit branches give the stated acceleration and charge relations, so B and D are correct." level1_testmini_000001,1.44,"Using Snell's law and the measured geometry, the refractive index is approximately 1.44." ``` ## JSONL Example ```jsonl {"question_id":"level1_testmini_000000","prediction":"BD","reasoning":"The induced-current relations make B and D correct."} {"question_id":"level5_testmini_000000","prediction":"A","reasoning":"The diagram matches option A after comparing the labeled quantities."} ``` ## Evaluation Rules Submissions are evaluated against hidden reference answers. For each question, the evaluation system checks whether: 1. A final answer is provided. 2. A reasoning field is provided. 3. The reasoning is relevant to the problem. 4. The submitted final answer is equivalent to the reference answer. 5. The reasoning supports the submitted final answer. 6. Required units are respected when applicable. A response may receive no credit if it contains any of the following issues: - missing final answer; - missing reasoning; - malformed or unreadable submission format; - unrelated or empty reasoning; - reasoning that contradicts the submitted answer; - incorrect final answer; - missing or incorrect units when units are required. ## Leaderboard Metrics The public leaderboard reports performance on the released public evaluation split. | Metric | Questions | Weight in Overall | |---|---:|---:| | Level 1 | 200 | 200 / 830 | | Level 2 | 200 | 200 / 830 | | Level 3 | 200 | 200 / 830 | | Level 4 | 200 | 200 / 830 | | Solve150 Testmini | 30 | 30 / 830 | The overall score is computed as: ```text overall = total_correct_questions / 830 ``` The leaderboard also reports: - `Judged`: number of rows successfully evaluated; - `Missing`: number of rows missing either a prediction or reasoning; - `Invalid`: number of malformed rows or rows missing required fields. ## Dataset Release The Hugging Face dataset is released as a public no-answer version for participant use. It intentionally excludes the following ground-truth fields: - `answer` - `unit` - `error_range` These fields are reserved for private evaluation and are not distributed to participants. ## Notes Participants are responsible for ensuring that all `question_id` values match the released dataset exactly. Duplicate predictions for the same `question_id` are not allowed and may be treated as invalid.
pages.content_zh:
# SeePhys Pro 评估
重要提示：每份提交必须提供最终答案和支持说明。仅包含最终答案而没有推理过程的响应将不被认为是完整的。
## 提交格式 请在存档根目录提交包含公开和私有预测文件的 `.zip` 文件。 您的提交应包含： ```text submission.zip ├── prediction.csv └── prediction_private.csv ``` ### 必需文件 #### 1. `prediction.csv` 此文件用于 Codabench 公开排行榜。 #### 2. `prediction_private.csv` 此文件保留用于私有排行榜。 私有排行榜**不会**在 Codabench 上自动评估。在竞赛截止日期后，组织者将下载最终提交并使用私有参考答案离线评估 `prediction_private.csv`。 ### 必需字段 `prediction.csv` 和 `prediction_private.csv` 都必须包含以下字段： ```text question_id prediction reasoning ``` ### 重要说明 Codabench 上显示的公开排行榜分数仅从 `prediction.csv` 计算得出。 私有排行榜分数仅从 `prediction_private.csv` 离线计算得出。 如果缺少 `prediction_private.csv`，提交将无法获得有效的私有排行榜分数。 ## CSV 示例 ```csv question_id,prediction,reasoning level1_testmini_000000,BD,"两个电路分支给出所述的加速度和电荷关系，因此 B 和 D 是正确的。" level1_testmini_000001,1.44,"使用斯涅尔定律和测量的几何关系，折射率约为 1.44。" ``` ## JSONL 示例 ```jsonl {"question_id":"level1_testmini_000000","prediction":"BD","reasoning":"感应电流关系使 B 和 D 正确。"} {"question_id":"level5_testmini_000000","prediction":"A","reasoning":"比较标记的量后，图表与选项 A 相匹配。"} ``` ## 评估规则 提交针对隐藏的参考答案进行评估。对于每个问题，评估系统检查是否： 1. 提供了最终答案。 2. 提供了推理字段。 3. 推理与问题相关。 4. 提交的最终答案等价于参考答案。 5. 推理支持提交的最终答案。 6. 在适用时尊重所需的单位。 如果响应包含以下任何问题，可能无法获得分数： - 缺少最终答案； - 缺少推理； - 格式错误或提交格式不可读； - 无关或空白推理； - 与提交答案矛盾的推理； - 最终答案不正确； - 在需要单位时缺少或单位不正确。 ## 排行榜指标 公开排行榜报告已发布公开评估分割上的性能。 | 指标 | 问题数 | 总体权重 | |---|---:|---:| | 第 1 级 | 200 | 200 / 830 | | 第 2 级 | 200 | 200 / 830 | | 第 3 级 | 200 | 200 / 830 | | 第 4 级 | 200 | 200 / 830 | | Solve150 Testmini | 30 | 30 / 830 | 总体分数计算如下： ```text overall = total_correct_questions / 830 ``` 排行榜还报告： - `Judged`：成功评估的行数； - `Missing`：缺少预测或推理的行数； - `Invalid`：格式错误或缺少必需字段的行数。 ## 数据集发布 Hugging Face 数据集作为无答案公开版本发布供参与者使用。它故意不包括以下真值字段： - `answer` - `unit` - `error_range` 这些字段预留用于私有评估，不分发给参与者。 ## 说明 参与者有责任确保所有 `question_id` 值与发布的数据集完全匹配。同一 `question_id` 的重复预测不被允许，可能被视为无效。

pages.title: Awards
pages.content:
# Awards

The total prize pool for the **AI4Math @ ICML 2026** challenge is **$8,000**, distributed across all four tracks.

### Prize per Track

| Place | Prize per Track |
| :--- | :--- |
| 🥇 **1st** | $1,000 |
| 🥈 **2nd** | $600 |
| 🥉 **3rd** | $400 |

**Challenge Period:** May 1 – June 15, 2026.
pages.content_zh:
# 奖项

**AI4Math @ ICML 2026** 竞赛的总奖金池为 **8,000 美元**，分配给所有四个赛道。

### 每个赛道的奖金

| 名次 | 每个赛道的奖金 |
| :--- | :--- |
| 🥇 **第一名** | $1,000 |
| 🥈 **第二名** | $600 |
| 🥉 **第三名** | $400 |

**竞赛周期**：2026 年 5 月 1 日 – 6 月 15 日。

pages.title: Announcement
pages.content:
---
#### Notice, May 11, 2026

Dear participants,

Going forward, the following rules apply:

1. **Reproducibility is now required** for all submissions. You need to provide code or a clear description of your method. For the top 3 finalists, reproducibility is a condition for receiving your ranking and prize money.

2. Manual annotation of test samples is not a valid approach and will lead to disqualification.

3. We also **encourage everyone to write a short technical report** on your methodology and [submit it to the AI4Math Workshop](https://ai4math2026.github.io/#cfp) by May 24, 2026 AoE. Please start your paper title with "Technical Report for AI4Math-2026 Track n" followed by your own subtitle. It gives you more visibility and a chance to present your work in person at ICML.

Good luck!
Challenge Chairs

---

---
#### 通知，2026年5月11日

亲爱的参赛者，

1. **可重现性现已成为强制要求**，适用于所有提交的作品。您需要提供代码或清晰的方法描述。对于前3名入选者，可重现性是获得排名和奖金的必要条件。

2. 手动标注测试样本不是有效方法，将导致取消资格。

3. 我们**鼓励所有人撰写一份关于您方法论的简短技术报告**，并[在2026年5月24日午夜前（AoE时区）提交至AI4Math研讨会](https://ai4math2026.github.io/#cfp)。请在论文标题开头写上"AI4Math-2026第n赛道技术报告"，后跟您自己的副标题。这将增加您的曝光度，并为您提供在ICML大会上现场展示作品的机会。

祝您好运！
挑战主席

pages.title: Terms
pages.content:
# Seephys Pro Terms and Participation Rules

By participating in this challenge, participants agree to follow the rules below.

## Allowed

- Use the Hugging Face dataset for model development, prompting, analysis, and submission generation.
- Use external models and tools, unless they violate Codabench, Hugging Face, or API provider policies.
- Submit final answers with reasoning in the required format.

## Not Allowed

- Attempt to recover, infer, scrape, or leak hidden reference answers.
- Attack, overload, or bypass the Codabench evaluation system.
- Submit prompt-injection text intended to manipulate the judge rather than answer the physics problem.
- Submit empty, unrelated, or intentionally deceptive reasoning.
- Upload content that violates the policies of Codabench, Hugging Face, OpenAI, or applicable law.

## Evaluation Integrity

The public judge uses an OpenAI API based semantic evaluator. The judge is intended to evaluate answer equivalence and reasoning consistency, not to reward formatting tricks. Submissions may be reviewed or removed if they exploit the evaluator or violate the spirit of the benchmark.

## Data Use

The Hugging Face dataset distributed for this challenge excludes answer-bearing fields. Reference answers are used only for Codabench scoring.
pages.content_zh:
# Seephys Pro 条款与参与规则

参与本挑战的参赛者同意遵守以下规则。

## 允许的行为

- 使用Hugging Face数据集进行模型开发、提示工程、分析和提交生成。
- 使用外部模型和工具，但不得违反Codabench、Hugging Face或API提供商的政策。
- 按照要求的格式提交包含推理过程的最终答案。

## 禁止的行为

- 尝试恢复、推断、爬取或泄露隐藏的参考答案。
- 攻击、过载或绕过Codabench评估系统。
- 提交旨在操纵评判器而非回答物理问题的提示注入文本。
- 提交空白、无关或故意欺骗性的推理。
- 上传违反Codabench、Hugging Face、OpenAI或适用法律政策的内容。

## 评估完整性

公开评判器使用基于OpenAI API的语义评估器。评判器旨在评估答案等价性和推理一致性，而非奖励格式技巧。如果提交内容利用评估器漏洞或违反基准精神，可能被审查或删除。

## 数据使用

为本挑战分发的Hugging Face数据集不包含含答案的字段。参考答案仅用于Codabench评分。

pages.title: overview
pages.content:
The 2nd Workshop on Low-level Vision Frontiers (LoViF) @ ECCV 2026

 

Challenge on Real-World All-in-One Image Restoration

Important dates

  • 2026.05.20 Release of train data (input and output)
  • 2026.05.20 Release of validation data (inputs only)
  • 2026.05.20 Validation server online
  • 2026.07.01 Final test data release (inputs only)
  • 2026.07.08 Test output results submission deadline
  • 2026.07.08 Fact sheets and code/executable submission deadline
  • 2026.07.15 Preliminary test results release to the participants
  • 2026.07.15 Paper submission deadline for entries (Internal Review)
  • 2026.09.x LoViF workshop and challenges, results and award ceremony (ECCV 2026)

 

Challenge overview

The 2nd Workshop on Low-level Vision Frontiers with Generative AI, Preference Optimization and Agentic Systems (LoViF) will be held in September 2026 in conjunction with ECCV 2026.

Low-level vision is undergoing a paradigm shift. Traditional restoration methods are being augmented and redefined by Generative AI, Preference Optimization, and Agentic Systems. The LoViF workshop aims to explore these frontiers, focusing on how generative foundation models can provide superior priors, how human feedback can refine visual quality, and how intelligent agents can autonomously handle complex restoration tasks.

Recent advancements have shown that low-level tasks are no longer just about pixel-wise accuracy (like PSNR), but about perceptual richness and alignment with human intent. This evolution opens new doors in fields ranging from professional content creation to autonomous systems and wearable devices, where visual signal integrity and aesthetic quality are paramount.

This challenge, as a core part of the LoViF workshop, provides a platform for researchers and practitioners to benchmark their latest algorithms and push the boundaries of what is possible in next-generation image and video processing.

 

Motivation for this competition

While classic image restoration has seen significant progress, real-world scenarios often involve complex, non-linear degradations and subjective quality requirements that traditional metrics fail to capture. The emergence of Generative AI offers powerful tools to "re-imagine" lost details, yet maintaining high fidelity and aligning these outputs with human preferences remains a critical challenge.

To bridge this gap, the LoViF Challenge introduces a specialized benchmark focused on real-world all-in-one image restoration. We aim to encourage solutions that not only achieve high quantitative scores but also demonstrate superior visual quality and adaptability, potentially leveraging agent-based reasoning or preference-based refinement.

The goal of this competition is to establish a new and applicable benchmark for the community. The final ranking will be determined by a combination of standard metrics (e.g., PSNR, SSIM, LPIPS) and advanced perceptual or alignment-based evaluations.

Please refer to [1] [2] for more details about real-world all-in-one restoration data.

[1] Hao Li, Xiang Chen, Jiangxin Dong, Jinhui Tang, Jinshan Pan. "FoundIR: Unleashing Million-scale Training Data to Advance Foundation Models for Image Restoration", In: ICCV, 2025.

[2] Qiyuan Guan, Qianfeng Yang, Xiang Chen, Tianyu Song, Guiyue Jin, Jiyu Jin. "WeatherBench: A Real-World Benchmark Dataset for All-in-One Adverse Weather Image Restoration", In: ACM MM, 2025.

Top-ranked participants will be awarded and invited to submit their workshop papers to the ECCV 2026 Workshop proceedings, describing their winning solutions.

More details are found in the competition data section.

Competition

The training data will be made available to the registered participants according to the timeline.

Award

The following awards are provided:
  • One first-class award (i.e., the champion) with case prize US$1000;
  • One second-class award with case prize US$600;
  • One thrid-class award with case prize US$400;

Provided Resources

  • Scripts: The organizers will provide baseline models and evaluation scripts to ensure reproducibility and fair comparison. Check the "Data" page for more information.
  • Contact: Please use the forum on the challenge page for technical discussions, or contact the LoViF organizing committee via the official website: https://lovif-eccv2026-workshop.github.io/, or directly contact the challenge organizers by email ( chenxiang [at] njust.edu.cn and haoli [at] njust.edu.cn) if you have doubts or any question.
pages.content_zh:
第2届低级视觉前沿工作坊 (LoViF) @ ECCV 2026

 

真实世界全能图像恢复挑战赛

重要日期

  • 2026.05.20 发布训练数据（输入和输出）
  • 2026.05.20 发布验证数据（仅输入）
  • 2026.05.20 验证服务器上线
  • 2026.07.01 发布最终测试数据（仅输入）
  • 2026.07.08 测试输出结果提交截止日期
  • 2026.07.08 事实表和代码/可执行文件提交截止日期
  • 2026.07.15 向参赛者发布初步测试结果
  • 2026.07.15 参赛论文提交截止日期（内部评审）
  • 2026.09.x LoViF工作坊和挑战赛、结果及颁奖仪式（ECCV 2026）

 

挑战赛概览

第2届低级视觉前沿工作坊——生成式AI、偏好优化与智能体系统 (LoViF)将于2026年9月与ECCV 2026联合举办。

低级视觉正在经历范式转变。传统恢复方法正在被生成式AI、偏好优化和智能体系统所增强和重新定义。LoViF工作坊旨在探索这些前沿方向，重点关注生成式基础模型如何提供更优的先验知识、人类反馈如何优化视觉质量，以及智能体如何自主处理复杂恢复任务。

最近的进展表明，低级任务不再仅仅是像素级精度（如PSNR），而是关于感知丰富度和与人类意图的对齐。这一演变在专业内容创建、自主系统和可穿戴设备等领域开启了新的可能，其中视觉信号完整性和美学质量至关重要。

本挑战赛作为LoViF工作坊的核心部分，为研究人员和从业者提供了一个平台，用于基准测试其最新算法，并推动下一代图像和视频处理技术的边界。

 

竞赛的动机

虽然经典图像恢复取得了显著进展，但真实场景往往涉及复杂的非线性退化和主观质量要求，这是传统指标无法捕捉的。生成式AI的出现提供了强大的工具来"重新想象"丢失的细节，但保持高保真度并使这些输出与人类偏好对齐仍然是一个关键挑战。

为了弥合这一差距，LoViF挑战赛引入了一个专门的基准，重点关注真实世界全能图像恢复。我们旨在鼓励不仅能取得高定量分数，而且表现出卓越视觉质量和适应性的解决方案，可能利用基于智能体的推理或基于偏好的细化。

本竞赛的目标是为社区建立一个新的、可应用的基准。最终排名将由标准指标（如PSNR、SSIM、LPIPS）和先进的感知或对齐评估的组合决定。

请参考 [1] [2]了解有关真实世界全能恢复数据的更多详情。

[1] Hao Li, Xiang Chen, Jiangxin Dong, Jinhui Tang, Jinshan Pan. "FoundIR: Unleashing Million-scale Training Data to Advance Foundation Models

pages.title: data
pages.content:
The 2nd Workshop on Low-level Vision Frontiers (LoViF) @ ECCV 2026

 

Challenge on Real-World All-in-One Image Restoration

 

Data overview

The dataset for this challenge is provided by FoundIR and the LoViF organizing committee. It is specifically designed to benchmark the frontiers of low-level vision, incorporating five categories of common real-world degraded images: blur, low-light, haze, rain, and snow. The dataset includes diverse scenes for training, validation, and testing. Specifically, each degradation contains 4,900 paired LQ–GT image (512 X 512) pairs for training, 100 images for validation, and 100 images for testing.

In this competition, the dataset is composed of:

  • Train data: 24,500 pairs of degraded images and their corresponding ground truth references.
  • Validation data: 500 degraded images for performance tuning during the development phase.
  • Test data: 500 degraded images reserved for final performance evaluation.

Please note that in both the validation and test sets, images indexed from 0001-0100 correspond to Blur, 0101-0200 to Low-light, 0201-0300 to Haze, 0301-0400 to Rain, and 0401-0500 to Snow.

 

Data access

By accessing the data, participants implicitly agree with the terms and conditions of the LoViF challenge.

 

    • Development phase / Learning:
      • Train Data: [Google Drive]. Participants are allowed to use publicly available external datasets for training, as long as the sources of the data are explicitly documented in the factsheet. More details about the training data can be found in FoundIR.
      • Validation Data (input images only): [Google Drive].
      • Test Data (input images only): [TBA]

 

Final test phase submission guidelines (Factsheet can be found in [overleaf])

The final submission should be emailed to the organizers (see the "Organizers" tab for contact details).

The title of the mail should be: [LoViF2026] - [TASK_NAME] - [TEAM_NAME]

The body of the mail shall include the following:

      1. Challenge full name (LoViF 2026 - [Task Name])
      2. Team name
      3. Team leader's name and email address
      4. Rest of the team members
      5. Affiliations of the team members
      6. Team name and user names on the LoViF 2026 Codabench server
      7. Executable/source code attached or download links
      8. Factsheet attached (please use the provided LoViF template)
      9. Download link(s) to the FULL results of ALL test frames

The executable/source code should include trained models or necessary parameters to ensure reproducibility. A README file explaining how to run the code is mandatory. The Factsheet must be a compiled PDF file along with its LaTeX source files.

 

Final test phase - preliminary results [TBA]

Preliminary results will be posted here once the evaluation is complete.

Note that top-ranked participants will be invited to co-author the challenge report and contribute a paper to the LoViF 2026 workshop. All challenge papers will undergo a peer-review process. For more information, please check the LoViF 2026 official website.
pages.content_zh:
第二届低级视觉前沿研讨会 (LoViF) @ ECCV 2026

 

真实世界全能图像修复挑战赛

 

数据概览

本挑战赛的数据集由 FoundIR 和 LoViF 组织委员会提供。该数据集专门设计用于评估低级视觉的前沿技术，包含五类常见真实退化图像：模糊、低光、雾霾、雨和雪。数据集包含用于训练、验证和测试的多样化场景。具体来说，每种退化类型包含 4,900 对配对的 LQ-GT 图像对（512 X 512）用于训练，100 张图像用于验证，100 张图像用于测试。

在本竞赛中，数据集包含：

  • 训练数据：24,500 对退化图像及其对应的真值参考。
  • 验证数据：500 张退化图像，用于开发阶段的性能调优。
  • 测试数据：500 张退化图像，用于最终性能评估。

请注意，在验证集和测试集中，索引编号 0001-0100 对应模糊，0101-0200 对应低光，0201-0300 对应雾霾，0301-0400 对应雨，0401-0500 对应雪。

 

数据获取

通过访问数据，参赛者默认同意 LoViF 挑战赛的条款和条件。

 

    • 开发阶段/学习：
      • 训练数据：[Google Drive]。参赛者可以使用公开可用的外部数据集进行训练，但必须在信息表中明确记录数据来源。有关训练数据的更多详情，请参见 FoundIR。
      • 验证数据（仅输入图像）：[Google Drive]。
      • 测试数据（仅输入图像）：[待定]

 

最终测试阶段提交指南（信息表可在 [overleaf] 中找到）

最终提交应通过电子邮件发送给组织者（请参见"组织者"标签了解联系方式）。

邮件主题应为：[LoViF2026] - [任务名称] - [团队名称]

邮件正文应包含以下内容：

      1. 挑战赛全名（LoViF 2026 - [任务名称]）
      2. 团队名称
      3. 团队负责人的名字和电子邮件地址
      4. 其他团队成员
      5. 团队成员的所属机构
      6. LoViF 2026 Codabench 服务器上的团队名称和用户名
      7. 附加可执行程序/源代码或下载链接
      8. 附加信息表（请使用提供的 LoViF 模板）
      9. 所有测试帧完整结果的下载链接

可执行程序/源代码应包含经过训练的模型或必要参数，以确保可重现性。必须包含说明如何运行代码的 README 文件。信息表必须是编译后的 PDF 文件及其 LaTeX 源文件。

 

最终测试阶段 - 初步结果 [待定]

评估完成后，初步结果将在此处发布。

请注意，排名靠前的参赛者将被邀请共同撰写挑战赛报告，并为 LoViF 2026 研讨会投稿论文。所有挑战赛论文将经过同行评审。有关更多信息，请查看相关页面。

pages.title: evaluation
pages.content:
The 2nd Workshop on Low-level Vision Frontiers (LoViF) @ ECCV 2026

 

Challenge on Real-World All-in-One Image Restoration

 

Evaluation

The evaluation consists of the comparison of the restored/generated images with the reference ground truth images. Please ensure all images are processed using a unified model to maintain consistency across different test scenarios.

To comprehensively assess performance in the frontiers of low-level vision, we consider both fidelity-based and perception-based metrics, including PSNR, SSIM, and LPIPS.

As the goal of this challenge is to explore a robust and powerful all-in-one image restoration model, participants are required to use one restoration network to handle all types of degradations and obtain the final results. Specifically, participants should ensure that their final submission contains only one trained image restoration model parameter file (e.g., .pt, .pth), which facilitates reproducibility of the experimental results by the organizers. It should be noted that the use of existing pre-trained networks (e.g., VAE, CLIP) for feature extraction is allowed and will not be counted as the aforementioned model parameter file.

The final ranking will be determined by a composite score that balances pixel-wise accuracy and perceptual quality. The weighted formula for the initial results is:
Final_Score = PSNR (Y) + 10 * SSIM (Y) - 5 * LPIPS.

For submitting the results, please follow the instructions below:

  1. To ensure fairness in evaluation, participants are recommended to save the model's output images in JPG format using the code provided below. 
    
from PIL import Image
img = Image.open(img_path)
img.load()
if img.mode != "RGB":
    img = img.convert("RGB")
save_kwargs = dict(format="JPEG", quality=96, subsampling=0, optimize=True)
img.save(out_path, **save_kwargs)
  2. Process the input images and keep the same name for the output image results as produced by your method (example: for an input file with the name "0001.jpg" the output file should be "0001.jpg"). Note that the output images should have the size of the input images. [Submission example] .
  3. Ensure your submission includes a short description of your method (Fact Sheet).
  4. Submit your results to the Codabench server before the deadline.

 

For more details regarding the workshop themes such as Preference Optimization or Agentic Systems in evaluation, please visit our official website: https://lovif-eccv2026-workshop.github.io/
pages.content_zh:
第2届低层次视觉前沿工作坊（LoViF）@ ECCV 2026

 

真实世界全能图像恢复挑战赛

 

评估

评估包括将恢复/生成的图像与参考真实图像进行比较。请确保所有图像都使用统一的模型进行处理，以保持不同测试场景下的一致性。

为了全面评估低层次视觉前沿领域的性能，我们同时考虑保真度指标和感知指标，包括PSNR、SSIM和LPIPS。

由于本挑战赛的目标是探索一个鲁棒且强大的全能图像恢复模型，参赛者需要使用一个恢复网络来处理所有类型的图像退化并获得最终结果。 具体来说，参赛者应确保其最终提交仅包含一个已训练的图像恢复模型参数文件（例如.pt、.pth），这有利于赛事组织者重现实验结果。 需要注意的是，允许使用现有的预训练网络（例如VAE、CLIP）进行特征提取，这些不计入上述模型参数文件。

最终排名将由平衡像素级精度和感知质量的综合评分确定。初始结果的加权公式为：
Final_Score = PSNR (Y) + 10 * SSIM (Y) - 5 * LPIPS。

提交结果时，请按以下说明操作：

  1. 为确保评估的公平性，参赛者建议使用以下提供的代码将模型输出图像保存为JPG格式。 
    
from PIL import Image
img = Image.open(img_path)
img.load()
if img.mode != "RGB":
    img = img.convert("RGB")
save_kwargs = dict(format="JPEG", quality=96, subsampling=0, optimize=True)
img.save(out_path, **save_kwargs)
  2. 处理输入图像，并保持输出图像结果的名称与您的方法生成的名称相同（例如：输入文件名为"0001.jpg"，输出文件应为"0001.jpg"）。注意输出图像的尺寸应与输入图像相同。[提交示例]。
  3. 确保您的提交包含方法的简短描述（事实说明书）。
  4. 在截止日期前将结果提交到Codabench服务器。

 

有关工作坊主题的更多详情，如偏好优化或评估中的智能体系统，请访问我们的官方网站：https://lovif-eccv2026-workshop.github.io/

16121.title: MISP Challenge 2026
16121.description: The challenge focuses on audio-visual-text multimodal keyword spotting task in realistic speech processing scenarios.
16121.description_zh: 该挑战重点关注现实语音处理场景中的音视频文本多模态关键词识别任务。

pages.title: overview
pages.content:
🏆 MISP Challenge 2026

Audio-Visual-Text Multimodal Keyword Spotting Challenge

📋 Overview

This competition focuses on audio-visual-text multimodal keyword spotting task in realistic speech processing scenarios.

Task Description

Your model needs to determine whether given audio-video pairs come from the same speaker and the same keyword.

inset vs outset

Term Description
inset Keywords seen during training
outset New keywords not seen during training

Evaluation Metrics

AUC_INSET

AUC on inset keywords
Higher is better

EER_INSET

EER on inset (%)
Lower is better

AUC_OUTSET

AUC on outset keywords
Higher is better

EER_OUTSET

EER on outset (%)
Lower is better

🚀 Quick Start - 5 Steps to Submit

1
Generate Predictions

Run your model on the test set and collect prediction results

2
Prepare CSV

Save predictions in the required CSV format

3
Compress CSV

Directly compress CSV file to zip format

4
Upload to Codabench

Submit your zip file on this platform

5
View Results

Wait for scoring and check the leaderboard!

⚠️ Important Update:

You do NOT need to submit code or models! Only a CSV file with your predictions is required.

📊 Metric Interpretation

AUC (Area Under Curve)

  • Value Range: 0.0 - 1.0
  • Perfect Score: 1.0
  • Random Score: 0.5
  • Higher AUC indicates better model discrimination ability

EER (Equal Error Rate)

  • Value Range: 0% - 100%
  • Perfect Score: 0%
  • Random Score: 50%
  • Lower EER indicates better model balance performance

📞 Technical Support

If you encounter issues during submission, please check the Data & Submission Guide tab or use the competition forum.

🏆 MISP Challenge 2026

音频-视频-文本多模态关键词检测竞赛

📋 竞赛概述

本次竞赛聚焦于真实语音处理场景下的音频-视频-文本多模态关键词检测任务。

任务简介

您的模型需要判断给定的音频-视频对是否来自同一说话人和同一关键词。

inset vs outset

术语 说明
inset 训练时见过的词汇
outset 训练时未见过的新词汇

评价指标

AUC_INSET

inset词汇的AUC
越高越好

EER_INSET

inset词汇的EER (%)
越低越好

AUC_OUTSET

outset词汇的AUC
越高越好

EER_OUTSET

outset词汇的EER (%)
越低越好

🚀 快速入门 - 5步完成提交

1
生成预测

在测试集上运行您的模型，收集预测结果

2
准备CSV

按照要求格式保存预测结果为CSV

3
压缩CSV

直接将CSV文件压缩为zip格式

4
上传到Codabench

在此平台提交您的zip文件

5
查看结果

等待评分，查看排行榜！

⚠️ 重要更新:

您不需要提交代码和模型！只需提交预测结果的CSV文件即可。

📊 指标解读

AUC (Area Under Curve)

  • 取值范围: 0.0 - 1.0
  • 完美分数: 1.0
  • 随机分数: 0.5
  • AUC越高表示模型的区分能力越好

EER (Equal Error Rate)

  • 取值范围: 0% - 100%
  • 完美分数: 0%
  • 随机分数: 50%
  • EER越低表示模型的平衡性能越好

📞 技术支持

如果在提交过程中遇到问题，请查看数据与提交指南标签页，或使用竞赛论坛。
pages.content_zh:
# 🏆 MISP竞赛2026

音视频-文本多模态关键词识别挑战赛

## 📋 概述

本竞赛重点关注真实语音处理场景中的音视频-文本多模态关键词识别任务。

### 任务描述

您的模型需要判断给定的音频-视频对是否来自同一说话者和同一关键词。

### 内集 vs 外集

| 术语 | 描述 |
|------|------|
| **内集（inset）** | 训练期间看到的关键词 |
| **外集（outset）** | 训练期间未看到的新关键词 |

### 评估指标

**AUC_内集**
- 内集关键词的AUC
- 越高越好

**EER_内集**
- 内集的EER (%)
- 越低越好

**AUC_外集**
- 外集关键词的AUC
- 越高越好

**EER_外集**
- 外集的EER (%)
- 越低越好

## 🚀 快速开始 - 5步提交

1. **生成预测**
   运行您的模型在测试集上并收集预测结果

2. **准备CSV**
   以所需的CSV格式保存预测结果

3. **压缩CSV**

pages.title: data
pages.content:
📊 Data & Submission Guide

Complete guide for data format and submission requirements

📁 Submission Requirements

⚠️ Important:

You only need to submit a zip file containing ONE CSV file. No code or model files required!

File Required Description
predictions.csv ✅ Required Your prediction results (any filename ending with .csv works)
submission.zip ✅ Required Zip file containing only the CSV file

📝 CSV Format Specification

Required Columns

Column Name Type Description
id Integer Sample index, starting from 0
type String Must be either "inset" or "outset"
prediction Float Prediction score between 0.0 and 1.0

Important Rules

  • id Numbering: Both inset and outset must start from 0 and be numbered separately
  • Prediction Scores: Must be float values between 0.0 and 1.0 representing match probability
    • Closer to 1 means more likely to be a match
    • Closer to 0 means less likely to be a match
  • Sample Order: Prediction order must strictly match the test set order
  • Completeness: Must include predictions for all test samples

CSV Example

id,type,prediction
0,inset,0.85
1,inset,0.12
2,inset,0.95
3,inset,0.34
...
99,inset,0.67
0,outset,0.45
1,outset,0.78
2,outset,0.23
...
99,outset,0.56

💻 How to Generate Prediction CSV

Python Code Example

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

def generate_predictions_csv(inset_predictions, outset_predictions, output_file="predictions.csv"):
    """
    Generate CSV file for competition submission

    Args:
        inset_predictions: list/array, inset prediction scores (0-1 between)
        outset_predictions: list/array, outset prediction scores (0-1 between)
        output_file: output filename
    """
    data = []

    # Add inset predictions
    for i, pred in enumerate(inset_predictions):
        data.append({
            'id': i,
            'type': 'inset',
            'prediction': pred
        })

    # Add outset predictions
    for i, pred in enumerate(outset_predictions):
        data.append({
            'id': i,
            'type': 'outset',
            'prediction': pred
        })

    # Create DataFrame and save
    df = pd.DataFrame(data)
    df.to_csv(output_file, index=False)

    print(f"✅ Prediction CSV generated: {output_file}")
    print(f"   - inset samples: {len(inset_predictions)}")
    print(f"   - outset samples: {len(outset_predictions)}")
    print(f"   - Total samples: {len(data)}")

    return df

# Usage example
if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Replace with your actual predictions
    num_inset = 100   # Replace with actual number of inset samples
    num_outset = 100  # Replace with actual number of outset samples

    my_inset_preds = np.random.rand(num_inset)   # Replace with your predictions
    my_outset_preds = np.random.rand(num_outset) # Replace with your predictions

    # Generate CSV
    generate_predictions_csv(my_inset_preds, my_outset_preds, "predictions.csv")

Prediction Value Notes

✅ Correct Prediction Values:
  • Floats between 0.0 and 1.0
  • Probability values after sigmoid or softmax
❌ Incorrect Prediction Values:
  • Negative numbers: -0.1
  • Numbers greater than 1: 1.5
  • Logits (not through sigmoid): 2.3, -1.2

📤 How to Submit

Submission Steps

  1. Prepare CSV File: Generate predictions.csv following the instructions above
  2. Compress File: Compress the CSV file to zip format
    • Windows: Right-click CSV file → Send to → Compressed (zipped) folder
    • Mac/Linux: zip submission.zip predictions.csv
  3. Upload to Codabench: Click "Submit" button and upload your zip file
  4. Wait for Processing: Platform will automatically process your submission
  5. View Results: After processing is complete, results will appear on the leaderboard
⚠️ Important Notes:
  • Ensure the zip contains only one CSV file
  • Do not include folders in the zip
  • Ensure CSV format is correct
  • Prediction order must be correct

📥 Downloads

Available Downloads

  • Test Dataset - Preprocessed test data
  • Sample Predictions - Example CSV file showing correct format
  • Example Code - Python script for generating submission CSV

📊 数据与提交指南

数据格式和提交要求的完整指南

📁 提交要求

⚠️ 重要:

您只需要提交一个包含CSV文件的zip文件！不需要提交代码或模型文件！

文件 必需 说明
predictions.csv ✅ 必需 您的预测结果（任何以.csv结尾的文件名都可以）
submission.zip ✅ 必需 只包含CSV文件的zip文件

📝 CSV格式说明

必需列

列名 类型 说明
id 整数 样本编号，从0开始
type 字符串 必须是 "inset" 或 "outset"
prediction 浮点数 0.0到1.0之间的预测分数

重要规则

  • id编号: inset和outset必须分别从0开始编号
  • 预测分数: 必须是0.0到1.0之间的浮点数，表示匹配概率
    • 越接近1表示越可能匹配
    • 越接近0表示越不可能匹配
  • 样本顺序: 预测顺序必须严格与测试集顺序一致
  • 完整性: 必须包含所有测试样本的预测

CSV示例

id,type,prediction
0,inset,0.85
1,inset,0.12
2,inset,0.95
3,inset,0.34
...
99,inset,0.67
0,outset,0.45
1,outset,0.78
2,outset,0.23
...
99,outset,0.56

💻 如何生成预测CSV

Python代码示例

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

def generate_predictions_csv(inset_predictions, outset_predictions, output_file="predictions.csv"):
    """
    生成竞赛提交用的CSV文件

    参数:
        inset_predictions: list/array，inset的预测分数（0-1之间）
        outset_predictions: list/array，outset的预测分数（0-1之间）
        output_file: 输出文件名
    """
    data = []

    # 添加inset预测
    for i, pred in enumerate(inset_predictions):
        data.append({
            'id': i,
            'type': 'inset',
            'prediction': pred
        })

    # 添加outset预测
    for i, pred in enumerate(outset_predictions):
        data.append({
            'id': i,
            'type': 'outset',
            'prediction': pred
        })

    # 创建DataFrame并保存
    df = pd.DataFrame(data)
    df.to_csv(output_file, index=False)

    print(f"✅ 预测CSV已生成: {output_file}")
    print(f"   - inset样本数: {len(inset_predictions)}")
    print(f"   - outset样本数: {len(outset_predictions)}")
    print(f"   - 总样本数: {len(data)}")

    return df

# 使用示例
if __name__ == "__main__":
    # 替换为您的实际预测
    num_inset = 100   # 替换为实际的inset样本数
    num_outset = 100  # 替换为实际的outset样本数

    my_inset_preds = np.random.rand(num_inset)   # 替换为您的预测
    my_outset_preds = np.random.rand(num_outset) # 替换为您的预测

    # 生成CSV
    generate_predictions_csv(my_inset_preds, my_outset_preds, "predictions.csv")

预测值注意事项

✅ 正确的预测值:
  • 0.0到1.0之间的浮点数
  • 经过sigmoid或softmax后的概率值
❌ 错误的预测值:
  • 负数: -0.1
  • 大于1的数: 1.5
  • logits（未经过sigmoid）: 2.3, -1.2

📤 如何提交

提交步骤

  1. 准备CSV文件: 按照上述说明生成predictions.csv
  2. 压缩文件: 将CSV文件压缩为zip格式
    • Windows: 右键CSV文件 → 发送到 → 压缩(zipped)文件夹
    • Mac/Linux: zip submission.zip predictions.csv
  3. 上传到Codabench: 点击"提交"按钮，上传您的zip文件
  4. 等待处理: 平台会自动处理您的提交
  5. 查看结果: 处理完成后，结果将显示在排行榜上
⚠️ 重要注意事项:
  • 确保zip中只包含一个CSV文件
  • zip中不要包含文件夹
  • 确保CSV格式正确
  • 预测顺序必须正确

📥 下载

可用下载

  • 测试数据集 - 预处理后的测试数据
  • 示例预测 - 展示正确格式的示例CSV文件
  • 示例代码 - 用于生成提交CSV的Python脚本

⚠️ 重要提示：

📝 CSV格式规范

16375.title: EventAid Frame Interpolation Challenge @ EBMV Workshop, ECCV 2026
16375.description: Event-based RGB frame interpolation with 1, 3, 7, and 15 skipped-frame settings, based on EventAid benchmark. EventAid Challenge is a track of the EBMV (Event-Based Multimodal Vision: Imaging, Perception, and Understanding) Workshop @ ECCV 2026!
16375.description_zh: 基于EventAid基准的事件驱动RGB帧插值，包含1、3、7和15帧跳跃设置。EventAid挑战赛是ECCV 2026事件驱动多模态视觉（EBMV）研讨会的一个赛道（事件驱动多模态视觉：成像、感知和理解）！

pages.title: Participation
pages.content:
# EventAid Frame Interpolation Challenge

This challenge is a track of the EBMV (Event-Based Multimodal Vision: Imaging, Perception, and Understanding) Workshop @ ECCV 2026! Workshop homepage: https://eventbasemultimodalvision.github.io/

If you encounter any issues, please contact the organizers: Peiqi Duan (duanqi0001@pku.edu.cn), Boyu Li (liboyu@pku.edu.cn), or Hanyue Lou (hylz@pku.edu.cn).

## Task

This challenge is an event-based frame interpolation task. For each sequence, a method is given:

- a sparse set of RGB input frames,
- event streams between frames,
- timestamps and frame metadata.

The goal is to reconstruct every frame marked as `[TODO]` in `frame_info.txt`.

The data is organized into four interpolation settings:

- `1skip`: reconstruct 1 missing frame between neighboring input frames.
- `3skip`: reconstruct 3 missing frames between neighboring input frames.
- `7skip`: reconstruct 7 missing frames between neighboring input frames.
- `15skip`: reconstruct 15 missing frames between neighboring input frames.

Larger skip settings have wider temporal gaps and are more difficult.

## Data Usage

This challenge uses a subset of the publicly released EventAid dataset. The full public EventAid dataset includes ground-truth data for scenes that overlap with this challenge, and using it can easily cause overfitting or data leakage even if the ground truth is not directly inspected. Therefore, participants must **not** use the publicly released EventAid dataset, or any data derived from it, for training, validation, model selection, hyperparameter tuning, pretraining, or any other part of method development.

Participants may use the data officially provided for this challenge, including the released input data and the validation sequence ground truth, for developing and validating their methods. The validation sequence is provided only to help participants verify the data format and evaluation pipeline; it is not part of the final scored test set.

Participants may use any other real or synthetic datasets, pretrained models, or external resources, provided that they are legally accessible and are not based on the public EventAid dataset. All external datasets and pretrained resources must be clearly disclosed in the final technical report. Example training datasources include: Synthetic EVFI data based on video datasets such as [GOPRO](https://github.com/SeungjunNah/DeepDeblur_release), [REDS](https://seungjunnah.github.io/Datasets/reds) and [Adobe240FPS](https://www.cs.ubc.ca/labs/imager/tr/2017/DeepVideoDeblurring/), and event simulators such as [ESIM](https://github.com/uzh-rpg/rpg_esim), [V2E](https://github.com/SensorsINI/v2e) and [V2V](https://github.com/HYLZ-2019/V2V); Real EVFI datasets such as [HQ-EVFI](https://openimaginglab.github.io/TimeLens-XL/) and [ERF-X170FPS](https://github.com/intelpro/CBMNet).

The hidden test set must only be used for official evaluation. Participants must not attempt to access, reconstruct, manually annotate, or reverse-engineer the hidden ground-truth data.

## Public Data

Download `challenge_data.zip` from the public data tab and extract it:

```bash
unzip challenge_data.zip
```

The extracted directory contains:

```text
challenge_data/
  validation/
    {1skip,3skip,7skip,15skip}/{sequence}/
      input/
      event/
      gt/
      event_info.txt
      frame_info.txt
      shape.txt
  test/
    {1skip,3skip,7skip,15skip}/{sequence}/
      input/
      event/
      event_info.txt
      frame_info.txt
      shape.txt
  run_baseline_method.py
  evaluate_results.py
  terms.md
```

The validation split includes ground-truth frames under `gt/` so that participants can verify their pipeline locally. The test split does not include ground truth and is used for official ranking.

## Submission Format

Submit only reconstructed `[TODO]` frames. The required layout is:

```text
results/
  {skip}/
    {sequence}/
      {frame_index:06d}.png
```

For example:

```text
results/1skip/ball/000001.png
results/15skip/traffic/000016.png
```

All submitted images must be PNG files with the exact `width height` in the corresponding `shape.txt`.

Create the submission zip from the directory containing `results/`:

```bash
zip -r submission.zip results
```

## Local Baseline and Evaluation

A simple linear interpolation baseline is provided. It ignores events and interpolates between neighboring input frames:

```bash
python challenge_data/run_baseline_method.py \
  --data-dir challenge_data \
  --output-dir results
```

After producing a `results/` directory, run:

```bash
python challenge_data/evaluate_results.py \
  --data-dir challenge_data \
  --results-dir results
```

This script checks the submission format for both validation and test splits, and computes PSNR/SSIM only on the public validation ground truth. The script cannot compute test-set metrics because test ground truth is not public.

## Evaluation Phases

The validation phase reports PSNR and SSIM on the public validation split. After the validation phase closes, the organizers evaluate each team's last valid validation-phase submission on the hidden test split for the final ranking.
pages.content_zh:
# EventAid帧插值挑战赛

本挑战是ECCV 2026 EBMV（基于事件的多模态视觉：成像、感知与理解）研讨会的一个赛道！研讨会主页：https://eventbasemultimodalvision.github.io/

如遇到任何问题，请联系组织者：Peiqi Duan (duanqi0001@pku.edu.cn)、Boyu Li (liboyu@pku.edu.cn) 或 Hanyue Lou (hylz@pku.edu.cn)。

## 任务

本挑战是一个基于事件的帧插值任务。对于每个序列，给定以下信息：

- 一组稀疏的RGB输入帧，
- 帧间的事件流，
- 时间戳和帧元数据。

目标是重建在`frame_info.txt`中标记为`[TODO]`的每一帧。

数据分为四个插值设置：

- `1skip`：在相邻输入帧之间重建1个缺失帧。
- `3skip`：在相邻输入帧之间重建3个缺失帧。
- `7skip`：在相邻输入帧之间重建7个缺失帧。
- `15skip`：在相邻输入帧之间重建15个缺失帧。

更大的skip设置具有更宽的时间间隔，难度更高。

## 数据使用

本挑战使用公开发布的EventAid数据集的子集。完整的公开EventAid数据集包含与本挑战重叠的场景的真实数据，使用它会导致严重的过拟合或数据泄露，即使不直接查看真实数据亦然。因此，参赛者**必须不**使用公开发布的EventAid数据集或其任何衍生数据进行训练、验证、模型选择、超参数调整、预训练或方法开发的任何其他部分。

参赛者可使用本挑战官方提供的数据，包括已发布的输入数据和验证序列的真实数据，用于开发和验证其方法。验证序列仅用于帮助参赛者验证数据格式和评估流程；它不是最终评分测试集的一部分。

参赛者可使用任何其他真实或合成数据集、预训练模型或外部资源，前提是它们在法律上可访问且不基于公开的EventAid数据集。所有外部数据集和预训练资源必须在最终技术报告中清楚地披露。训练数据源示例包括：基于[GOPRO](https://github.com/SeungjunNah/DeepDeblur_release)、[REDS](https://seungjunnah.github.io/Datasets/reds)和[Adobe240FPS](https://www.cs.ubc.ca/labs/imager/tr/2017/DeepVideoDeblurring/)等视频数据集的合成EVFI数据，以及[ESIM](https://github.com/uzh-rpg/rpg_esim)、[V2E](https://github.com/SensorsINI/v2e)和[V2V](https://github.com/HYLZ-2019/V2V)等事件模拟器；真实EVFI数据集如[HQ-EVFI](https://openimaginglab.github.io/TimeLens-XL/)和[ERF-X170FPS](https://github.com/intelpro/CBMNet)。

隐藏测试集只能用于官方评估。参赛者不得尝试访问、重建、手动标注或逆向工程隐藏的真实数据。

## 公开数据

从公开数据选项卡下载`challenge_data.zip`并解压：

```bash
unzip challenge_data.zip
```

解压后的目录包含：

```text
challenge_data/
  validation/
    {1skip,3skip,7skip,15skip}/{sequence}/
      input/
      event/
      gt/
      event_info.txt
      frame_info.txt
      shape.txt
  test/
    {1skip,3skip,7skip,15skip}/{sequence}/
      input/
      event/
      event_info.txt
      frame_info.txt
      shape.txt
  run_baseline_method.py
  evaluate_results.py
  terms.md
```

验证集在`gt/`下包含真实帧，以便参赛者在本地验证其流程。测试集不包含真实数据，用于官方排名。

## 提交格式

仅提交重建的`[TODO]`帧。所需的目录结构为：

```text
results/
  {skip}/
    {sequence}/
      {frame_index:06d}.png
```

例如：

```text
results/1skip/ball
```

pages.title: Timeline
pages.content:
# Timeline

## Validation Phase

The validation phase is open until **June 30, 2026**. Participants may submit reconstructed frames and receive validation PSNR/SSIM feedback.

## Final Evaluation

After the validation phase closes, the organizers evaluate each team's last valid validation-phase submission on the hidden test split.

Final results will be announced on **July 5, 2026**.

## Technical Report

Technical reports are due on **July 10, 2026**. Reports should describe the method, training data, pretrained models, and any external resources used.

## 最终评估

验证阶段关闭后，组织者在隐藏测试集上评估每支队伍最后一份有效的验证阶段提交。

16261.title: Event-Based Multimodal Perception Challenge: Semantic Segmentation Track @ ECCV 2026

pages.title: Overview
pages.content:
# Event-Based Multimodal Perception Challenge: Semantic Segmentation Track @ ECCV 2026

Welcome to the **Semantic Segmentation Track** of the **Event-Based Multimodal Perception Challenge**, officially hosted at **ECCV 2026**!

## 🎯 Overview & Objective

This challenge aims to advance **semantic segmentation** using **event-based vision and frame data**. Unlike conventional RGB cameras, event cameras asynchronously capture brightness changes with high temporal resolution, low latency, and high dynamic range, making them particularly suitable for challenging scenarios such as high-speed motion, low-light environments, and scenes with strong illumination changes.

The objective of this track is to benchmark and promote robust semantic segmentation methods that can effectively leverage complementary information from **event streams and intensity frames**. Participants are expected to develop models that predict accurate pixel-level semantic labels from multimodal inputs, with a particular focus on generalization, robustness, and scene understanding under challenging visual conditions.

## 📝 Task Description

Participants are required to submit predicted semantic segmentation maps for the test set. Each prediction should follow the official submission format and correspond to the provided event and frame input data.

The task focuses on:

- **Semantic Segmentation**: Predicting pixel-level semantic labels from event and frame data.
- **Multimodal Fusion**: Effectively combining asynchronous event streams with image frames for semantic perception.
- **Pixel-Level Scene Understanding**: Assigning each valid pixel to a predefined semantic category.
- **Robust Perception**: Handling challenging visual conditions such as low illumination, high dynamic range.

## 📅 Important Dates

All deadlines are at **11:59 PM UTC**, unless otherwise specified.

- **May 13, 2026**: Challenge Website Opens.
- **May 15, 2026**: Training Set Release.
- **June 20, 2026**: Test Set & Evaluation Server Online.
- **June 30, 2026**: Challenge Submission Deadline.
- **July 5, 2026**: Challenge Results Announcement.
- **July 10, 2026**: Technical Report Deadline.

## 📊 Dataset

This track uses subsets of the **[CoSEC dataset](https://cosec.org.cn/)** and the **[REAL dataset](https://github.com/jinghan-xu/NEC-Diff)**. The dataset provides multimodal sensory data, including image frames, event streams, and semantic segmentation annotations.

The released data for this track includes:

- **Image Frames**: RGB frames captured from a monocular camera.
- **Event Streams**: Asynchronous event data recorded by an event camera.
- **Semantic Segmentation Labels**: Ground-truth pixel-level semantic labels provided for supervised training and validation.

The training sets are released for model development and performance validation. The test set will be used for official evaluation, and its ground-truth semantic segmentation labels will remain hidden.

The dataset statistics for this track are summarized below:

| Split | Number of Samples | 
|---|---:|
| Training | 2569 |
| Test | To be released | 

## ⚠️ Challenge Rules

To maintain fairness and scientific integrity, all participants must strictly follow the challenge rules:

- **No Test-Set Training**: The test set must not be used for training, validation, model selection, or manual tuning.
- **No Ground-Truth Probing**: Participants must not attempt to infer, reconstruct, or extract hidden ground-truth semantic segmentation labels from the evaluation server.
- **No Manual Annotation**: Submitted predictions must be generated by an automated method. Manually created, manually corrected, or manually tuned predictions are not allowed.
- **No Evaluation Server Exploitation**: Any attempt to exploit the evaluation system, submission feedback, or leaderboard behavior to gain an unfair advantage is strictly prohibited.
- **External Resources Disclosure**: Any external datasets, pretrained models, synthetic data, or additional training resources used by participants must be disclosed in the final technical report if requested.
- **Verification Requirement**: Top-ranked teams may be required to submit a short technical report, code, logs, or additional information for result verification.

## 📩 Contact

For questions about the challenge, please contact the organizers through the official competition page or by email.
pages.content_zh:
# ECCV 2026事件驱动多模态感知挑战赛：语义分割赛道

欢迎参加**事件驱动多模态感知挑战赛**的**语义分割赛道**，官方主办单位为**ECCV 2026**！

## 🎯 概述与目标

本挑战赛旨在推进使用**事件相机视觉和帧数据**的**语义分割**研究。与传统RGB相机不同，事件相机以异步方式捕捉亮度变化，具有高时间分辨率、低延迟和高动态范围的特点，特别适用于高速运动、低光环境和强光照变化等挑战性场景。

本赛道的目标是对能够有效利用**事件流和强度帧**互补信息的鲁棒语义分割方法进行基准测试和推广。参赛者需要开发能够从多模态输入预测准确的像素级语义标签的模型，特别关注在挑战性视觉条件下的泛化能力、鲁棒性和场景理解能力。

## 📝 任务描述

参赛者需要为测试集提交预测的语义分割图。每个预测应遵循官方提交格式，并对应提供的事件和帧输入数据。

该任务重点关注：

- **语义分割**：从事件和帧数据预测像素级语义标签。
- **多模态融合**：有效融合异步事件流和图像帧以进行语义感知。
- **像素级场景理解**：将每个有效像素分配到预定义的语义类别。
- **鲁棒感知**：处理低光照、高动态范围等挑战性视觉条件。

## 📅 重要日期

除特别说明外，所有截止时间均为**世界协调时间（UTC）下午11时59分**。

- **2026年5月13日**：挑战赛网站开放。
- **2026年5月15日**：训练集发布。
- **2026年6月20日**：测试集与评估服务器上线。
- **2026年6月30日**：挑战赛提交截止日期。
- **2026年7月5日**：挑战赛成绩公布。
- **2026年7月10日**：技术报告截止日期。

## 📊 数据集

本赛道使用**[CoSEC数据集](https://cosec.org.cn/)**和**[REAL数据集](https://github.com/jinghan-xu/NEC-Diff)**的子集。该数据集提供多模态传感器数据，包括图像帧、事件流和语义分割标注。

本赛道发布的数据包括：

- **图像帧**：由单目相机捕捉的RGB帧。
- **事件流**：由事件相机记录的异步事件数据。
- **语义分割标签**：为监督训练和验证提供的地面真值像素级语义标签。

训练集用于模型开发和性能验证。测试集用于官方评估，其地面真值语义分割标签将保密。

本赛道的数据集统计信息汇总如下：

| 数据集划分 | 样本数量 | 
|---|---:|
| 训练集 | 2569 |
| 测试集 | 待发布 | 

## ⚠️ 挑战赛规则

为维护公平性和科学完整性，所有参赛者必须严格遵守挑战赛规则：

- **禁止使用测试集进行训练**：不得将测试集用于训练、验证、模型选择或人工调参。
- **禁止探测地面真值**：参赛者不得尝试从评估服务器推断、重建或提取隐藏的地面真值语义分割标签。
- **禁止手工标注**：提交的预测必须由自动化方法生成。不允许手工创建、手工纠正或手工调参的预测。
- **禁止利用评估服务器**：严格禁止任何试图利用评估系统、提交反馈或排行榜行为获得不公平优势的行为。
- **外部资源披露**：任何外部数据集、预训练模型、合成数据或额外资源

pages.title: Data Preparation
pages.content:
# Data Preparation Guide

To participate in the **Event-Based Multimodal Perception Challenge: Semantic Segmentation Track**, participants need to prepare the training data, understand the data format, and generate semantic segmentation predictions for the test set once it is released.

This track uses a curated subset of the **[CoSEC dataset](https://cosec.org.cn/)** for event-based semantic segmentation. Each sequence contains multimodal data, including image frames, event streams, timestamps, and semantic segmentation annotations for training.

## Training Set Download

The training set contains image frames, event streams, timestamps, and ground-truth semantic segmentation labels. Participants may use the training set to develop, train, and validate their semantic segmentation models.

The training split includes:

- Image frames from the left camera.
- Event streams from the left event camera.
- Timestamps for samples.
- Ground-truth semantic segmentation labels.

Please download the official training set from the following links:

👉 **Download CoSEC Segmentation Training Set**

- **Baidu Netdisk**: [train.zip](https://pan.baidu.com/s/1TeXpiWIRDxGcbjubGB1bCA?pwd=HUST)

- **Google Drive**: [train.zip](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1V29ahlLQTm2O__I-VI_qloR9JkzGYmex/view?usp=drive_link)

## Test Set Download

The official test set has **not been released yet**.

The test set will be released according to the challenge schedule. Once released, the download links will be updated on this page.

The test split will contain image frames, event streams, and timestamps. The ground-truth semantic segmentation labels for the test set will remain hidden and will be used only by the Codabench evaluation server.

Please download the official test set from the following links once it is released:

👉 **Download CoSEC Semantic Segmentation Test Set**

- **Baidu Netdisk**: To be released

- **Google Drive**: To be released

## Dataset Structure and Format

The expected dataset structure is:

```text
segmentation_challenge
├── train
│   ├── Day_Campus_000
│   │   ├── img_co_left
│   │   ├── segment_co
│   │   ├── events_co_left.h5
│   │   └── timestamps.txt
│   ├── Day_Campus_001
│   │   ├── img_co_left
│   │   ├── segment_co
│   │   ├── events_co_left.h5
│   │   └── timestamps.txt
│   └── ...
└── test
    ├── Day_Campus_012
    │   ├── img_co_left
    │   ├── events_co_left.h5
    │   └── timestamps.txt
    ├── Day_Campus_013
    │   ├── img_co_left
    │   ├── events_co_left.h5
    │   └── timestamps.txt
    └── ...
```

Each sample is identified by its sequence name and frame file name. The image frame and semantic segmentation label with the same file name correspond to the same sample.

For a training sample:

```text
segmentation/train/Day_Campus_000/img_co_left/000000.png
segmentation/train/Day_Campus_000/segment_co/000000.png
```

The corresponding event data is stored in the sequence-level HDF5 file:

```text
segmentation/train/Day_Campus_000/events_co_left.h5
```

The timestamps are stored as:

```text
segmentation/train/Day_Campus_000/timestamps.txt
```

For a test sample after the test set is released:

```text
segmentation/test/Day_Campus_012/img_co_left/000000.png
```

the expected submitted prediction should be:

```text
Day_Campus_012/segment_co/000000.
```

### Image Frame Format

Image frames are stored as `.png` files under the `img_co_left` folder, with a resolution of `1200 x 624`. They have been aligned with the event streams and can be used together with the event data for multimodal semantic segmentation.

### Event Stream Format

Each sequence contains one `events_co_left.h5` file. The event stream has already been aligned with the image frames.

A typical event file contains the following fields:

```text
x          # event x coordinates
y          # event y coordinates
p          # event polarities
t          # event timestamps
ms_to_idx  # mapping from millisecond timestamp to event index
```

The event timestamps `t` are stored in microseconds. The `ms_to_idx` array provides a convenient way to quickly locate event indices by millisecond-level timestamps.

For a given image frame, participants may use the corresponding timestamp in `timestamps.txt` to extract a temporal window of events. For example, one can take the events within `50 ms` before the image exposure end timestamp and use them together with the image frame for multimodal semantic segmentation.

Example code:

```python
import os
import cv2
import h5py
import numpy as np

seq_dir = "path/to/sequence"

frame_id = 0
event_window_ms = 50

# Load image timestamp.
timestamps = np.loadtxt(os.path.join(seq_dir, "timestamps.txt"), dtype=np.int64)
img_ts_us = timestamps[frame_id]  # exposure end timestamp of this image, in microseconds

# Load image frame.
img_path = os.path.join(seq_dir, "img_co_left", f"{frame_id:06d}.png")
img = cv2.imread(img_path, cv2.IMREAD_UNCHANGED)

# Load event file.
event_path = os.path.join(seq_dir, "events_co_left.h5")
with h5py.File(event_path, "r") as events:
    ms_to_idx = events["ms_to_idx"][:]

    # Select events within [img_ts_us - 50 ms, img_ts_us].
    start_us = img_ts_us - event_window_ms * 1000
    end_us = img_ts_us

    start_ms = int(round(start_us / 1000.0))
    end_ms = int(round(end_us / 1000.0))

    start_idx = ms_to_idx[start_ms]
    end_idx = ms_to_idx[end_ms]

    xs = events["x"][start_idx:end_idx]
    ys = events["y"][start_idx:end_idx]
    ps = events["p"][start_idx:end_idx]
    ts = events["t"][start_idx:end_idx]

# img and the selected event segment can then be used together.
```

### Timestamp Format

Each sequence provides a `timestamps.txt` file. Each line stores the timestamp of one image frame, and the order is consistent with the frame order in `img_co_left`.

The timestamp can be regarded as the exposure end timestamp of the corresponding image frame.

For example:

```text
img_co_left/000000.png  ->  first line in timestamps.txt
img_co_left/000001.png  ->  second line in timestamps.txt
img_co_left/000002.png  ->  third line in timestamps.txt
```

For the training set, the semantic segmentation labels share the same timestamps as the corresponding image frames.

The timestamps are stored as integer values in microseconds. They can be used to associate each image/label sample with a corresponding segment of the event stream.

### Semantic Segmentation Label Format

For the semantic segmentation task, each label is stored as a **single-channel PNG mask**. Each pixel value in the mask represents the semantic class ID of the corresponding pixel.

The valid class IDs range from `0` to `18`. Pixels with values outside this range are considered invalid during evaluation. The mask should be saved with an integer pixel data type. We recommend saving the mask as an **8-bit unsigned integer (`uint8`) PNG**, where each pixel stores the corresponding class ID directly.

| Class ID | Class Name |
| ---: | --- |
| 0 | road |
| 1 | sidewalk |
| 2 | building |
| 3 | wall |
| 4 | fence |
| 5 | pole |
| 6 | traffic light |
| 7 | traffic sign |
| 8 | vegetation |
| 9 | terrain |
| 10 | sky |
| 11 | person |
| 12 | rider |
| 13 | car |
| 14 | truck |
| 15 | bus |
| 16 | train |
| 17 | motorcycle |
| 18 | bicycle |

Please note that the PNG mask should store the **class IDs as raw pixel values**, not visualization colors. For example, a pixel value of `13` represents the class `car`, and a pixel value of `0` represents the class `road`.

# 数据准备指南

要参加**基于事件的多模态感知挑战赛：语义分割赛道**，参赛者需要准备训练数据、理解数据格式，并在测试集发布后为其生成语义分割预测。

本赛道使用精选的**[CoSEC数据集](https://cosec.org.cn/)**子集进行基于事件的语义分割。每个序列包含多模态数据，包括图像帧、事件流、时间戳和用于训练的语义分割标注。

## 训练集下载

训练集包含图像帧、事件流、时间戳和真值语义分割标签。参赛者可以使用训练集开发、训练和验证其语义分割模型。

训练集划分包括：

- 左摄像机的图像帧。
- 左事件摄像机的事件流。

官方测试集**尚未发布**。

测试集将根据挑战赛计划发布。发布后，下载链接将在本页面更新。

## 数据集结构和格式

预期的数据集结构为：

```text
segmentation_challenge
├── train
│   ├── Day_Campus_000
│   │   ├── img_co_left
│   │   ├── segment_co
│   │   ├── events_co_left.h5
│   │   └── timestamps.txt
│   ├── Day_Campus_001
│   │   ├── img_co_left
│   │   ├── segment_co
│   │   ├── events_co_left.h5
│   │   └── timestamps.txt
│   └── ...
└── test
    ├── Day_Campus_012
    │   ├── img_co_left
    │   ├── events_co_left.h5
    │   └── timestamps.txt
    ├── Day_Campus_013
    │   ├── img_co_left
    │   ├── events_co_left.h5
    │   └── timestamps.txt
    └── ...
```

每个样本由其序列名称和帧文件名标识。具有相同文件名的图像帧和语义分割标签对应同一样本。

对于训练样本：

```text
segmentation/train/Day_Campus_000/img_co_left/000000.png
segmentation/train/Day_Campus_000/segment_co/000000.png
```

对应的事件数据存储在序列级HDF5文件中：

```text
segmentation/train/Day_Campus_000/events_co_left.h5
```

时间戳存储为：

```text
segmentation/train/Day_Campus_000/timestamps.txt
```

对于测试集发布后的测试样本：

```text
segmentation/test/Day_Campus_012/img_co_left/000000.png
```

预期的提交预测应为：

```text
Day_Campus_012/segment_co/000000.
```

### 图像帧格式

图像帧以`.png`文件形式存储在`img_co_left`文件夹下，分辨率为`1200 x 624`。它们已与事件流对齐，可与事件数据一起用于多模态语义分割。

### 事件流格式

每个序列包含一个`events_co_left.h5`文件。事件流已与图像帧对齐。

典型的事件文件包含以下字段：

```text
x          # 事件x坐标
y          # 事件y坐标
p          # 事件极性
t          # 事件时间戳
ms_to_idx  # 毫秒时间戳到事件索引的映射
```

pages.title: Evaluation Details
pages.content:
# Evaluation Metrics & Submission Guidelines

## Submission Format

Participants are required to submit semantic segmentation predictions for all samples in the test set. Each prediction should be a pixel-level semantic label map corresponding to one test image frame.

The submission must be a single `.zip` archive. The archive should contain all predicted semantic segmentation masks while preserving the required sequence-level directory structure.

Each prediction file must satisfy the following requirements:

- The file extension must be `.png`.
- Each prediction file must contain a semantic segmentation label map.
- The predicted label map must be a **single-channel PNG mask**.
- The predicted label map must be saved as integer pixel values. We recommend using `uint8`.
- The shape of the predicted label map must match the corresponding ground-truth semantic segmentation label.
- The file name must be identical to the corresponding test sample name.
- The predicted label values must follow the official semantic class definitions.
- Valid label values are integers from `0` to `18`.
- Invalid label values outside the predefined class set will be treated as incorrect predictions.

## Semantic Label Format

The submitted semantic segmentation map should store pixel-level semantic class IDs directly.

Each pixel value represents its predicted semantic category according to the official class definition. For example, a pixel value of `13` represents the class `car`, while a pixel value of `0` represents the class `road`.

The prediction mask should store the **class IDs as raw pixel values**, not visualization colors. Participants should not submit colorized RGB visualization results as prediction masks.

The valid semantic classes are:

| Class ID | Class Name |
| ---: | --- |
| 0 | road |
| 1 | sidewalk |
| 2 | building |
| 3 | wall |
| 4 | fence |
| 5 | pole |
| 6 | traffic light |
| 7 | traffic sign |
| 8 | vegetation |
| 9 | terrain |
| 10 | sky |
| 11 | person |
| 12 | rider |
| 13 | car |
| 14 | truck |
| 15 | bus |
| 16 | train |
| 17 | motorcycle |
| 18 | bicycle |

## Submission Structure

An example submission structure is shown below:

```text
submission.zip
├── Day_Campus_000/
│   └── segment_co/
│       ├── 000000.png
│       ├── 000001.png
│       ├── 000002.png
│       └── ...
├── Day_Campus_001/
│   └── segment_co/
│       ├── 000000.png
│       ├── 000001.png
│       └── ...
└── ...
```

A dummy submission file is provided to help participants verify the required file structure and submission format.

👉 **Download Dummy Submission**

- **Baidu Netdisk**: [dummy_submission.zip](https://pan.baidu.com/s/1Q8BdgnOfex4XX3JUFgS-JA?pwd=HUST)
- **Google Drive**: [dummy_submission.zip](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1-KTZAXIKoWlOQsBBnyNZHebq_WJ-Juqe/view?usp=drive_link)

Participants may use the dummy submission as a reference when preparing their own prediction files.

For each test image frame, for example:

```text
test/Day_Campus_000/img_co_left/000000.png
```

the submitted prediction should be:

```text
Day_Campus_000/segment_co/000000.png
```

Please make sure that the submitted `.zip` file directly contains the sequence folders. Do not include unnecessary parent directories.

## How to Submit

1. **Prepare your predictions**: Generate semantic segmentation predictions for all test samples.
2. **Convert label format**: Convert model outputs to the required semantic label format according to the official class ID mapping.
3. **Save predictions**: Save each prediction as a single-channel `.png` mask with integer class IDs from `0` to `18`.
4. **Check your file structure**: Make sure the directory structure matches the required format. Each test sample should have exactly one corresponding prediction file.
5. **Compress your results**: Zip the sequence folders into a single `.zip` archive.
6. **Upload**: Navigate to the **My Submissions** tab on the challenge page, select the active phase, and upload your `.zip` file.
7. **Evaluation**: The evaluation server will automatically process your submission and update the leaderboard.

## Metrics

The challenge evaluates semantic segmentation performance on the test set. The evaluation server compares the submitted semantic segmentation predictions with the ground-truth semantic segmentation labels using standard semantic segmentation metrics.

The following metrics are reported on the leaderboard:

- **mIoU**: Mean Intersection over Union.
- **mAcc**: Mean Class Accuracy.
- **aAcc**: Overall Pixel Accuracy.

The main leaderboard ranking metric is **mIoU**, where higher is better.

## Final Scoring

The final leaderboard ranking is determined by **mIoU** on the test set.

Higher **mIoU** indicates better semantic segmentation performance. Other metrics, including **mAcc** and **aAcc**, are also reported for comprehensive comparison, but they are not used as the primary ranking criterion.
pages.content_zh:
# 评估指标与提交指南

## 提交格式

参赛者需要为测试集中的所有样本提交语义分割预测结果。每个预测应为对应一帧测试图像的像素级语义标签图。

提交物必须为单个 `.zip` 压缩文件。该压缩文件应包含所有预测的语义分割掩码，同时保留所需的序列级目录结构。

每个预测文件必须满足以下要求：

- 文件扩展名必须为 `.png`。
- 每个预测文件必须包含一个语义分割标签图。
- 预测标签图必须为**单通道PNG掩码**。
- 预测标签图必须保存为整数像素值。我们建议使用 `uint8`。
- 预测标签图的形状必须与对应的真实值语义分割标签相匹配。
- 文件名必须与对应测试样本名称相同。
- 预测标签值必须遵循官方语义类定义。
- 有效标签值为 `0` 到 `18` 的整数。
- 预定义类集之外的无效标签值将被视为错误预测。

## 语义标签格式

提交的语义分割图应直接存储像素级语义类ID。

每个像素值根据官方类定义表示其预测的语义类别。例如，像素值 `13` 表示类别 `car`，而像素值 `0` 表示类别 `road`。

预测掩码应存储**类ID作为原始像素值**，而不是可视化颜色。参赛者不应提交彩色RGB可视化结果作为预测掩码。

有效的语义类别为：

| 类ID | 类名 |
| ---: | --- |
| 0 | 道路 |
| 1 | 人行道 |
| 2 | 建筑 |
| 3 | 墙壁 |
| 4 | 栅栏 |
| 5 | 电杆 |
| 6 | 交通灯 |
| 7 | 交通标志 |
| 8 | 植被 |
| 9 | 地形 |
| 10 | 天空 |
| 11 | 行人 |
| 12 | 骑手 |
| 13 | 汽车 |
| 14 | 卡车 |
| 15 | 公交车 |
| 16 | 火车 |
| 17 | 摩托车 |
| 18 | 自行车 |

## 提交结构

下面展示了提交结构示例：

```text
submission.zip
├── Day_Campus_000/
│   └── segment_co/
│       ├── 000000.png
│       ├── 000001.png
│       ├── 000002.png
│       └── ...
├── Day_Campus_001/
│   └── segment_co/
│       ├── 000000.png
│       ├── 000001.png
│       └── ...
└── ...
```

提供了一个虚拟提交文件，帮助参赛者验证所需的文件结构和提交格式。

👉 **下载虚拟提交文件**

- **百度网盘**: [dummy_submission.zip](https://pan.baidu.com/s/1Q8BdgnOfex4XX3JUFgS-JA?pwd=HUST)
- **Google Drive**: [dummy_submission.zip](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1-KTZAXIKoWlOQsBBnyNZHebq_WJ-Juqe/view?usp=drive_link)

参赛者可以在准备自己的预测文件时以虚拟提交作为参考。

对于每个测试图像帧，例如：

```text
test/Day_Campus_000/img_co_left/000000.png
```

提交的预测应为：

```text
Day_Campus_000/segment_co/000000.png
```

请确保提交的 `.zip` 文件直接包含序列文件夹。不要包含不必要的上级目录。

## 如何提交

1. **准备预测结果**：为所有测试样本生成语义分割预测。
2. **转换标签格式**：根据官方类ID映射将模型输出转换为所需的语义标签格式。
3. **保存预测**：将每个预测保存为单通道 `.png` 掩码，其中整数类ID范围从 `0` 到 `18`。
4. **检查文件结构**：确保目录结构与所需格式相匹配。每个测试样本应恰好有一个对应的预测文件。
5. **压缩结果**：将序列文件夹压缩为单个 `.zip` 文件。
6. **上传**：在竞赛页面导航至**我的提交**标签页，选择活跃阶段，并上传您的 `.zip` 文件。
