pages.title: Evaluation Details
pages.content:
# Evaluation Metrics & Submission Guidelines

## Submission Format
Your submission must be a single JSON file. The file must contain a **flat dictionary** (key-value pairs), where the key is the test question `id` and the value is your model's predicted `answer`. 

*(Note: Please ensure your submission is a direct JSON object `{...}` and NOT wrapped in a JSON array `[{...}]` to ensure compatibility with our scoring engine.)*

**Example of `predictions.json`:**
```json
{
  "228350c5-4a8f-4976-abf9-fe52b86bddae": "D",
  "a959e02d-7ef6-457b-9a16-9c85934ba980": "5.3198",
  "e495b370-59ba-471b-8642-5ea239380f5a": "4",
  "d42e3a21-2c72-48ef-9f33-522338dc1c44": "left",
  "97258721-3d86-4bc2-82d1-481b59e1b7cd": "spatial\ntemporal"
}
```

## How to Submit
1. **Prepare your file:** Format your model's predictions and save them strictly as `predictions.json`.
2. **Compress:** Zip the `predictions.json` file directly into a `.zip` archive (e.g., `submission.zip`). **Do not** include any parent folders in the archive.
3. **Upload:** Navigate to the **"My Submissions"** tab on the challenge page, select the active phase, and upload your `.zip` file. The evaluation server will process your results automatically.

## Metrics
The challenge evaluates models across 5 distinct tasks. The evaluation server calculates the accuracy for each category based on the following specific matching rules:

* **Detailed Understanding**: Single-choice question. **Metric:** Exact match (A, B, C, or D).
* **Action Recognition**: Single-choice question. **Metric:** Exact match (A, B, C, or D).
* **Gesture Recognition**: Single-choice question. **Metric:** Exact match (A, B, C, or D).
* **Casual Reasoning**: Single-choice question. **Metric:** Exact match (A, B, C, or D).
* **Event OCR**: Character matching. **Metric:** Exact match required. *(Note: Please use the newline character `\n` to represent line breaks, e.g., `spatial\ntemporal`)*.

## Final Scoring
The evaluation script will independently calculate the individual accuracy scores for all 5 tasks. The final leaderboard ranking is strictly determined by the **Average Score**, which is the mean of the scores across all task categories.

* **动作识别**：单选题。**指标：** 完全匹配（A、B、C 或 D）。
* **手势识别**：单选题。**指标：** 完全匹配（A、B、C 或 D）。
* **因果推理**：单选题。**指标：** 完全匹配（A、B、C 或 D）。
* **事件 OCR**：字符匹配。**指标：** 需要完全匹配。*(注意：请使用换行符 `\n` 表示换行，例如 `spatial\ntemporal`)*。

## 最终得分
评分脚本将独立计算所有 5 个任务的个别准确度分数。最终排行榜排名完全由**平均分数**决定，即所有任务类别分数的平均值。

16027.title: MICCAI STSR 2026 Task1: Semi-supervised Teeth and Root Plup Canal Segmentation and Metal Artifact Removal in CBCT Scans

pages.title: Overview
pages.content:
## MICCAI STSR 2026 Challenge Task 1: Semi-supervised Teeth and Pulp Segmentation and Metal Artifact Removal in 3D CBCT Scans Accurate segmentation of teeth and dental pulp in 3D CBCT scans is critical for precise endodontic treatment planning, digital dental modeling, and personalized clinical decision-making. However, manual delineation of complex dental structures across volumetric data is inherently labor-intensive and expertise-dependent, while metallic restorations frequently induce severe streaking artifacts that degrade image fidelity and hinder reliable segmentation. Semi-supervised learning, integrated with metal artifact removal, offers a practical pathway to alleviate the annotation bottleneck by leveraging limited labeled data alongside abundant unlabeled clinical scans, while simultaneously restoring diagnostic image quality. - **STSR 2025**: Teeth and Pulp Root Canal Segmentation in CBCT Scans and Crown and Root Registration for IOS Scans and CBCT Scans: - [Task 1](https://www.codabench.org/competitions/6468/): Teeth and pulp root canal segmentation in CBCT scans - [Task 2](https://www.codabench.org/competitions/6470/): Crown and root registration for IOS scans and CBCT scans - **STSR 2026**: Based on STSR 2025, we add a novel metal artifact removal and a multi-modal analysis tasks into STSR 2026 Challenge - [Task 1](https://www.codabench.org/competitions/16027/): Teeth and Pulp Segmentation and Metal Artifact Removal in 3D CBCT Scans - [Task 2](https://www.codabench.org/competitions/16042/): Crown and root registration for IOS scans and CBCT scans - [Task 3](https://www.codabench.org/competitions/16117/): MMDental Multi-modal Analysis In MICCAI STSR 2026 Challenge Task 1, we scale up the dataset and provide more fine-grained annotations for different teeth, including teeth and corresponding root pulp canal. The segmentation algorithm is expected to accurately segment permanent teeth (including wisdom teeth) and corresponding root pulp canal. This task has three main features: (1) Task: teeth and root pulp canal segmentation and metal artifact removal (2) Dataset: Training set: 30 labeled CBCT images, 299 unlabeled CBCT images；Validation set: 20 labeled CBCT images (3) Evaluation: segmentation accuracy (Dice, NSD, mIoU, IA) and segmentation efficiency (GPU consumption, running time) ![task1](https://nixy495.github.io/miccai2026/images/1776948850806.jpg) --- ### 📣 News - . . . ### 🚀 How to participate - **Step1: Get the qualification**
a. To avoid the misuse of our data, you may just finish a simple (very simple) task and fill out the Data Access Agreement at first to get access to the data. [Pre-Task](https://www.codabench.org/competitions/16040/)
Data and agreement site:
-[Huggingface](https://huggingface.co/datasets/Ricoooo/MICCAI-STS26-Challenge-Pre-Task)
-[Modelscope](https://modelscope.cn/datasets/lizhii/MICCAI-STS26-Challenge-Pre-Task/summary)
-[Baidu Netdisk(extracting code = bm2u)](https://pan.baidu.com/s/1U090bZnMGEJQaD3jwqaQuA)
-[Google Drive](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1lER9eIavr99g28aTO0kuxIcos_k9FBSx?usp=sharing)
Or you could use the [STS-Skill](https://github.com/ricoleehduu/sts-skill) as when you use Claude Code, like:
"Use STS-Skill https://github.com/ricoleehduu/sts-skill to help me finishing the Pre-Task." b. Fill out the registration form and upload the Agreement:
- [For domestic participants (WPS Form)](https://f.wps.cn/g/VV1UmM0O/)
- [For international participants (Google Form)](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSf4hxOwxQdkIHkSICzlD2xnlAuPWr3EvUeB7oeuRKRPUimAKQ/viewform?usp=publish-editor) - **Step2: Join the challenge and download the data** You should register an account on this website and join the challenge. We will send you the data downloading link in 2-4 working days after reviewing your email. - **Step3: Submit result to the online submission system** ### 💡 Baseline On the way. . . ### Validation submission - All teams can directly **submit the segmentation results** on codabench to get the segmentation accuracy metrics. - All teams can also **make docker submissions**. Update on May 1st: To avoid submission jams, each team can make one docker submission per 15 days. Here are the pre-defined deadlines: 5.15, 5.30, 6.15, 6.30, 7.15, 7.30 (12am EST). - To make docker submissions, please send us ([SemiTeethSegChallenge@outlook.com](mailto:SemiTeethSegChallenge@outlook.com)): (Email subject: YourTeamName-TeamLeaderName-Validation Submission) - **(1)** a download link to your Docker container (teamname.tar.gz file size < 5Gb). - **(2)** a sanity test video (example). Please test your docker on the demo cases and record the prediction process. - When the evaluation is finished, we will return the metrics via email. ### 🏆 Awards - The top two teams will receive cash prizes (900USD for 1st place, 300USD for 2nd place). - Following the conclusion of STS 2026, the first authors of the top 5 teams on the test leaderboard will be invited to contribute to a collaborative review paper summarizing the challenge outcomes (intended for submission to a top-tier medical imaging journal). - All participating teams are eligible to submit technical papers. Submissions passing the peer-review process organized by the committee will be published in the Lecture Notes in Computer Science (LNCS) series by Springer, which is indexed by EI Compendex. ### 📧 Contact For any inquiries, please feel free to reach out via email at [SemiTeethSegChallenge@outlook.com](mailto:SemiTeethSegChallenge@outlook.com) or [zhi.li@hdu.edu.cn](mailto:zhi.li@hdu.edu.cn). We welcome any questions, feedback, or requests for further information regarding the STSR 2026 Challenge. We look forward to meeting each one of you this year, face to face if possible. See you at Daejeon, Republic of Korea!
#### References: [1] Wang Y, Zhang Y, Chen X, et al. STS MICCAI 2023 Challenge: Grand challenge on 2D and 3D semi-supervised tooth segmentation[J]. arXiv preprint arXiv:2407.13246, 2024. [2] Semi-supervised Tooth Segmentation: First MICCAI Challenge, SemiToothSeg 2023, Held in Conjunction with MICCAI 2023, Vancouver, BC, Canada, October 8, 2023, Proceedings[M]. Springer Nature, 2025. [3] Wang Y, Zhang Y, Chen X, et al. MICCAI 2023 STS Challenge: A retrospective study of semi-supervised approaches for teeth segmentation[J]. Pattern Recognition, 2025: 112049. [4] Wang Y, Li Z, Wu C, et al. MICCAI STS 2024 Challenge: Semi-Supervised Instance-Level Tooth Segmentation in Panoramic X-ray and CBCT Images[J]. arXiv preprint arXiv:2511.22911, 2025.
pages.content_zh:
## MICCAI STSR 2026挑战赛任务1：3D CBCT扫描中的半监督牙齿和牙髓分割及金属伪影去除 在3D CBCT扫描中准确分割牙齿和牙髓对于精确的根管治疗计划制定、数字化牙科建模和个性化临床决策至关重要。然而，在体积数据中手动描绘复杂牙科结构本质上是劳动密集且依赖专业知识的，而金属修复体经常引起严重的条纹伪影，导致图像保真度下降，阻碍可靠的分割。半监督学习与金属伪影去除相结合，提供了一条实用途径来缓解标注瓶颈——通过利用有限的标注数据和大量未标注的临床扫描，同时恢复诊断图像质量。 - **STSR 2025**：CBCT扫描中的牙齿和牙髓根管分割以及IOS扫描和CBCT扫描的牙冠和牙根配准： - [任务1](https://www.codabench.org/competitions/6468/)：CBCT扫描中的牙齿和牙髓根管分割 - [任务2](https://www.codabench.org/competitions/6470/)：IOS扫描和CBCT扫描的牙冠和牙根配准 - **STSR 2026**：基于STSR 2025，我们在STSR 2026挑战赛中增加了新的金属伪影去除和多模态分析任务 - [任务1](https://www.codabench.org/competitions/16027/)：3D CBCT扫描中的牙齿和牙髓分割及金属伪影去除 - [任务2](https://www.codabench.org/competitions/16042/)：IOS扫描和CBCT扫描的牙冠和牙根配准 - [任务3](https://www.codabench.org/competitions/16117/)：MMDental多模态分析 在MICCAI STSR 2026挑战赛任务1中，我们扩展了数据集，为不同的牙齿提供了更精细的标注，包括牙齿和相应的牙根髓腔。分割算法应准确分割永久齿（包括智齿）和相应的牙根髓腔。该任务具有三个主要特点： （1）任务：牙齿和牙根髓腔分割及金属伪影去除 （2）数据集：训练集：30张标注CBCT图像，299张未标注CBCT图像；验证集：20张标注CBCT图像 （3）评估：分割精度（Dice、NSD、mIoU、IA）和分割效率（GPU消耗、运行时间） ![task1](https://nixy495.github.io/miccai2026/images/1776948850806.jpg) --- ### 📣 新闻 - . . . ### 🚀 参赛方式 - **步骤1：获得资格认证**
a. 为了避免滥用我们的数据，您首先需要完成一个简单（非常简单）的任务并填写数据访问协议以获得数据访问权限。[预备任务](https://www.codabench.org/competitions/16040/)
数据和协议网址：
-[Huggingface](https://huggingface.co/datasets/Ricoooo/MICCAI-STS26-Challenge-Pre-Task)
-[Modelscope](https://modelscope.cn/datasets/lizhii/MICCAI-STS26-Challenge-Pre-Task/summary)
-[百度网盘(提取码 = bm2u)](https://pan.baidu.com/s/1U090bZnMGEJQaD3jwqaQuA)
-[Google Drive](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1lER9eIavr99g28aTO0kuxIcos_k9FBSx?usp=sharing)
或者您可以使用[STS-Skill](https://github.com/ricoleehduu/sts-skill)，就像使用Claude Code一样：
"使用STS-Skill https://github.com/ricoleehduu/sts-skill帮助我完成预备任务。" b. 填写注册表并上传协议：
- [国内参赛者(WPS表单)](https://f.wps.cn/g/VV1UmM0O/)
- [国际参赛者(Google表单)](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSf4hxOwxQdkIHkSICzlD2xnlAuPWr3EvUeB7oeuRKRPUimAKQ/viewform?usp=publish-editor) - **步骤2：加入挑战赛并下载数据** 您应在本网站注册账户并加入挑战赛。我们将在审核您的邮件后的2-4个工作日内向您发送数据下载链接。 - **步骤3：向在线提交系统提交结果**

pages.title: Dataset
pages.content:
## 🗂️ Dataset

The data download link will be sent to approved teams via email. Please make sure that you can download large files from Google Drive or Baidu Netdisk and have enough space and computing resources to process them.

- **Training set:** 30 labeled CBCT images, 299 unlabeled CBCT images
- **Validation set:** 20 labeled CBCT images

Folder structure (download link will be sent to registered qualified participants):

- **Train-Labeled:** Tooth images with segmentation masks for teeth, wisdom teeth, and multiple types of root canal structures.
- **Train-Unlabel:** only images are provided and the corresponding annotation files are not available
- **Validation-Public:** The labels of hidden validation set will not be released. Please submit the segmentation results on codabench to get the metrics.

**Label Information:** The dataset contains precise segmentation of 65 classes (including background) with the following label mapping:

- Label_0: Background
- Label_1: Upper Right Central Incisor
- Label_2: Upper Right Lateral Incisor
- Label_3: Upper Right Canine
- Label_4: Upper Right First Premolar
- . . .
- Label_61: Lower Right Second Premolar Pulp
- Label_62: Lower Right First Molar Pulp
- Label_63: Lower Right Second Molar Pulp
- Label_64: Lower Right Third Molar (Wisdom Tooth) Pulp

Note:

- Competitors should submit validation set results in strict accordance with the format required in the Submission Guidelines.
- This data is only used for scientific research purposes, secondary distribution of data and other commercial purposes are not allowed. The organizer of this challenge reserves the right to pursue the legal responsibility of those who violate the relevant regulations.
pages.content_zh:
## 🗂️ 数据集

数据下载链接将通过电子邮件发送给获批的参赛队伍。请确保您能够从谷歌云盘或百度网盘下载大文件，并具有足够的存储空间和计算资源来处理这些文件。

- **训练集：** 30张标注的CBCT图像，299张未标注的CBCT图像
- **验证集：** 20张标注的CBCT图像

文件夹结构（下载链接将发送给注册合格的参赛者）：

- **Train-Labeled：** 包含牙齿分割掩码、智齿和多种根管结构的牙齿图像。
- **Train-Unlabel：** 仅提供图像，对应的标注文件不可用
- **Validation-Public：** 隐藏验证集的标签将不予发布。请将分割结果提交至codabench平台以获取评估指标。

**标签信息：** 数据集包含65个类别（包括背景）的精确分割，标签映射如下：

- Label_0：背景
- Label_1：上右中切牙
- Label_2：上右侧切牙
- Label_3：上右犬牙
- Label_4：上右第一前磨牙
- . . .
- Label_61：下右第二前磨牙牙髓
- Label_62：下右第一磨牙牙髓
- Label_63：下右第二磨牙牙髓
- Label_64：下右第三磨牙（智齿）牙髓

注意事项：

- 参赛者应按照《提交指南》中要求的格式严格提交验证集结果。
- 该数据仅用于科学研究目的，不允许二次分发数据及其他商业用途。本竞赛主办方保留追究违反相关规定者法律责任的权利。

pages.title: Evaluation
pages.content:
## 👑 Evaluation Metrics

In STSR 2026, the challenge evaluation criteria are not limited to segmentation accuracy, but also include runtime and GPU memory consumption, providing a comprehensive assessment of segmentation algorithms.

**Segmentation accuracy metrics:**

- Dice Similarity Coefficient (DSC): instance-level and image-level
- Normalized Surface Distance (NSD): instance-level and image-level
- mean Intersection-over-Union (mIoU): instance-level and image-level
- Identification Accuracy (IA)

**Segmentation efficiency metrics:**

- Running time: We give running time a tolerance of 45s (including the docker starting time). Moreover, the inference time for each case should be within 60s. Otherwise, the corresponding case will be regarded as a failed case.
- GPU memory consumption: area under GPU memory-time curve

**❗NOTICE❗**
Since the GPU consumption and running time of the algorithm cannot be verified in the validation stage, the player needs to take into account both the performance of the algorithm and the time and space complexity in beginning of algorithm design.

🔔 We have updated and released the evaluation code and submission instructions. If you encounter a bug or have questions about the evaluation metrics, you can check out our Github link: [https://github.com/ricoleehduu/STSR-Challenge/tree/main](https://github.com/ricoleehduu/STSR-Challenge/tree/main).

---

## 🛣️ Evaluation Platform for Docker

The submitted docker containers will be evaluated on a Ubuntu 20.04 desktop. Detailed information is listed as follows:

- CPU: Intel(R) Xeon(R) Gold 6248R CPU @ 3.00GHz × 12
- GPU: GeForce RTX 3090 (24G)
- RAM: 28G
- Driver Version: 550.78
- CUDA Version: 11.3
- Docker version 26.1.1

---

## 🔝 Ranking Scheme

Following the recommendation in ChallengeR [1], the ranking scheme includes the following steps:

**Step 1.** In order to maintain consistency with our previous challenge and assess the algorithm's performance at the image level, our evaluation framework will first initially compute two metrics, Dice and NSD, on a per-image basis to derive the algorithm's Dice (image_Dice) and NSD (image_NSD) scores at image-level.

**Step 2.** Next, given the challenge's focus on instance-level analysis, we also will employ a greedy algorithm to match the corresponding prediction results with the ground truth, allowing for the calculation of Dice (instance_Dice), NSD (instance_NSD), and IA (instance_IA) scores at the instance level.

**Step 3.** Then, the evaluation will include measurements of running time (RT) and GPU consumption (GPU_area) for each algorithm.

**Step 4.** Take the mean of the eight metrics over the test cases, and rank separately among the teams.

**Step 5.** A final overall rank is given by taking the average of the four ranks. In the case of equal average rankings for two teams, they are considered tied.

[1] Wiesenfarth, M., Reinke, A., Landman, B.A. et al. Methods and open-source toolkit for analyzing and visualizing challenge results. Sci Rep 11, 2369 (2021).
pages.content_zh:
## 👑 评估指标

在STSR 2026中，挑战的评估标准不仅限于分割准确性，还包括运行时间和GPU内存消耗，为分割算法提供全面评估。

**分割准确性指标：**

- 骰子相似系数（DSC）：实例级和图像级
- 归一化表面距离（NSD）：实例级和图像级
- 平均交并比（mIoU）：实例级和图像级
- 识别准确率（IA）

**分割效率指标：**

- 运行时间：容限为45秒（包含Docker启动时间）。此外，每个病例的推理时间应在60秒内。否则，相应病例将被视为失败病例。
- GPU内存消耗：GPU内存-时间曲线下面积

**❗注意❗**
由于算法的GPU消耗和运行时间无法在验证阶段验证，参赛者需要在算法设计初期同时考虑算法性能以及时间和空间复杂度。

🔔 我们已更新并发布了评估代码和提交说明。如果您遇到Bug或对评估指标有疑问，可以查看我们的Github链接：[https://github.com/ricoleehduu/STSR-Challenge/tree/main](https://github.com/ricoleehduu/STSR-Challenge/tree/main)。

---

## 🛣️ Docker评估平台

提交的Docker容器将在Ubuntu 20.04桌面上进行评估。详细信息如下：

- CPU：Intel(R) Xeon(R) Gold 6248R CPU @ 3.00GHz × 12核
- GPU：GeForce RTX 3090（24G）
- RAM：28G
- 驱动程序版本：550.78
- CUDA版本：11.3
- Docker版本：26.1.1

---

## 🔝 排名方案

按照ChallengeR [1]的建议，排名方案包括以下步骤：

**第1步：** 为了与我们之前的挑战保持一致并在图像级别评估算法性能，评估框架将首先在逐图像的基础上计算骰子和NSD两个指标，以得出算法在图像级别的骰子（image_Dice）和NSD（image_NSD）评分。

**第2步：** 接下来，考虑到挑战对实例级分析的重点，我们将采用贪心算法将相应的预测结果与真实标注进行匹配，从而计算实例级的骰子（instance_Dice）、NSD（instance_NSD）和IA（instance_IA）评分。

**第3步：** 随后，评估将包括每个算法的运行时间（RT）和GPU消耗（GPU_area）的测量。

**第4步：** 计算八个指标在测试病例上的均值，并在各队之间分别排名。

**第5步：** 通过对四个排名的平均值进行平均计算，给出最终综合排名。在两个队伍的平均排名相等的情况下，它们被视为并列。

[1] Wiesenfarth, M., Reinke, A., Landman, B.A. et al. Methods and open-source toolkit for analyzing and visualizing challenge results. Sci Rep 11, 2369 (2021).

pages.title: Submission Instructions
pages.content:
## 🚩 Instructions

For 3D CBCT Semi-supervised Segmentation task, there are some rules to be followed when submitting prediction results for validation set.

- To get a score for the validation set, competitors should submit a .zip file to the system, which contains 20 nii.gz files, each corresponding to the instance segmentation prediction result of a CBCT image in the validation set.
- All nii.gz files for the predicted results must be named in the following format:  
  **Validation_xxxx_Mask.nii.gz**, where **xxxx** stands for the id of the validation image. The id between submitted predicted nii.gz file and original validation image must be consistent.

**❗NOTICE❗**  
The output prediction nii.gz files must be directly packaged into a zip file, no subfolder should be included.
pages.content_zh:
## 🚩 说明

对于3D CBCT半监督分割任务，提交验证集预测结果进行评分时需要遵循以下规则：

- 为了获得验证集的分数，竞赛者应向系统提交一个.zip文件，其中包含20个nii.gz文件，每个文件对应验证集中一个CBCT图像的实例分割预测结果。
- 所有预测结果的nii.gz文件必须按照以下格式命名：  
  **Validation_xxxx_Mask.nii.gz**，其中**xxxx**代表验证图像的ID。提交的预测nii.gz文件与原始验证图像之间的ID必须一致。

**❗注意❗**  
输出的预测nii.gz文件必须直接打包到zip文件中，不应包含任何子文件夹。

pages.title: Participation
pages.content:
# How to participate

ArGuard 2026 — Task 1 / Track A · **Subtask A1: Binary Hateful Meme Detection**.

## Who can join
You can take part as an individual or as a team. A person can participate in multiple shared tasks within ArGuard 2026 — see the full list at the [task website](https://araieval.gitlab.io/ArGuard2026/).

## How to register
1. Sign up on this CodaBench competition page.
2. Read the [Terms](#) tab — pay particular attention to the submission file format and naming convention.
3. Download the data from the Hugging Face Hub: [`QCRI/ArGuard-Task1`](https://huggingface.co/datasets/QCRI/ArGuard-Task1).
4. Clone the starter kit: [`araieval/ArGuard-2026-tasks`](https://github.com/araieval/ArGuard-2026-tasks).
5. Train your model and generate predictions on the `dev_test` split during the development phase, and on the `test` split during the final-evaluation phase.

## Submitting
- One team member submits per phase via the **Submit** tab.
- Upload your TSV prediction file as `prediction.zip` (see Terms).
- The latest valid submission before the deadline counts as your team's final submission for that phase.

## System description paper
All teams are encouraged to submit a system-description paper for the workshop. A single paper may cover multiple subtasks if you participated in more than one.

## Contact
Questions: arguard2026-organizers@googlegroups.com
pages.content_zh:
# 参加方式

ArGuard 2026 — 任务 1 / 赛道 A · **子任务 A1：二元仇恨梗图检测**。

## 谁可以参加
您可以以个人或团队身份参加。一个人可以参加 ArGuard 2026 内的多个共享任务——查看[任务网站](https://araieval.gitlab.io/ArGuard2026/)上的完整列表。

## 如何注册
1. 在此 CodaBench 竞赛页面上注册。
2. 阅读[条款](#)选项卡——特别注意提交文件格式和命名约定。
3. 从 Hugging Face Hub 下载数据：[`QCRI/ArGuard-Task1`](https://huggingface.co/datasets/QCRI/ArGuard-Task1)。
4. 克隆启动工具包：[`araieval/ArGuard-2026-tasks`](https://github.com/araieval/ArGuard-2026-tasks)。
5. 在开发阶段在 `dev_test` 拆分上训练您的模型并生成预测，在最终评估阶段在 `test` 拆分上生成预测。

## 提交
- 每个阶段由一名团队成员通过**提交**选项卡提交。
- 将您的 TSV 预测文件上传为 `prediction.zip`（见条款）。
- 截止日期前的最新有效提交将计为您团队在该阶段的最终提交。

## 系统描述论文
所有团队都被鼓励为研讨会提交系统描述论文。如果您参加了多个子任务，单篇论文可以涵盖多个子任务。

## 联系方式
问题咨询：arguard2026-organizers@googlegroups.com

pages.title: Dataset
pages.content:
# Dataset

ArGuard Task 1 — Track A is built around an Arabic multimodal meme dataset: each instance is an image with overlaid Arabic text (OCR-extracted). Memes are manually annotated for hatefulness.

For **Subtask A1**, every meme has a single binary label from {`Hateful`, `Not Hateful`}.

## Get the data
The official splits live on the Hugging Face Hub at [`QCRI/ArGuard-Task1`](https://huggingface.co/datasets/QCRI/ArGuard-Task1). Download them with the helper script from the starter kit:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/araieval/ArGuard-2026-tasks.git
cd ArGuard-2026-tasks/task1
pip install -r requirements.txt
python data/download_data.py
```

## Splits

| Split        | Records | Labels   | Phase                    | Purpose                                    |
|--------------|--------:|----------|--------------------------|--------------------------------------------|
| `train`      |   3,500 | full     | development              | model training                             |
| `dev`        |     500 | full     | development              | hyperparameter selection, local evaluation |
| `dev_test`   |     500 | **none** | development (leaderboard)| submission target for the dev-phase leaderboard |
| `test`       |     500 | full     | final evaluation         | blind ranking (triple-annotated gold)      |

The `dev_test` split is released with labels stripped — that is the file you submit predictions for during the development phase.

## Record schema (after `download_data.py`)
```json
{
    "id": "f9a8…b1.jpg",
    "image_path": "img/f9a8…b1.jpg",
    "text": "OCR-extracted Arabic text…",
    "label": "Hateful" | "Not Hateful" | null,
    "fine_grained_label": ["Mocking", "Incitement"],
    "annotations": []
}
```

## Licence
The dataset is released under **CC BY-NC 4.0** (non-commercial research use only).

## Resources
- Starter kit: <https://github.com/araieval/ArGuard-2026-tasks>
- Task website: <https://araieval.gitlab.io/ArGuard2026/>
pages.content_zh:
# 数据集

ArGuard Task 1 — Track A 围绕阿拉伯语多模态表情包数据集构建：每个实例包含一张图像和叠加的阿拉伯文本（OCR 提取）。表情包经人工标注仇恨属性。

对于**子任务 A1**，每个表情包都有一个二元标签：{`Hateful`（仇恨）、`Not Hateful`（非仇恨）}。

## 获取数据
官方数据集存放在 Hugging Face Hub 上：[`QCRI/ArGuard-Task1`](https://huggingface.co/datasets/QCRI/ArGuard-Task1)。使用启动工具包中的辅助脚本下载：

```bash
git clone https://github.com/araieval/ArGuard-2026-tasks.git
cd ArGuard-2026-tasks/task1
pip install -r requirements.txt
python data/download_data.py
```

## 数据划分

| 数据集        | 记录数 | 标签   | 阶段                    | 用途                                    |
|--------------|------:|--------|------------------------|------------------------------------------|
| `train`      | 3,500 | 完整   | 开发阶段                | 模型训练                             |
| `dev`        |   500 | 完整   | 开发阶段                | 超参数选择、本地评估 |
| `dev_test`   |   500 | **无** | 开发阶段（排行榜）      | 开发阶段排行榜的提交目标 |
| `test`       |   500 | 完整   | 最终评估                | 盲目排序（三重标注黄金标准）      |

`dev_test` 数据集发布时已移除标签——这是开发阶段提交预测结果的文件。

## 记录架构（运行 `download_data.py` 后）
```json
{
    "id": "f9a8…b1.jpg",
    "image_path": "img/f9a8…b1.jpg",
    "text": "OCR 提取的阿拉伯文本…",
    "label": "Hateful" | "Not Hateful" | null,
    "fine_grained_label": ["Mocking", "Incitement"],
    "annotations": []
}
```

## 许可证
数据集采用 **CC BY-NC 4.0** 许可证发布（仅限非商业研究使用）。

## 资源
- 启动工具包：<https://github.com/araieval/ArGuard-2026-tasks>
- 任务网站：<https://araieval.gitlab.io/ArGuard2026/>

pages.title: Timeline
pages.content:
# Timeline

## Development phase
Use this phase to tune your models on `train.jsonl` / `dev.jsonl` and submit predictions on `dev_test.jsonl` for the leaderboard.

**Window:** `2026-05-14 → 2026-07-19`
**Submissions:** up to 5 per day, 100 total.

## Testing phase
The final blind test split is released. Train on `train.jsonl` + `dev.jsonl`, submit predictions on `test.jsonl` — your best valid submission counts toward the official ranking.

**Window:** `2026-07-20 → 2026-07-29`
**Submissions:** up to 20 per day, 100 total.

> Adjust the dates inside `competition.yaml` (the `phases.start` / `phases.end` fields) if your task schedule shifts.

## Workshop
Paper submissions and notification dates will be announced on the [task website](https://araieval.gitlab.io/ArGuard2026/).
pages.content_zh:
# 时间表

## 开发阶段
使用此阶段在 `train.jsonl` / `dev.jsonl` 上调试模型，并在 `dev_test.jsonl` 上提交预测以参与排行榜竞争。

**时间窗口：** `2026-05-14 → 2026-07-19`
**提交次数：** 每天最多 5 次，总计 100 次。

## 测试阶段
发布最终的盲测数据集。基于 `train.jsonl` + `dev.jsonl` 进行训练，在 `test.jsonl` 上提交预测——您最好的有效提交将被计入官方排名。

**时间窗口：** `2026-07-20 → 2026-07-29`
**提交次数：** 每天最多 20 次，总计 100 次。

> 如果任务日程有变更，请调整 `competition.yaml` 中的日期（`phases.start` / `phases.end` 字段）。

## 研讨会
论文提交和通知日期将在 [任务网站](https://araieval.gitlab.io/ArGuard2026/) 上发布。

15830.title: Neural encoding with deep neural networks (v9 - 2026-04-20)
15830.description:
Predict fixation-aligned MEG activity (204 gradiometer channels, 110 ms post-fixation) for a held-out subject, given natural scene images and eye movement metadata from the Active Vision Semantics (AVS) dataset.

Sulewski, P., Amme, C., Hebart, M., König, P., & Kietzmann, T. (2025). Why we linger: Memory encoding, rather than visual processing demand, drives fixation timing on natural scenes-evidence from a large-scale MEG dataset.

Academic use only. The AVS dataset and all derived materials are provided exclusively for academic research and educational purposes within the course "Neural encoding with deep neural networks" (SoSe 2026) at Osnabrück University.
15830.description_zh:
预测注视对齐MEG活动（204个梯度计通道，注视后110毫秒），基于自然场景图像和来自主动视觉语义（AVS）数据集的眼动元数据，针对留存受试者进行预测。

Sulewski, P., Amme, C., Hebart, M., König, P., & Kietzmann, T. (2025). 为什么我们停留：记忆编码而非视觉处理需求驱动自然场景上的注视时间——来自大规模MEG数据集的证据。

仅限学术使用。AVS数据集和所有衍生材料专门为学术研究和教育目的提供，用于奥斯那布鲁克大学"深度神经网络神经编码"课程（2026年夏季学期）。

pages.title: Overview
pages.content:
# MEG Brain Encoding Challenge

## Goal

Build a model that predicts how the human brain responds to visual scenes during natural free viewing. Specifically, you will predict **fixation-aligned MEG activity** — the pattern of neural responses measured across 204 MEG gradiometer channels at 110 ms after each eye fixation onset.

## Background

The data come from the **Active Vision Semantics (AVS)** dataset, in which participants freely explored natural scene photographs while their eye movements and whole-head MEG were simultaneously recorded.

You are given data from five training subjects (subjects 1–5) and asked to generalise to a **held-out subject (subject 60)**, whose scene-viewing behaviour and associated MEG responses form the evaluation set.

## Task

**Challenge 1 — Snapshot encoding at 110 ms**

Given the fixation metadata (scene ID, gaze coordinates, duration, sequence position, etc.) and the corresponding scene images, predict the vector of 204 MEG gradiometer amplitudes at 110 ms post-fixation onset.

**Submission shape:** `(7750, 204)` — one row per fixation in the development set, 204 channels.

## Timeline

| Date | Event |
|------|-------|
| April 2026 | Challenge opens |
| July 2026 | Challenge closes |

## Limits

- 5 submissions per day
- 50 submissions total
pages.content_zh:
# MEG 脑编码挑战

## 目标

构建一个模型，预测人脑在自然自由浏览视觉场景时的响应方式。具体来说，你需要预测**固视对齐的 MEG 活动** — 即每次眼睛固视开始后 110 毫秒时在 204 个 MEG 梯度计通道上测量的神经反应模式。

## 背景

数据来自**主动视觉语义学（AVS）**数据集，其中参与者在自由探索自然场景照片的同时，他们的眼动和全头 MEG 被同时记录。

你获得来自五个训练被试（被试 1–5）的数据，并需要泛化到**保留的被试（被试 60）**，其场景浏览行为和相关 MEG 反应构成评估集。

## 任务

**挑战 1 — 110 毫秒的快照编码**

根据固视元数据（场景 ID、凝视坐标、持续时间、序列位置等）和相应的场景图像，预测固视开始后 110 毫秒处 204 个 MEG 梯度计的幅度向量。

**提交形状：** `(7750, 204)` — 开发集中每个固视一行，204 个通道。

## 时间表

| 日期 | 事件 |
|------|------|
| 2026 年 4 月 | 挑战开启 |
| 2026 年 7 月 | 挑战关闭 |

## 限制

- 每天 5 次提交
- 总共 50 次提交

pages.title: Data
pages.content:
# Data

## What You Receive

All data are distributed through the course repository. You do **not** upload data to Codabench — only your predictions.

### Training data (subjects 1–5)

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `training/meg_110ms.npy` | MEG at 110 ms, shape `(n_fixations, 204)` |
| `training/metadata.csv` | Per-fixation metadata (see columns below) |
| `training/channel_names.txt` | Ordered list of 204 gradiometer channel names |
| `avs_scenes/` | Full scene images (shared across subjects) |

### Development set (subject 60)

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `subject60/challenge1_dev/metadata.csv` | 7750 fixations for which you submit predictions |

The ground truth MEG for subject 60 is **withheld** and used only for scoring.

## Metadata Columns

Key columns in `metadata.csv`:

| Column | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `sceneID` | Integer scene identifier — links to image file |
| `mean_gx`, `mean_gy` | Mean gaze position in screen-centred pixels (origin = centre, y-axis up) |
| `duration` | Fixation duration [ms] |
| `fix_sequence` | Fixation index within the trial (1 = first fixation) |
| `time_in_trial` | Time since trial onset [ms] |
| `subject` | Subject identifier |

## Scene Images

Scene images are JPEGs named `{sceneID:012d}_MEG_size.jpg`, scaled to the presentation resolution (1024 × 768 px). Fixation patches (112 × 112 px, centred on gaze) can be extracted using `megcourselib.data.stim.extract_crop()`.
pages.content_zh:
# 数据

## 你将获得的内容

所有数据通过课程仓库分发。你**不需要**将数据上传到 Codabench — 只需上传你的预测结果。

### 训练数据（被试 1–5）

| 文件 | 描述 |
|------|-------------|
| `training/meg_110ms.npy` | 110 ms 处的脑磁图，形状 `(n_fixations, 204)` |
| `training/metadata.csv` | 每个凝视点的元数据（见下表列名） |
| `training/channel_names.txt` | 204 个梯度计通道名称的有序列表 |
| `avs_scenes/` | 完整场景图像（被试间共享） |

### 开发集（被试 60）

| 文件 | 描述 |
|------|-------------|
| `subject60/challenge1_dev/metadata.csv` | 7750 个凝视点，需要提交预测 |

被试 60 的真实脑磁图被**隐藏**，仅用于评分。

## 元数据列

`metadata.csv` 中的关键列：

| 列名 | 描述 |
|--------|-------------|
| `sceneID` | 整数场景标识符 — 链接至图像文件 |
| `mean_gx`, `mean_gy` | 屏幕中心坐标系中的平均凝视位置（原点 = 中心，y 轴向上） |
| `duration` | 凝视持续时间 [毫秒] |
| `fix_sequence` | 试验内凝视序号（1 = 首次凝视） |
| `time_in_trial` | 试验开始后的时间 [毫秒] |
| `subject` | 被试标识符 |

## 场景图像

场景图像为 JPEG 文件，命名格式 `{sceneID:012d}_MEG_size.jpg`，缩放至呈现分辨率（1024 × 768 像素）。凝视块（112 × 112 像素，以凝视位置为中心）可使用 `megcourselib.data.stim.extract_crop()` 提取。

pages.title: Evaluation
pages.content:
# Evaluation

## Metric

Submissions are evaluated using **Pearson correlation (r)** between predicted and actual MEG amplitudes, computed **independently for each of the 204 channels** across all 7750 fixations in the development set.

This gives a vector **r** ∈ ℝ^204, from which four leaderboard scores are derived:

| Column | Definition |
|--------|-----------|
| **Mean r** *(primary)* | `mean(r)` — average encoding across all channels |
| **Median r** | `median(r)` — robust central tendency |
| **Max r** | `max(r)` — best single-channel encoding |
| **Worst-10 Channels** | `mean(r[argsort(r)[:10]])` — average of the 10 worst channels |

Rankings are determined by **Mean r** (higher is better). All other columns are shown for diagnostic purposes.

## Submission Format

1. Create a NumPy array of predictions with shape `(7750, 204)` and dtype `float64`.
   - Row order must match `subject60/challenge1_dev/metadata.csv` exactly.
   - Column order must match `channel_names.txt` exactly (MEG0001 … MEG0204).

2. Save it as `predictions.npy`:
   ```python
   import numpy as np
   np.save("predictions.npy", pred)   # shape (7750, 204)
   ```

3. Zip it (the file must be at the root of the zip, not in a subfolder):
   ```bash
   zip submission.zip predictions.npy
   ```

4. Upload `submission.zip` via the Submit button.

## Validity Checks

The scoring program rejects submissions that:
- Have the wrong shape (anything other than 7750 × 204)
- Contain NaN or Inf values

Invalid submissions receive a score of 0.0 on all columns.
pages.content_zh:
# 评估

## 指标

提交内容使用**皮尔逊相关系数(r)**进行评估，该系数在**204个通道中各自独立计算**，涵盖开发集中所有7750个注视点。

这产生一个向量 **r** ∈ ℝ^204，据此推导四个排行榜分数：

| 列 | 定义 |
|--------|-----------|
| **平均 r** *(主要)* | `mean(r)` — 所有通道的平均编码 |
| **中位数 r** | `median(r)` — 稳健的集中趋势 |
| **最大 r** | `max(r)` — 最佳单通道编码 |
| **最差10通道** | `mean(r[argsort(r)[:10]])` — 最差10个通道的平均值 |

排名由**平均 r**决定（越高越好）。所有其他列仅用于诊断目的。

## 提交格式

1. 创建一个形状为 `(7750, 204)`、数据类型为 `float64` 的NumPy数组。
   - 行顺序必须与 `subject60/challenge1_dev/metadata.csv` 完全匹配。
   - 列顺序必须与 `channel_names.txt` 完全匹配（MEG0001 … MEG0204）。

2. 将其保存为 `predictions.npy`：
   ```python
   import numpy as np
   np.save("predictions.npy", pred)   # 形状 (7750, 204)
   ```

3. 压缩它（文件必须在zip的根目录，不在子文件夹中）：
   ```bash
   zip submission.zip predictions.npy
   ```

4. 通过提交按钮上传 `submission.zip`。

## 有效性检查

评分程序拒绝以下提交：
- 形状错误（非7750 × 204）
- 包含NaN或Inf值

无效提交在所有列上的得分为0.0。

15805.title: GenText-Forensics: Challenge on Explainable Forensics and Adversarial Generation for Text-Centric Images

pages.title: Overview
pages.content:
The rapid advancement of AIGC technologies has significantly lowered the barrier to digital content manipulation, posing unprecedented challenges to multimedia information security. Attackers are no longer confined to conventional visual forgery; instead, they employ increasingly covert and sophisticated techniques to maliciously tamper with critical information in text-rich images such as financial documents and medical reports, introducing severe risks. In the AGI era, document tampering is evolving from "visible visual modifications" to a deep fusion of "visual forgery and semantic manipulation": subtler forgery traces, more abstract high-level semantic manipulation, and adversarial strategies such as visually similar character substitution not only evade traditional visual detection but may also trigger cascading poisoning risks across OCR–LLM pipelines. Meanwhile, semantic ambiguity and expression bias in multilingual scenarios further amplify security vulnerabilities. Against the backdrop of increasingly stringent global AI regulations, simple binary authenticity judgments can no longer meet practical demands, and explainable analytical and decision-making capabilities are becoming essential. However, existing research largely remains limited to unimodal or low-level visual features and treats detection, localization, and explanation as isolated tasks falling short of the AGI era's demand for complete evidence chains and reasoning capabilities. To address these challenges, we launch the ACM Multimedia 2026 Grand Challenge — "GenText-Forensics: Explainable Text Image Forensics and Adversarial Generation Challenge." This is the first AI security challenge dedicated to global multilingual text image multimedia forensics, aimed at tackling the rapidly growing critical security threats in the generative AI era. Grounded in multimedia forensics, this challenge reframes document tampering detection as a unified generative "forgery analysis" task that automatically produces structured forensic reports, enabling joint modeling of detection, localization, and explanation — thereby bridging the gap in evidence traceability and reasoning insight, streamlining the workflow, and significantly enhancing information security processing efficiency.
To overcome data bottlenecks, we have constructed the world's first large-scale multilingual document forgery benchmark — RealText-V2. This dataset contains 20K+ multimodal samples spanning 6 major application domains including finance, healthcare, and education, and supports 6 languages including Chinese, English, and Thai. It combines real-world data with AIGC-generated samples, encompasses 100+ attack methods ranging from character-level to sentence-level manipulations, and provides pixel-level tampering localization, attack type annotations, and expert-level natural language explanations, establishing a solid data foundation for the unified forgery analysis report generation task.
pages.content_zh:
AIGC技术的快速进步显著降低了数字内容操控的门槛，对多媒体信息安全构成了前所未有的挑战。攻击者不再局限于传统视觉伪造手段，而是采用日益隐蔽和复杂的技术恶意篡改文本丰富图像（如财务文件和医疗报告）中的关键信息，引入了严重风险。在AGI时代，文档篡改正从"可见的视觉修改"演变为"视觉伪造和语义操纵"的深度融合：更加细微的伪造痕迹、更加抽象的高级语义操纵，以及视觉相似字符替换等对抗策略不仅规避了传统视觉检测，还可能在OCR-LLM管道中引发级联中毒风险。同时，多语言场景中的语义歧义和表达偏差进一步放大了安全漏洞。在全球AI监管日益严格的背景下，简单的二元真伪判断已无法满足实际需求，可解释性分析和决策能力正成为必需品。然而，现有研究主要局限于单模态或低层级视觉特征，且将检测、定位和解释视为孤立任务，不符合AGI时代对完整证据链和推理能力的需求。为应对这些挑战，我们启动ACM Multimedia 2026大挑战赛——"GenText-Forensics：可解释文本图像取证与对抗生成挑战赛"。这是全球首个致力于多语言文本图像多媒体取证的AI安全挑战赛，旨在应对生成式AI时代快速增长的关键安全威胁。植根于多媒体取证，本挑战赛将文档篡改检测重新定义为统一生成式"伪造分析"任务，自动生成结构化取证报告，实现检测、定位和解释的联合建模——从而填补证据追溯和推理洞察的空白，简化工作流程，显著提升信息安全处理效率。

为克服数据瓶颈，我们构建了全球首个大规模多语言文档伪造基准——RealText-V2。该数据集包含20000+多模态样本，跨越金融、医疗和教育等6大应用领域，支持中文、英文和泰文等6种语言。它融合了真实世界数据和AIGC生成样本，包含从字符级到句子级操纵的100多种攻击方法，并提供了像素级篡改定位、攻击类型标注和专家级自然语言解释，为统一伪造分析报告生成任务奠定了坚实的数据基础。

pages.title: Overview
pages.content:
# Foundation Model for Ultrasound Biometry (FU_Biometry) - Basic Information

All time nodes are for 2026, with the final result announcement subject to MICCAI 2026 official deadlines.

Accurate and reliable ultrasound measurement is essential for clinical diagnosis and decision-making in prenatal, pediatric, and adult care. Current manual and semi-automated methods are time-consuming, operator-dependent, and suffer from high inter- and intra-observer variability. Automated and generalizable systems can reduce measurement time by 50%–80% and improve clinical workflow efficiency.

Despite advances from previous challenges (PSFHS 2023, IUGC 2024/2025, FUGC 2025, FM_UIA 2026), task-specific deep learning models lack robustness and generalization across diverse scanners, protocols, anatomies, and clinical settings.
This challenge establishes a large-scale unified benchmark for ultrasound biometry, covering prenatal, intrapartum, and adult cardiac ultrasound with over 20 clinical parameters. It promotes generalizable, multi-task, cross-domain models with improved efficiency, measurement consistency, and real-world clinical utility.

# Challenge Objectives
To develop automated, generalizable ultrasound biometry methods robust to devices, centers, and populations, with clinically validated end-to-end evaluation.

# Aim of the Challenge
1.Provide a large multi-center, multi-device dataset with expert annotations for training and validation.

2.Evaluate anatomical landmark detection and over 20 clinical biometric measurements.

3.Assess key clinical biomarkers derived from automated measurements.

4.Foster collaboration between AI researchers and clinical imaging experts.

5.Advance reliable fully automatic ultrasound quantification using landmark error and clinical measurement error.

# Resources
Participants can get the baseline the following channel：

https://github.com/lijiake2408/Foundation-Model-for-Ultrasound-Biometry

Training set: Released with full annotations for model development.
Validation set: Raw images distributed to participants. Upload your predictions in submission.zip to the platform for scoring.
Test set: Hosted by the organizers. Your Docker image will be run on this data during the final phase.

How to get datasetsParticipants can get the train dataset through the following channels:

Baiduyunpan：https://pan.baidu.com/s/14pFgrB0jPxtN3MrMAX4GsQ?pwd=1234
GoogleDrive:https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1xae7zzKL5YsiIteFJWZEqCfAsSFeGk-z?usp=sharing

If you have any questions regarding the task, please send an email to:

lijiakekobe@gmail.com

ljk.me@qq.com
pages.content_zh:
# 超声生物测量基础模型（FU_Biometry）- 基本信息

所有时间节点均为2026年，最终结果公布以MICCAI 2026官方截止日期为准。

准确可靠的超声测量对产前、儿科和成人医疗的临床诊断和决策至关重要。当前手工和半自动化方法耗时较长、依赖操作者，且存在高的观察者间和观察者内变异性。自动化和可推广的系统可将测量时间减少50%-80%，并提高临床工作流效率。

尽管以往挑战赛（PSFHS 2023、IUGC 2024/2025、FUGC 2025、FM_UIA 2026）取得进展，但特定任务的深度学习模型在不同扫描仪、协议、解剖结构和临床场景中缺乏鲁棒性和泛化能力。

本挑战赛建立了大规模统一的超声生物测量基准，涵盖产前、分娩期和成人心脏超声，包含超过20个临床参数。旨在推动可推广的多任务、跨域模型的发展，提高测量效率、测量一致性和实际临床应用价值。

# 挑战赛目标
开发自动化、可推广的超声生物测量方法，对设备、医疗中心和人群具有鲁棒性，并通过临床验证进行端到端评估。

# 挑战赛宗旨
1. 提供大规模多中心、多设备数据集，包含专家标注，用于模型训练和验证。

2. 评估解剖学标志点检测和超过20种临床生物测量。

3. 评估从自动测量导出的关键临床生物标志物。

4. 促进AI研究人员与临床影像专家的合作。

5. 推进完全自动超声定量的可靠性，通过标志点误差和临床测量误差进行评估。

# 资源
参赛者可通过以下途径获得基线：

https://github.com/lijiake2408/Foundation-Model-for-Ultrasound-Biometry

训练集：发布完整标注，供模型开发使用。
验证集：向参赛者分发原始图像。将预测结果上传至submission.zip提交到平台进行评分。
测试集：由组织者托管。在最终阶段，您的Docker镜像将在该数据上运行。

# 获取数据集方式
参赛者可通过以下途径获取训练数据集：

百度网盘：https://pan.baidu.com/s/14pFgrB0jPxtN3MrMAX4GsQ?pwd=1234
谷歌云盘：https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1xae7zzKL5YsiIteFJWZEqCfAsSFeGk-z?usp=sharing

如您对任务有任何疑问，请发送邮件至：

lijiakekobe@gmail.com

ljk.me@qq.com

pages.title: Evaluation
pages.content:
# FU_Biometry Challenge - Evaluation Methods

## 1. Assessment Aims (No Priority Order)
- Optimize algorithm performance in the following properties:
  - Accuracy
  - Complexity
  - Hardware requirements
  - Runtime
  - Robustness
  - Sensitivity
  - Specificity

## 2. Core Evaluation Metrics
Two metrics with **equal 50% weighting**, both used for final ranking:
### 2.1 Mean Radial Error (MRE)
- **Purpose**: Measure landmark localization accuracy.
- **Calculation**: For a case with K required landmarks (predictions {p_k}, references {g_k}):
  \[MRE =(1 / K) \sum_{k=1}^{K}|| p_k-g_k||_2\]
- **Unit**: Pixels; converted to millimeters (mm) if pixel spacing is available.
- **Note**: Only computed for the applicable subset of landmarks for each image.

### 2.2 Ultrasound Parameter Prediction Difference
- **Purpose**: Measure the accuracy of clinically derived parameters.
- **Calculation**: Mean Absolute Error (MAE) |ŷ_j − y_j| (ŷ_j = predicted parameter, y_j = manual measurement) in native physical units (mm/degrees).
- **Normalization**: Each parameter is normalized using clinically defined tolerance ranges or interquartile range (IQR) to prevent large-range parameters from dominating evaluation.
- **Reporting**: Both raw and normalized errors are published for transparency.

## 3. Ranking Calculation Method
### 3.1 Score Aggregation
- Final score = **50% normalized MRE + 50% normalized parameter error** (equal weighting to balance geometric accuracy and clinical measurement reliability).

### 3.2 Ranking Framework (ChallengeR-style rank-then-aggregate)
1. Domain-balanced weighting across 4 predefined data domains (avoid dominance of a single acquisition source).
2. Dual leaderboards: **overall ranking** + **domain-stratified ranking** (for transparency).
3. Tie-breaking rule: Superior measurement accuracy (lower normalized parameter error) takes priority (higher clinical relevance).

### 3.3 Missing Result Handling
- Submissions with missing test case results are penalized by assigning the **worst performance score** for the corresponding metric among all valid submissions.

## 4. Statistical Analyses
### 4.1 Software Tools
- R language; Python libraries (SciPy, Pandas) (widely accepted in scientific research).

### 4.2 Missing Data Handling
- No multiple imputation; missing outputs are penalized (final results have no missing values).
- Bootstrap 95% confidence intervals (CIs) with 1,000 resamples are reported for all metrics.

### 4.3 Performance Precision Assessment
- **Percentile bootstrap 95% CIs (1,000 resamples)** on the test set for all algorithm performance estimates.

### 4.4 Performance Variability Assessment
- Across test cases: Per-case metric distributions reported via standard deviation (SD), interquartile range (IQR), boxplots/violin plots, and explicit outlier reporting.
- Ranking variability: Assessed using the **bootstrap method** (nonparametric, minimal assumptions).

### 4.5 Statistical Significance Test
- **Paired Wilcoxon signed-rank test** for pairwise algorithm performance comparisons (nonparametric, adapted for non-normal metric distributions).
- Multiple-comparison correction (Holm or Benjamini–Hochberg) to avoid false positives.
- Effect sizes (e.g., Cliff’s delta) reported alongside p-values for clinical interpretability.
- Domain/device-stratified analyses to contextualize cross-domain generalization.

### 4.6 Data Assumption Validation
- Pre-validation tests for all statistical methods:
  1. Shapiro-Wilk test: Assess data normality.
  2. Levene's test: Evaluate variance homogeneity.

## 5. Further Analyses
1. Performance verification of **ensemble models** built from the top-ranked teams’ algorithms.
2. Analysis of inter-algorithm variability, common biases/problems of submitted methods.
3. In-depth analysis of ranking variability across different data domains/devices.
pages.content_zh:
# FU_Biometry Challenge - 评估方法

## 1. 评估目标（无优先级顺序）
- 优化以下属性的算法性能：
  - 精确度
  - 复杂性
  - 硬件要求
  - 运行时间
  - 鲁棒性
  - 灵敏度
  - 特异性

## 2. 核心评估指标
两个指标**等权重50%**，均用于最终排名：

### 2.1 平均径向误差（MRE）
- **目的**：测量地标定位精度。
- **计算**：对于具有K个必需地标的病例（预测值{p_k}、参考值{g_k}）：
  \[MRE =(1 / K) \sum_{k=1}^{K}|| p_k-g_k||_2\]
- **单位**：像素；如果有像素间距，转换为毫米（mm）。
- **注意**：仅对每个图像的适用地标子集计算。

### 2.2 超声参数预测差异
- **目的**：测量临床衍生参数的精度。
- **计算**：平均绝对误差（MAE）|ŷ_j − y_j|（ŷ_j = 预测参数，y_j = 手动测量）以原始物理单位（mm/度）表示。
- **归一化**：使用临床定义的容差范围或四分位间距（IQR）对每个参数进行归一化，防止大范围参数主导评估。
- **报告**：为保证透明性，发布原始误差和归一化误差。

## 3. 排名计算方法
### 3.1 分数汇总
- 最终得分 = **50% 归一化MRE + 50% 归一化参数误差**（等权重以平衡几何精度和临床测量可靠性）。

### 3.2 排名框架（ChallengeR风格的排名-后聚合）
1. 跨4个预定义数据域的域平衡加权（避免单一采集源的主导）。
2. 双排行榜：**总体排名** + **域分层排名**（保证透明性）。
3. 并列打破规则：优先考虑测量精度更高（归一化参数误差更低）（临床相关性更高）。

### 3.3 缺失结果处理
- 缺少测试用例结果的提交通过为相应指标分配所有有效提交中的**最差性能分数**进行处罚。

## 4. 统计分析
### 4.1 软件工具
- R语言；Python库（SciPy、Pandas）（在科学研究中被广泛接受）。

### 4.2 缺失数据处理
- 无多重填补；缺失输出被罚（最终结果无缺失值）。
- 报告所有指标的95%引导置信区间（CI），1,000次重抽样。

### 4.3 性能精度评估
- **百分位数引导法95% CI（1,000次重抽样）**用于测试集上所有算法性能估计。

### 4.4 性能变异性评估
- 跨测试用例：通过标准差（SD）、四分位间距（IQR）、箱线图/小提琴图和明确异常值报告的逐案例指标分布。
- 排名变异性：使用**引导法**（非参数，最少假设）评估。

### 4.5 统计显著性检验
- **成对Wilcoxon符号秩检验**用于算法性能的成对比较（非参数，适用于非正态分布指标）。
- 多重比较校正（Holm或Benjamini-Hochberg）以避免假阳性。
- 效应量（如Cliff's delta）与p值一同报告，便于临床解释。
- 域/设备分层分析以阐明跨域泛化。

### 4.6 数据假设验证
- 所有统计方法的预验证测试：
  1. Shapiro-Wilk检验：评估数据正态性。
  2. Levene检验：评估方差齐性。

## 5. 进一步分析
1. 由排名最高的团队算法构建的**集成模型**的性能验证。
2. 算法间变异性、提交方法的常见偏差/问题的分析。
3. 跨不同数据域/设备排名变异性的深入分析。

pages.title: Participation Rules
pages.content:
# FU_Biometry Challenge - Participation Rules

## 1. Challenge Life Cycle
- Repeated event with annual fixed conference submission deadline

## 2. Participation Policies
### 2.1 Algorithm Development
- Participants must use all unlabeled data to develop semi-supervised methods.
- Only publicly available external datasets and pre-trained models are allowed for training; additional private annotations of public data are prohibited.

### 2.2 Organizer Institute Participants
- May participate in the challenge but are **not eligible for awards** and **not listed in the leaderboard**.

## 3. Submission Rules
### 3.1 Submission Method
- Testing phase: Only **one successful Docker container submission** per team (prevent repeated tuning on hidden test set).
- Strict separation between public validation leaderboard and private final test evaluation; final rankings based solely on unseen test set.
- Top-ranked submissions undergo manual verification (Docker executability, method descriptions, rule compliance).

### 3.2 Validation Phase Submission
- Teams may submit up to **10 times in total**, with no more than **2 submissions per day**.
- Validation scores serve only as sanity checks (completeness and format), not for ranking or comparative evaluation.

## 4. Award Policy
1. Certificates and cash awards for top teams, the most innovative method team, and teams with the most careful method description.
2. **Top 10 teams** are invited to give oral presentations at MICCAI 2026 and encouraged to submit extended manuscripts to the IEEE Transactions on Biomedical Engineering (TBME) Special Issue.
3. Co-authorship of the challenge compendium paper to be submitted to top journals (MedIA/TMI).

## 5. Result Announcement
- The **top 10 performing methods** are publicly announced by organizers.
- Announcement includes team names and overall ranking based on official evaluation metrics.

## 6. Publication Policy
### 6.1 Authorship Qualification
- Members of top-10 teams who made significant contributions to research conceptualization, design, execution or interpretation qualify as authors.

### 6.2 Independent Publication
- Participating teams may publish results separately, with respect for the challenge’s collective efforts.

### 6.3 Embargo Period
- Organizers publish a comprehensive challenge paper first; individual teams are encouraged to refrain from publishing complete findings independently during this period.

## 7. Code Availability Requirement
- Algorithm code release (via GitHub repository) and corresponding conference paper submission are **prerequisites for award eligibility** and further data usage.
pages.content_zh:
# FU_Biometry Challenge - 参与规则

## 1. 竞赛生命周期
- 重复性赛事，具有固定的年度会议投稿截止期

## 2. 参与政策
### 2.1 算法开发
- 参赛者必须使用所有无标签数据来开发半监督方法。
- 仅允许使用公开可用的外部数据集和预训练模型进行训练；禁止对公开数据进行额外的私人标注。

### 2.2 组织机构参赛者
- 可以参与竞赛，但**无获奖资格**且**不在排行榜中列出**。

## 3. 提交规则
### 3.1 提交方法
- 测试阶段：每支队伍仅允许提交**一个成功的Docker容器**（防止在隐藏测试集上重复调优）。
- 公开验证排行榜与私有最终测试评估严格分离；最终排名仅基于未见过的测试集。
- 排名靠前的提交需要进行人工验证（Docker可执行性、方法描述、规则符合性）。

### 3.2 验证阶段提交
- 队伍在总共**10次**提交中，每天最多**2次**提交。
- 验证分数仅用于完整性和格式的合理性检查，不用于排名或比较评估。

## 4. 奖励政策
1. 为排名靠前的队伍、最具创新方法的队伍以及方法描述最详细的队伍提供证书和现金奖励。
2. **前10支队伍**受邀在MICCAI 2026进行口头报告，并鼓励向《IEEE生物医学工程学报》(TBME) 特刊投稿扩展论文。
3. 竞赛综述论文的共同作者身份，该论文将投稿至顶级期刊（MedIA/TMI）。

## 5. 结果公布
- 组织方公布**排名前10的最优方法**。
- 公布内容包括队伍名称及基于官方评估指标的总体排名。

## 6. 发表政策
### 6.1 作者资格
- 前10支队伍的成员，若在研究的概念化、设计、执行或解释中做出重大贡献，符合作者资格。

### 6.2 独立发表
- 参赛队伍可以独立发表结果，同时尊重竞赛的集体努力。

### 6.3 禁发期
- 组织方首先发表全面的竞赛论文；建议各队伍在此期间避免独立发表完整研究成果。

## 7. 代码可用性要求
- 算法代码发布（通过GitHub仓库）和相应的会议论文投稿是**获奖资格**和进一步数据使用的**先决条件**。

15553.title: MICCAI FLARE 2026 Task 2: AutoMSC: Automated Medical Image Segmentation and Classification
15553.description: MICCAI FLARE 2026 Task 2: AutoMSC: Automated Medical image Segmentation and Classification
15553.description_zh: MICCAI FLARE 2026 任务2：AutoMSC：自动医学影像分割与分类

pages.title: Overview
pages.content:
##
[MICCAI FLARE26](https://flare-medfm.github.io/) Task 2: AutoMSC: Automated Medical image Segmentation and Classification
Deep learning models for 3D medical imaging have traditionally been handcrafted for individual tasks, either segmentation or classification, requiring extensive manual tuning of architectures, hyperparameters, and preprocessing pipelines. As clinical datasets grow in scale and complexity, this manual design process becomes increasingly inefficient and limits the deployment of adaptable, general-purpose models. Motivated by the success of the Medical Segmentation Decathlon challenge and nnU-Net (the winning solution), Task 2 focuses on building self-configured models capable of automatically adapting to diverse 3D medical imaging datasets while simultaneously performing both segmentation and classification. This task aims to advance the next generation of autonomous modeldesign systems for 3D medical imaging. Participants are required to design models that can automatically infer optimal configurations (e.g., architecture, resolution, patch size, normalization strategy, and loss balancing) and jointly learn highquality segmentation masks and clinically meaningful classification labels. These models should generalize across imaging modality, tumor types without taskspecific manual engineering, including prostate cancer recurrence prediction in MRI scans, lung nodule diagnosis in CT scans, and liver metastasis origin prediction in PET/CT scans. Key Features: - **Multi-task Learning**: Models must perform both 3D segmentation and classification from the same input volume, optimizing for accuracy in both outputs while maintaining computational efficiency. - **Self-configuration**: Solutions should include automated configuration mechanisms capable of adapting to dataset properties, such as voxel spacing, image size, contrast variability, and class imbalance, without human-designed heuristics. - **Dataset Diversity**: The benchmark includes heterogeneous 3D medical imaging scans with varying modalities (CT, MRI, and PET-CT) and classification targets, encouraging the development of truly generalizable 3D models. - **Robustness and Efficiency**: Models will be evaluated on segmentation accuracy (DSC and NSD), classification performance (balanced accuracy and AUROC), and resource efficiency (runtime), highlighting their potential for real-world clinical adoption. Participants are also encouraged to explore other challenge tracks at https://flare-medfm.github.io/, including: pan-cancer segmentation, and multimodal models. --- ## 🚀 Join the challenge (Open on 2026.04.01) * Register an account on this website and join the challenge * Fill out the [registration form](https://forms.gle/my1MimQ758oDyhWb9) Note: To change team information, please simply re-submit the form. * Request Dataset [FLARE-AutoMSC](https://huggingface.co/datasets/FLARE-MedFM/FLARE-AutoMSC). We will approve your request in 1-2 working days after reviewing your application. Remarks: * This challenge has two tracks: 1) Unified models for joint segmentation and classification (e.g., [JSC](https://github.com/bowang-lab/JSC)). 2) Independent models for segmentation (e.g., [nnUNet](https://github.com/MIC-DKFZ/nnUNet)) or classification ([nnClass](https://github.com/bowang-lab/nnClass)). Participants can join one track or both tracks. * For both tracks, solutions should include automated configuration mechanisms capable of adapting to different datasets. * If you have any questions that are not listed on the QA page, please reach out to chingyuanyu.iec@gmail.com --- ## 🏊‍ Validation submission (2026.4.15-8.05) * All teams can directly submit the validation results on the challenge page to get the metrics. * All the ground truth files have been released for validation tasks. participants should report validation performance in the manuscript. --- ## 🏹 Testing submission (2026.8.05-8.20) The submitted Docker container will be evaluated with the following commands. If the Docker container does not work or the paper does not include all the necessary information to reproduce the method, we will return back the error information and review comments to participants. docker load -i teamname.tar.gz docker container run --gpus "device=1" -m 28G --name teamname --rm -v \$PWD/FLARE_Test/:/workspace/inputs/ -v \$PWD/teamname_outputs/:/workspace/outputs/ teamname:latest /bin/bash -c "sh predict.sh" ## Paper submission(Due on 8.31) Please parepare the paper with this [template-tbd]() and submit it on [openreview](https://openreview.net/group) by Aug. 31, 2026. Please carefully read the template and the common issue before writing the manuscript. The evaluation process mainly focuses on the paper's completeness. Don't worry about the low performance. Since this task is very challenging, all attempts are worth sharing with readers. We will not reject papers because of Iow performance. --- ## 🏆 Awards - If there are more than 20 testing docker submissions, we will provide cash prizes for the top 5 teams (Amazon gift cards with 300/200/150/100/100 CAD, respectively) and souvenirs for the top 6-10 teams. Otherwise, only the winning team will be provided with the cash prize. - The top 5 teams will be invited to give oral presentations during MICCAI 2026. - The first author of the top 5 teams will be invited to contribute to a challenge summary paper. - A certificate will be awarded to the top 10 teams. (Top teams should make their code publicly available on GitHub for reproducibility.) - All teams have the opportunity to publish their methods in the challenge proceeding (Tentative LNCS, EI Index). [Here](https://link.springer.com/conference/flare) is an example of our previous FLARE Challenge. The challenge summary paper will be submitted to top-tired journals. Here are some of our previous summaries: * "Fast and low-GPU-memory abdomen CT organ segmentation: the flare challenge." Medical Image Analysis 82 (2022): 102616. * "Unleashing the strengths of unlabeled data in pan-cancer abdominal organ quantification: the flare22 challenge." Lancet Digital Health (2024). * "The multimodality cell segmentation challenge: toward universal solutions." Nature Methods (2024): 21, 1103–1113.
pages.content_zh:
## <center>[MICCAI FLARE26](https://flare-medfm.github.io/) 任务2：AutoMSC：自动医学影像分割与分类</center>

用于3D医学影像的深度学习模型传统上是针对单个任务（分割或分类）手工设计的，需要大量的架构、超参数和预处理管道的手动调整。随着临床数据集规模和复杂性的增加，这种手动设计流程变得越来越低效，限制了可适应的通用模型的部署。受医学分割十项全能挑战赛和获胜解决方案nnU-Net成功的启发，任务2重点关注构建自配置模型，能够自动适应多种3D医学影像数据集，同时执行分割和分类两项任务。

该任务旨在推进3D医学影像自主模型设计系统的下一代发展。参赛者需要设计能够自动推断最优配置（例如，架构、分辨率、补丁大小、归一化策略和损失平衡）的模型，并联合学习高质量分割掩码和临床有意义的分类标签。这些模型应该能够跨越成像模态和肿瘤类型进行泛化，无需特定任务的手动工程，包括MRI扫描中的前列腺癌复发预测、CT扫描中的肺结节诊断和PET/CT扫描中的肝转移灶来源预测。

主要特性：

- **多任务学习**：模型必须从同一输入体积执行3D分割和分类，同时优化两个输出的准确性，保持计算效率。
- **自配置**：解决方案应包括自动化配置机制，能够适应数据集属性，如体素间距、图像大小、对比度变异性和类别不平衡，无需人工设计的启发式方法。
- **数据集多样性**：基准包括具有不同模态（CT、MRI和PET-CT）和分类目标的异构3D医学影像扫描，促进真正可泛化3D模型的开发。
- **鲁棒性和效率**：将根据分割准确性（DSC和NSD）、分类性能（平衡准确性和AUROC）和资源效率（运行时间）对模型进行评估，突出其在实际临床应用中的潜力。

参赛者还被鼓励探索https://flare-medfm.github.io/上的其他挑战赛道，包括：泛癌分割和多模态模型。

---

## 🚀 加入挑战赛（2026.04.01开放）

* 在本网站上注册账户并加入挑战赛
* 填写[注册表](https://forms.gle/my1MimQ758oDyhWb9)   注：如需更改团队信息，请重新提交表单。
* 申请数据集[FLARE-AutoMSC](https://huggingface.co/datasets/FLARE-MedFM/FLARE-AutoMSC)。我们将在审查您的申请后的1-2个工作日内批准您的请求。

备注：
* 本挑战赛有两个赛道：1）用于联合分割和分类的统一模型（例如[JSC](https://github.com/bowang-lab/JSC)）。2）分割的独立模型（例如[nnUNet](https://github.com/MIC-DKFZ/nnUNet)）或分类模型（[nnClass](https://github.com/bowang-lab/nnClass)）。参赛者可参加一个赛道或两个赛道。
* 对于两个赛道，解决方案应包括能够适应不同数据集的自动化配置机制。
* 如果您有问题未在常见问题解答页面中列出，请联系chingyuanyu.iec@gmail.com

---

## 🏊‍ 验证提交（2026.4.15-8.05）

* 所有团队可直接在挑战赛页面上提交验证结果以获取指标。
* 所有真值文件已为验证任务发布。参赛者应在手稿中报告验证性能。

---

## 🏹 测试提交（2026.8.05-8.20）

提交的Docker

pages.title: Dataset
pages.content:
## Dataset

                                                                                      
                                                                                                   
  ### Dataset 001 — PETWB_Lung                                                                      
  A subset of the **PETWB-REP** whole-body FDG PET/CT collection, paired with 
  TotalSegmentator-derived **lung masks** and a **lung-cancer vs. other** label. 490 cases drawn   
  from 4 cohorts, 2 channels (CT, PET). Tasks: binary lung segmentation + binary cancer-type 
  classification. Licence: CC BY 4.0.                                                              
  **Source:** Xue L, Feng G, Wenbo Z, et al. *PETWB-REP: A Multi-Cancer Whole-Body FDG PET/CT 
  Dataset with Corresponding Radiology Reports.* **Scientific Data**, 2026.                        
   
  ### Dataset 002 — BMLMPS_FLAIR                                                                    
  The FLAIR arm of the **Brain-Mets-Lung-MRI-Path-Segs** cohort: brain metastases from lung cancer
  imaged on FLAIR MRI, with **whole-tumor** masks and **EGFR mutation status** as the              
  classification target. 104 scans / 103 patients, single channel.
  **Source:** Chadha S, Sritharan DV, Dolezal D, et al. *Matched MRI, Segmentations, and           
  Histopathologic Images of Brain Metastases from Primary Lung Cancer.* **Scientific Data**, 10 Jan
   2026.
                                                                                                   
  ### Dataset 003 — BMLMPS_T1CE
  The T1 contrast-enhanced arm of the **same BMLMPS cohort** as Dataset 002, but targeting the 
  **enhancing core tumor** instead of whole tumor. 107 scans / 103 patients, single channel, also  
  paired with **EGFR mutation status**. Datasets 002 and 003 share patients but differ in MRI 
  sequence and segmentation target.                                                                
  **Source:** Chadha S, Sritharan DV, Dolezal D, et al. *Matched MRI, Segmentations, and 
  Histopathologic Images of Brain Metastases from Primary Lung Cancer.* **Scientific Data**, 10 Jan
   2026.
                                                                                                   
  ### Dataset 004 — BrainMets (PROTEAS)                                                             
  A **longitudinal multi-modal brain metastasis** dataset with 6 co-registered channels (T1, T1c, 
  T2, FLAIR, CT, RTP). Provides **3-class tumor segmentation** (necrotic core, enhancing tumor,    
  edema) and **NSCLC vs. Breast Carcinoma** primary-origin classification. 166 timepoints across 40
   patients. Licence: CC BY 4.0.                                                                   
  **Source:** Flouri D, Papanikas CP, Manikis GC, et al. *A longitudinal MRI dataset of brain 
  metastases with tumor segmentations, clinical & radiomic data.* **Scientific Data**, 19 Nov 2025;
   12:1828.
                                                                                                   
  ### Dataset 005 — MU_Glioma_Post                                                                  
  **Post-treatment glioma** multi-sequence MRI (t1c, t1n, t2f, t2w) with **4-class tumor 
  segmentation** (NCR, ED, ET, NET_RC) and a **3-class primary diagnosis** label (GBM / Astrocytoma
   / Other). 591 timepoints from 203 patients (longitudinal).
  **Source:** Mahmoud E, Gass J, Dhemesh Y, et al. *MU-Glioma Post: A comprehensive dataset of     
  automated MR multi-sequence segmentation and clinical features.* **Scientific Data**, 20 Nov     
  2025; 12(1):1847.
                                                                                                   
  ### Dataset 006 — JSC_UCSD_PTGB
  The **UCSD post-treatment glioblastoma** cohort imaged with T1post, FLAIR, and ADC. Provides
  **total cellular tumor** masks and **IDH mutation status** (wild-type vs. mutant). 184 cases, 3  
  channels.
  **Source:** Gagnon L, Gupta D, Nguyen U, et al. *The University of California San Diego          
  Post-Treatment Glioblastoma (UCSD-PTGBM) annotated multimodal MRI Dataset.* **Scientific Data**, 
  6 Jan 2026.
                                                                                                   
 ### Dataset 007 — PI-CAI                                                                          
  The largest dataset in the benchmark: **prostate MRI** (T2W, ADC, HBV) for **clinically 
  significant prostate cancer (csPCa) segmentation** and **6-class ISUP grade** classification     
  (Benign/Indolent + ISUP 1–5). 1,500 cases from 1,476 patients across 11 sites.
  **Source:** Saha A, Bosma JS, Twilt JJ, et al. *Artificial intelligence and radiologists in      
  prostate cancer detection on MRI (PI-CAI): an international, paired, non-inferiority,            
  confirmatory study.* **Lancet Oncology**, 2024; 25:879–887.
                                                                                                   
  ### Dataset 008 — PETWB_Liver
  Companion to Dataset 001 from the **same PETWB-REP source**, but targeting the **liver**: 
  TotalSegmentator-derived liver masks paired with a **liver-cancer vs. other** label. 490 cases, 2
   channels (CT, PET). Licence: CC BY 4.0.
  **Source:** Xue L, Feng G, Wenbo Z, et al. *PETWB-REP: A Multi-Cancer Whole-Body FDG PET/CT      
  Dataset with Corresponding Radiology Reports.* **Scientific Data**, 2026.                        
   
  ### Dataset 009 — LUNA25                                                                          
  Cropped **low-dose chest CT patches** from the **LUNA25 challenge** for lung cancer screening. 
  Each patch contains a nodule with a **segmentation mask** and a **benign vs. malignant** risk    
  label. 6,141 nodule crops, single CT channel. Licence: CC BY 4.0.
  **Source:** Peeters D, Obreja B, Antonissen N, Jacobs C. *Benchmarking of Artificial Intelligence
   and Radiologists for Lung Cancer Screening in CT: The LUNA25 Challenge.* **MICCAI 2025**        
  (Zenodo: [10.5281/zenodo.15094631](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.15094631)).
pages.content_zh:
## 数据集

### 数据集 001 — PETWB_Lung
**PETWB-REP** 全身 FDG PET/CT 集合的子集，配对 TotalSegmentator 衍生的**肺部掩膜**和**肺癌 vs. 其他**标签。来自 4 个队列的 490 个病例，2 个通道（CT、PET）。任务：二元肺分割 + 二元癌症类型分类。许可证：CC BY 4.0。

**来源：** Xue L, Feng G, Wenbo Z, 等. *PETWB-REP: A Multi-Cancer Whole-Body FDG PET/CT Dataset with Corresponding Radiology Reports.* **Scientific Data**, 2026.

### 数据集 002 — BMLMPS_FLAIR
**脑转移-肺-MRI-病理-分割**队列的 FLAIR 部分：肺癌脑转移在 FLAIR MRI 上成像，配有**全肿瘤**掩膜和**EGFR 突变状态**作为分类目标。104 次扫描 / 103 患者，单通道。

**来源：** Chadha S, Sritharan DV, Dolezal D, 等. *Matched MRI, Segmentations, and Histopathologic Images of Brain Metastases from Primary Lung Cancer.* **Scientific Data**, 2026 年 1 月 10 日.

### 数据集 003 — BMLMPS_T1CE
**相同 BMLMPS 队列**（数据集 002）的 T1 增强对比臂，但针对**增强肿瘤核心**而非全肿瘤。107 次扫描 / 103 患者，单通道，亦配对 **EGFR 突变状态**。数据集 002 和 003 共享患者但 MRI 序列和分割目标不同。

**来源：** Chadha S, Sritharan DV, Dolezal D, 等. *Matched MRI, Segmentations, and Histopathologic Images of Brain Metastases from Primary Lung Cancer.* **Scientific Data**, 2026 年 1 月 10 日.

### 数据集 004 — BrainMets (PROTEAS)
**纵向多模态脑转移**数据集，包含 6 个共配准通道（T1、T1c、T2、FLAIR、CT、RTP）。提供**3 类肿瘤分割**（坏死核心、增强肿瘤、水肿）和**NSCLC vs. 乳腺癌**原发部位分类。40 患者的 166 个时间点。许可证：CC BY 4.0。

**来源：** Flouri D, Papanikas CP, Manikis GC, 等. *A longitudinal MRI dataset of brain metastases with tumor segmentations, clinical & radiomic data.* **Scientific Data**, 2025 年 11 月 19 日; 12:1828.

### 数据集 005 — MU_Glioma_Post
**治疗后胶质瘤**多序列 MRI（t1c、t1n、t2f、t2w），包含**4 类肿瘤分割**（NCR、ED、ET、NET_RC）和**3 类原发诊断**标签（GBM / 星形细胞瘤 / 其他）。203 患者的 591 个时间点（纵向）。

**来源：** Mahmoud E, Gass J, Dhemesh Y, 等. *MU-Glioma Post: A comprehensive dataset of automated MR multi-sequence segmentation and clinical features.* **Scientific Data**, 2025 年 11 月 20 日; 12(1):1847.

### 数据集 006 — JSC_UCSD_PTGB
**加州大学圣地亚哥分校治疗后胶质母细胞瘤**队列，使用 T1post、FLAIR 和 ADC 成像。提供**全细胞肿瘤**掩膜和 **IDH 突变状态**（野生型 vs. 突变型）。184 个病例，3 个通道。

**来源：** [文本不完整]

pages.title: Evaluation
pages.content: ## Evaluation **Segmentation accuracy metrics:** * Dice Similarity Coefficient (DSC) * Normalized Surface Distance (NSD) **Binary classification accuracy metrics:** * Balanced Accuracy * Area Under the Receiver Operating Characteristic Curve (AUROC) **Accuracy Evaluation Scripts:** * https://github.com/medfm-flare/AutoMSC_Baseline **Segmentation efficiency metrics:** * Running time (s). We give running time a tolerance of 45s (including the docker starting time). Moreover, the inference time for each case should be within 60s. Otherwise, the corresponding case will be regarded as a failed case (DSC=0, NSD=0). ![running time](https://s2.loli.net/2024/04/06/Fvt1nmPYqZLWxsU.jpg) * GPU memory consumption (area under GPU memory-time curve). ![gpu memory](https://s2.loli.net/2024/03/14/XlnhM3qB27yVZtS.png) All metrics will be used to compute the ranking. We give GPU memory consumption a 4GB tolerance, GPU Memory = max(0, Real GPU Memory-4096MB) because all the previous top teams (i.e., FLARE21-23) can achieve state-of-the-art segmentation accuracy within this tolerance. We also give running time a tolerance (the running time of the winning solution in FLARE 2023) because it satisfies the typical clinical requirements and the code has been publicly available. Evaluation code has been publicly available at: https://github.com/JunMa11/FLARE docker load -i teamname.tar.gz docker container run --gpus "device=1" -m 28G --name teamname --rm -v \$PWD/FLARE_Test/:/workspace/inputs/ -v \$PWD/teamname_outputs/:/workspace/outputs/ teamname:latest /bin/bash -c "sh predict.sh" --- ## Evaluation Platform The submitted docker containers will be evaluated on a Ubuntu 20.04 desktop. Detailed information is listed as follows: * CPU: Intel® Xeon(R) W-2133 CPU @ 3.60GHz × 12 * GPU: NVIDIA QUADRO RTX5000 (16G) * RAM: 28G * CUDA Version: 13.0 * Docker version 29.4.2 --- ## Ranking Scheme Following the recommendation in ChallengeR [1], we eomply a rank-then-aggregate ranking scheme with the following three steps: Step 1. For each testing case, we compute the four metrics; Step 2. Rank participants for each of the N testing cases and each metric; Step 3. Average all these rankings. [1] Wiesenfarth, M., Reinke, A., Landman, B.A. et al. Methods and open-source toolkit for analyzing and visualizing challenge results. Sci Rep 11, 2369 (2021).
pages.content_zh: ## 评估 **分割精度指标：** * Dice相似系数（DSC） * 归一化表面距离（NSD） **二分类精度指标：** * 平衡精度 * 受试者工作特征曲线下面积（AUROC） **精度评估脚本：** * https://github.com/medfm-flare/AutoMSC_Baseline **分割效率指标：** * 运行时间（秒）。我们给予运行时间45秒的容限（包括Docker启动时间）。此外，每个病例的推理时间应在60秒以内。否则，相应病例将被视为失败病例（DSC=0，NSD=0）。 ![运行时间](https://s2.loli.net/2024/04/06/Fvt1nmPYqZLWxsU.jpg) * GPU内存消耗（GPU内存-时间曲线下面积）。 ![GPU内存](https://s2.loli.net/2024/03/14/XlnhM3qB27yVZtS.png) 所有指标都将用于计算排名。我们给予GPU内存消耗4GB的容限，GPU内存 = max(0, 实际GPU内存-4096MB)，因为之前所有顶尖团队（即FLARE21-23）都能在此容限内达到最先进的分割精度。我们还给予运行时间一个容限（FLARE 2023获胜方案的运行时间），因为它满足典型的临床要求，且代码已公开发布。 评估代码已公开发布于：https://github.com/JunMa11/FLARE docker load -i teamname.tar.gz docker container run --gpus "device=1" -m 28G --name teamname --rm -v \$PWD/FLARE_Test/:/workspace/inputs/ -v \$PWD/teamname_outputs/:/workspace/outputs/ teamname:latest /bin/bash -c "sh predict.sh" --- ## 评估平台 提交的Docker容器将在Ubuntu 20.04桌面上进行评估。详细信息如下： * CPU：Intel® Xeon(R) W-2133 CPU @ 3.60GHz × 12 * GPU：NVIDIA QUADRO RTX5000（16G） * RAM：28G * CUDA版本：13.0 * Docker版本 29.4.2 --- ## 排名方案 遵循ChallengeR[1]中的建议，我们采用排名后汇总的排名方案，分为以下三个步骤： 步骤1. 对于每个测试病例，计算四个指标； 步骤2. 对每个N个测试病例和每个指标的参与者进行排名； 步骤3. 对所有这些排名进行平均。 [1] Wiesenfarth, M., Reinke, A., Landman, B.A. et al. Methods and open-source toolkit for analyzing and visualizing challenge results. Sci Rep 11, 2369 (2021).

pages.title: Q&A
pages.content:
## Common Questions

Please send emails to chingyuanyu.iec@gmail.com if you have any other questions. 

**Q:  How long can the participation request be approved after sending the signed challenge rule?**

A:  The request will be approved within 2-4 working days if the signed challenge rule document is filled out correctly.

**Q: I'm only interested in the challenge dataset but I do not want to join the challenge. Can I download the dataset without joining the challenge?**

A: Thanks for your interest. To ensure enough submissions, the dataset is only available to participants during the challenge. 

**Q: How many people can form a team？**

A: Each team can have at most 6 people. The authors in your paper should be the same as the team member list.

**Q: I have joined the challenge and downloaded the dataset. Can I quit the challenge?**

A: No! Please respect the signed agreement. If registered participants do not make successful submissions, all the team members will be listed in the dishonest list.

Algorithm-related issues

**Q: Can we use other datasets or pre-trained models to develop the segmentation algorithms?**

A: Yes, but you need to post them in the forum first. 

**Q: Can we manually annotate the unlabeled images to enlarge the training set?**

A: No. Any manual interventions are not allowed. 

**Q: Can we manually select the high-quality pseudo labels for the unlabeled images?**

A: No. Any manual interventions are not allowed. 

**Q: Does the validation submission affects the final ranking?**

A: No.

**Q: During the testing phase, can I modify the methods and the paper?**

A: Yes, you can make modifications before the testing submission. After making the testing submission, you cannot make modifications.


**Q: Can I use validation set for model pre-training?**
A: No!

**Q: Can I use other public datasets for model pre-training?** 
A: Yes, but you should post the dataset link on forum and also need to submit an algorithm that is trained only with the challenge dataset. 

**Q: When can I access the testing set?**
A: The testing set will not be released during the challenge to avoid data leakage.
pages.content_zh:
## 常见问题

如有其他问题，请发送邮件至 chingyuanyu.iec@gmail.com。

**问：发送签署的挑战规则后，参赛请求需要多长时间才能批准？**

答：如果签署的挑战规则文件填写正确，请求将在 2-4 个工作日内批准。

**问：我只对挑战数据集感兴趣，但不想参加挑战。我可以不参加挑战直接下载数据集吗？**

答：感谢您的兴趣。为了确保有足够的提交，数据集仅在挑战期间向参赛者提供。

**问：一个团队最多可以由多少人组成？**

答：每个团队最多可以有 6 人。论文中的作者应与团队成员列表相同。

**问：我已经加入了挑战并下载了数据集。我可以退出挑战吗？**

答：不可以！请尊重已签署的协议。如果注册的参赛者没有成功提交，所有团队成员将被列入不诚实名单。

算法相关问题

**问：我们可以使用其他数据集或预训练模型来开发分割算法吗？**

答：可以，但您需要首先在论坛上发布它们。

**问：我们可以手动标注未标记的图像来扩大训练集吗？**

答：不可以。不允许任何手动干预。

**问：我们可以为未标记的图像手动选择高质量的伪标签吗？**

答：不可以。不允许任何手动干预。

**问：验证提交是否影响最终排名？**

答：不影响。

**问：在测试阶段，我可以修改方法和论文吗？**

答：可以，您可以在测试提交之前进行修改。进行测试提交后，您无法进行修改。

**问：我可以使用验证集进行模型预训练吗？**

答：不可以！

**问：我可以使用其他公共数据集进行模型预训练吗？**

答：可以，但您应在论坛上发布数据集链接，同时还需要提交仅使用挑战数据集训练的算法。

**问：我何时可以访问测试集？**

答：为了避免数据泄露，挑战期间不会发布测试集。

14290.title: AMPLIFAI CHALLENGE
14290.description:
Annotated Multi-Phase Liver Imaging For Artificial Intelligence.
The AMPLIFAI challenge focuses on automated characterization of hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) lesions in multi-phase abdominal CT using standardized, clinical imaging-based LI-RADS categories.
14290.description_zh:
人工智能用多期肝脏成像注释数据

AMPLIFAI挑战赛聚焦于使用标准化的、基于临床成像的LI-RADS分类，对多期腹部CT中肝细胞癌(HCC)病灶进行自动表征。

pages.title: Overview
pages.content: ![](https://ihc.umd.edu/wp-content/uploads/2022/05/IHC-Horizontal-Logo-for-header.svg) ![](https:(https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/7/7c/University_of_Maryland_Medical_Center_logo.svg/960px-University_of_Maryland_Medical_Center_logo.svg.png) ## Welcome Welcome to the **Annotated Multi-Phase Liver Imaging For Artificial Intelligence (AMPLIFAI)** challenge. AMPLIFAI is a MICCAI 2026 challenge focused on automated characterization of hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) lesions in multi-phase abdominal CT. Participants are tasked with classifying liver lesions according to the LI-RADS scoring system: a standardized, imaging-based framework used clinically to stratify the probability of HCC without requiring tissue biopsy. ## What is HCC? Hepatocellular carcinoma is the most common primary liver cancer and a leading cause of cancer-related mortality worldwide. It arises predominantly in patients with chronic liver disease or cirrhosis, a population that undergoes routine surveillance imaging, making CT a natural screening tool. Crucially, HCC is one of the few malignancies where diagnosis can be established entirely from imaging, no biopsy required. This is codified in the LI-RADS (Liver Imaging Reporting and Data System) framework, which assigns lesions a category from LR-1 ("definitely benign") to LR-5 ("definitely HCC") based on morphology and dynamic contrast enhancement patterns across multiple CT phases. An LR-5 designation alone is sufficient for clinical diagnosis and treatment planning. ## LI-RADS Framework The [LI-RADS](https://radiologyassistant.nl/abdomen/liver/li-rads) framework follows a strict clinical logic consisting of temporal analysis in multi-phase CT, where multiple contrast phases are acquired: before contrast injection (non-contrast) and at successive time points after injection (arterial, portal venous, and delayed) to capture dynamic enhancement patterns in the lesion. The primary gating feature is arterial phase hyperenhancement (APHE), where the lesion appears brighter than the surrounding healthy liver tissue in the early arterial phase. It is considered “non-rim” if the enhancement spreads internally and is not limited to the outer edge of the lesion. Only lesions exhibiting unequivocal non-rim APHE are considered for the highest probability categories (LR-3 to LR-5). If non-rim APHE is present, the LI-RADS category is determined by a combination of the lesion’s size and how non-rim APHE interacts with two major features: First, non-peripheral washout, where the lesion becomes darker than the liver in the portal venous or delayed phases. Second, enhancing capsule, where a thin, peripheral rim remains enhanced in the portal venous or delayed phases due to contrast retention. The lesion size and presence of one or both features increases the probability for HCC. For example, a lesion with non-rim APHE that is 2.3 cm and has one additional feature (washout or capsule) is categorized as LR-5 (“definitely HCC”) while a lesion without non-rim APHE can only reach a maximum of LR-4 (“probable HCC”), even if it is large and shows washout. ## Challenge Aim By benchmarking models against this clinically validated, multi-class diagnostic framework, AMPLIFAI aims to accelerate the development of trustworthy and interpretable AI systems for liver cancer diagnosis, helping bridge the gap between algorithmic performance and real-world clinical deployment. Participants will be provided with training data consisting of multi-phase CT volumes, lesion-level LI-RADS category labels, and voxel-level segmentations. Models will be evaluated solely on their ability to predict the correct LI-RADS category, enabling a focus on robust and clinically relevant classification performance.
pages.content_zh:
# 欢迎

欢迎来到**标注型多阶段肝脏影像用于人工智能(AMPLIFAI)**挑战赛。AMPLIFAI是一项MICCAI 2026挑战赛，专注于多阶段腹部CT中肝细胞癌(HCC)病灶的自动表征。参赛者的任务是根据LI-RADS评分系统对肝脏病灶进行分类：这是一套临床应用的标准化影像框架，用于评估HCC的概率，无需组织活检。

## 什么是HCC?

肝细胞癌是最常见的原发性肝癌，也是全球癌症相关死亡的主要原因。它主要发生在慢性肝病或肝硬化患者中，这类患者需要定期进行监测影像检查，使CT成为一种天然的筛查工具。至关重要的是，HCC是少数几种可以完全通过影像学确诊的恶性肿瘤之一，无需活检。这在LI-RADS(肝脏影像报告和数据系统)框架中有明确规定，该框架根据病灶形态和多个CT阶段的动态对比增强模式，将病灶分为LR-1("明确良性")到LR-5("明确HCC")等级。单独的LR-5等级足以用于临床诊断和治疗规划。

## LI-RADS框架

LI-RADS框架遵循严格的临床逻辑，包括多阶段CT的时间序列分析，其中采集多个对比阶段：注射对比剂前(非对比)和注射后的连续时间点(动脉期、门脉期和延迟期)，以捕捉病灶的动态增强模式。主要评判特征是动脉期高增强(APHE)，病灶在早期动脉期显示比周围健康肝组织更亮。如果增强在内部扩散且不局限于病灶外边缘，则称为"非边缘型"。只有表现出明确非边缘型APHE的病灶才能被考虑用于最高概率等级(LR-3至LR-5)。

如果存在非边缘型APHE，LI-RADS等级由病灶大小与非边缘型APHE与两个主要特征的相互作用的组合决定：第一，非周边廓清，病灶在门脉期或延迟期变暗，比肝脏暗。第二，增强型包膜，薄的周边缘由于对比剂滞留在门脉期或延迟期保持增强。病灶大小和一个或两个特征的存在增加了HCC的概率。例如，一个2.3厘米、具有非边缘型APHE且有一个额外特征(廓清或包膜)的病灶被分类为LR-5("明确HCC")，而没有非边缘型APHE的病灶最多只能达到LR-4("可能HCC")，即使它很大且表现出廓清。

## 挑战赛目标

通过根据这个经过临床验证的多分类诊断框架对模型进行基准测试，AMPLIFAI旨在加速开发可信且可解释的肝癌诊断AI系统，帮助缩小算法性能与真实临床应用之间的差距。

参赛者将获得由多阶段CT体积数据、病灶级LI-RADS分类标签和体素级分割组成的训练数据。模型将仅根据其预测正确LI-RADS等级的能力进行评估，有利于专注于稳健且临床相关的分类性能。

Pariticpiating Teams must register [here](https://forms.office.com/Pages/ResponsePage.aspx?id=pglwcd4gGkaIlAMSo5XKyWO--qpY16BHkqFYmo-jrRdURDM5R0c0VkZRMTM4S1lDQlo2MVZDT1VXTy4u), agree to the AMPLIFAI [Terms](https://github.com/UM2ii/amplifai-challenge/blob/main/terms-and-conditions.pdf) section, and apply for registration on the Codabench platform.
