pages.title: Challenge Data
pages.content:
## Challenge Data
The training and test datasets consist of multi-phase contrast-enhanced CT scans. All cases are annotated by three board-certified abdominal subspecialty radiologists, providing lesion-level LI-RADS category assignments and voxel-level segmentations for non-rim APHE, non-peripheral washout, and enhancing capsule. A case refers to a single patient containing all four contrast phases: non-contrast, arterial, portal venous, and delayed. All CT volumes and segmentations are provided in NIfTI format.

## Dataset Summary
#### Split - Cases
* Training Set - 601Cases
* Validation Set - 67 Cases
* Test Set (Private) - 257 Cases

The validation set comprises approximately 10% of the public data and is constructed to ensure equitable representation across all contributing datasets.

## Training and Validation Data
The public training and validation dataset was curated from four publicly accessible datasets and re-annotated for this challenge. The contributing datasets and their source institutions are listed below.

#### Dataset - Institution
* WAW-TACE - Medical University of Warsaw
* HCC-TACE-SEG - The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center
* TCGA-LIHC - Mayo Clinic, Rochester; University of North Carolina, Chapel Hill; Alberta Health Services; Lahey Hospital & Medical Center
* PLC-CECT - Chongqing Yubei District People's Hospital

Cases from contributing datasets that included existing annotations were partially pre-annotated; however, all training and validation cases were fully re-annotated by three board-certified radiologists following the challenge protocol.

## Test Data
The held-out test set was curated within the University of Maryland Medical System and consists of 257 cases acquired between February 2023 and December 2025, drawn from three different imaging centers within the system. It was annotated following the same protocol as the training data. The test set is private and will not be released publicly; no additional information is provided to ensure the integrity of the challenge.

## Annotation Protocol
All studies were randomly shuffled and pre-loaded into a custom 3D Slicer module prior to annotation. Board-certified abdominal subspecialty radiologists used this module to manually segment liver lesions and record LI-RADS category assignments and feature-level annotations using standard Slicer tools integrated into the custom interface. A brief onboarding session demonstrated the workflow and annotation fields, after which the same protocol was applied uniformly across all dataset splits. Radiologists were blinded to dataset splits, and the same annotation protocol was applied uniformly across training, validation, and test cases.

Rather than algorithmically merging annotations, a structured cross-validation review was performed among the three radiologists: each radiologist's annotations were independently reviewed and corrected by the other two. Segmentations and labels were revised as needed during this process, and the consensus-validated result was treated as the final reference standard. This review procedure was applied consistently across training, validation, and test cases.
pages.content_zh:
## 挑战数据
训练和测试数据集由多期增强CT扫描组成。所有病例均由三名经过认证的腹部亚专科放射科医师标注，提供病灶级的LI-RADS分类和体素级的分割，包括非边缘动脉期肝特异性增强（non-rim APHE）、非周边延迟期廓清（non-peripheral washout）和增强包膜（enhancing capsule）。一个病例指单个患者的全部四个对比增强相位：非增强相、动脉相、门静脉相和延迟相。所有CT体积数据和分割结果以NIfTI格式提供。

## 数据集概况
#### 分割 - 病例数
* 训练集 - 601个病例
* 验证集 - 67个病例
* 测试集（私有） - 257个病例

验证集约占公开数据的10%，经过设计以确保所有贡献数据集的公平表示。

## 训练和验证数据
公开的训练和验证数据集由四个公开可获得的数据集合并而成，并针对本挑战进行了重新标注。贡献数据集及其来源机构如下列表所示。

#### 数据集 - 机构
* WAW-TACE - 华沙医科大学
* HCC-TACE-SEG - 德州大学MD安德森癌症中心
* TCGA-LIHC - 梅奥诊所罗切斯特分院；北卡罗来纳大学教堂山分校；艾伯塔卫生服务局；拉海医院与医疗中心
* PLC-CECT - 重庆渝北区人民医院

来自贡献数据集且包含现有标注的病例经过部分预标注；但所有训练和验证病例均由三名经过认证的放射科医师按照挑战协议完整重新标注。

## 测试数据
保留的测试集由马里兰大学医学系统内部策划，包含257个病例，采集时间为2023年2月至2025年12月，来自该系统内三个不同的成像中心。测试集按照与训练数据相同的协议进行了标注。测试集为私有数据，不会公开发布；为了确保挑战的完整性，不提供额外信息。

## 标注协议
所有研究均被随机打乱并预加载到自定义的3D Slicer模块中供标注使用。经过认证的腹部亚专科放射科医师使用此模块手动分割肝脏病灶，并使用集成到自定义界面中的标准Slicer工具记录LI-RADS分类、病灶级标注和特征级标注。放射科医师被隐瞒数据集分割信息，训练、验证和测试病例应用统一的标注协议。

而非采用算法方式合并标注，采用了结构化的交叉验证审查流程：三名放射科医师中的每一位的标注均由其他两位独立审查和修正。在此过程中根据需要修订分割结果和标签，共识验证的结果被视为最终参考标准。此审查程序在训练、验证和测试病例中一致应用。

pages.content: To be posted.

pages.title: Evaluation
pages.content:
## How will submissions be evaluated?
Submissions will be ranked using Quadratic Weighted Kappa (QWK), a metric designed for ordinal classification that measures agreement between predicted and ground truth LI-RADS categories while penalizing larger disagreements more heavily. This is clinically appropriate: predicting LR-4 for a true LR-5 lesion is a less severe error than predicting LR-2.

The five ordinal categories considered are LR-1 ("definitely benign"), LR-2 ("probably benign"), LR-3 ("intermediate probability"), LR-4 ("probably HCC"), and LR-5 ("definitely HCC"). Lesions classified as LR-M are excluded from this task to preserve the ordinal structure of the classification problem.

QWK is the primary ranking metric; additional metrics will be reported for diagnostic purposes only and will not affect leaderboard standings. All submitted algorithms must produce a LI-RADS prediction for the single pre-defined target lesion in every test case. If a valid prediction is not generated for a given case, that case is excluded from the QWK calculation and a completion rate - defined as the percentage of test cases with valid predictions - is reported separately. Algorithms falling below 95% completion may be deemed non-compliant and excluded from final rankings and awards.
pages.content_zh:
## 提交内容如何评估？

提交的内容将使用二次加权卡帕系数（QWK）进行排名，这是一种为有序分类设计的指标，用于衡量预测的LI-RADS类别与真实标签之间的一致性，并对较大的偏差进行更严重的惩罚。这在临床上是合适的：将真实的LR-5病变预测为LR-4的错误比预测为LR-2的错误更为轻微。

考虑的五个有序类别为LR-1（"明确良性"）、LR-2（"可能良性"）、LR-3（"中等概率"）、LR-4（"可能HCC"）和LR-5（"明确HCC"）。分类为LR-M的病变被排除在本任务之外，以保持分类问题的有序结构。

QWK是主要排名指标；其他指标仅用于诊断目的，不影响排行榜排名。所有提交的算法必须为每个测试用例中预先定义的单个目标病变生成LI-RADS预测。如果某个用例未生成有效预测，该用例将被排除在QWK计算之外，并单独报告完成率（定义为具有有效预测的测试用例的百分比）。完成率低于95%的算法可能被认定为不符合要求，并被排除在最终排名和奖项之外。

14248.title: OncoSegment - Breast Tumor Mapping
14248.description: Segment benign and malignant tumors in breast ultrasound images.
14248.description_zh: 在乳腺超声图像中分割良性和恶性肿瘤。

pages.title: Description
pages.content:
# 🩺 
# **OncoSegment: Breast Tumor Mapping Challenge**

# 💡 
## **Why this Problem is Interesting**

Breast cancer remains one of the leading causes of death among women worldwide. Early and accurate detection through ultrasound is critical, but interpreting these images is notoriously difficult.Ultrasound images suffer from "speckle noise" and low contrast. Much like the "blobs" in plasma physics simulations, tumors in ultrasound are intermittent, have irregular boundaries, and can be easily confused with healthy tissue. This challenge asks you to develop an AI that can precisely outline tumor boundaries, a task that directly assists radiologists in biopsy planning and treatment.

# 📊 
## **The Data**

The dataset is derived from the Breast Ultrasound Images (BUSI) collection, featuring women aged 25–75. It consists of high-quality PNG scans with a standard resolution (approx. 500x500 px).

Dataset Breakdown:

Benign: Tumors that are typically oval or round with smooth edges.

Malignant: Cancerous tumors, often characterized by "spiky" (spiculated) or irregular borders that invade surrounding tissue.

Normal: Scans with no tumors (used to test your model's ability to avoid False Positives).

What you receive in the Starting Kit:
train_images: A list of PIL images (Grayscale) showing the breast 
tissue.
train_masks: Binary masks where 1 represents the tumor and 0 represents the background.
train_labels: Metadata identifying the tumor as benign or malignant.
test_images: Images where you must predict the mask.

 # 📏 
## **Metrics: Measuring Precision**

In medical imaging, a "box" is not enough. We need to know exactly which pixels are cancerous. We use two complementary overlap metrics:
1. Primary Metric: Dice Coefficient (F1-Score)The Dice coefficient is the gold standard for medical segmentation. It is twice the area of overlap divided by the total number of pixels in both the prediction and the ground truth.
$$Dice = \frac{2 \times |P \cap G|}{|P| + |G|}$$
Why? It is highly sensitive to the internal "fullness" of the mask and rewards models that get the shape exactly right.
2. Secondary Metric: Jaccard Index (IoU)
The Jaccard index is the area of intersection divided by the area of union.
$$Jaccard = \frac{|P \cap G|}{|P \cup G|}$$
Why? 
It is more "punishing" than Dice for small errors.
It helps break ties on the leaderboard by highlighting models with the fewest stray False Positive pixels.
pages.content_zh:
# 🩺 
# **OncoSegment：乳腺肿瘤分割挑战赛**

# 💡 
## **为什么这个问题很有趣**

乳腺癌仍然是全球女性主要死亡原因之一。通过超声波进行早期准确检测至关重要，但解读这些图像出了名地困难。超声图像受到"斑点噪声"和低对比度的影响。就像等离子体物理模拟中的"斑点"一样，超声中的肿瘤间歇性出现、边界不规则，容易与健康组织混淆。本挑战要求你开发一个能够精确勾勒肿瘤边界的人工智能，这项任务可以直接协助放射科医生进行活检规划和治疗。

# 📊 
## **数据集**

该数据集来自乳腺超声图像（BUSI）集合，包括25-75岁女性的数据。它由标准分辨率（约500x500像素）的高质量PNG扫描图像组成。

数据集分类：

良性肿瘤：通常为卵圆形或圆形，边界光滑的肿瘤。

恶性肿瘤：癌性肿瘤，通常以"尖刺"（分叶状）或不规则边界为特征，会侵入周围组织。

正常扫描：无肿瘤的扫描（用于测试模型避免假阳性的能力）。

启动工具包中包含的内容：
train_images：显示乳腺组织的PIL图像列表（灰度图）。
train_masks：二值掩膜，其中1代表肿瘤，0代表背景。
train_labels：识别肿瘤为良性或恶性的元数据。
test_images：需要预测掩膜的图像。

 # 📏 
## **评估指标：测量精度**

在医学成像中，"框"是不够的。我们需要准确了解哪些像素是癌变的。我们使用两个互补的重叠指标：

1. 主要指标：Dice系数（F1分数）

Dice系数是医学分割的黄金标准。它是重叠区域面积的两倍除以预测和真实标签中的像素总数。

$$Dice = \frac{2 \times |P \cap G|}{|P| + |G|}$$

原因？它对掩膜内部的"完整性"高度敏感，奖励那些形状完全正确的模型。

2. 次要指标：Jaccard指数（IoU）

Jaccard指数是交集面积除以并集面积。

$$Jaccard = \frac{|P \cap G|}{|P \cup G|}$$

原因？

它比Dice对小错误的"惩罚"更严厉。

它通过突出像素误差最少的模型来帮助打破排行榜并列。

## Development phase
Use this phase to iterate on segmentation models for breast ultrasound
images and optimize public Dice/Jaccard metrics.

Private scores remain hidden until the phase closes.

使用此阶段迭代乳腺超声图像分割模型，并优化公开的Dice/Jaccard指标。

12894.title: NTIRE 2026 3D Content Super-Resolution Challenge  - Track 2 Realistic Degradation

pages.title: Overview
pages.content:
NTIRE 2026: 3D Content Super-Resolution Challenge

Important Dates

  • 2026-01-15: Release of training and validation data;
  • 2026-01-30: Validation server online;
  • 2026-03-10: Final test data release, validation server closed;
  • 2026-03-17: Test result submission deadline;
  • 2026-03-17: Fact sheet / code / model submission deadline;
  • 2026-03-19: Test preliminary score release to the participants;
  • 2026-03-22: Challenge paper submission deadline;
  • 2026-03-24: Paper decision notification;
  • 2026-04-08: Camera-ready;
  • 2026-06-18 (TBU): Workshop day;

Introduction

Beyond traditional images and videos, 3D content has gained immense popularity to provide both appearance and geometry information of the scene. Recent years have witnessed exponential surge of 3D contents due to the advances in 3D generation and reconstruction. Nevertheless, current 3D generation models still suffer relatively low quality. For 3D reconstruction, due to diverse constraints in the real world, the observed multi-view images are usually of low resolution, which ultimately degrades the quality of reconstructed 3D content. In applications like AR/VR, increasing the resolution of these 3D contents is highly demanded to provide immersive experience and help to parse the real world.
Recently, remarkable progress has been achieved in image super-resolution (SR) to improve the visual quality of images. To enhance the quality of a 3D content, a straightforward approach is to employ an off-the-shelf image SR model to super-resolve the rendered images. However, this approach ignores the tightly coupled relationship between geometry and appearance information, resulting in multi-view inconsistency and flicker artifacts.
In this challenge, we aim at establishing a benchmark for 3D content SR. We aspire to highlight the specific challenges and research problems faced by 3D content SR. We hope that this challenge could inspire the community to explore the cross area of low-level vision and 3D vision, and ultimately drive technological advancement in emerging applications such as AR/VR.

Challenge Description

The 8th edition of NTIRE: New Trends in Image Restoration and Enhancement workshop will be held on June, 2026 in conjunction with CVPR 2026.
The objective of this challenge is to reconstruct high-resolution (HR) 3D contents from their low-resolution (LR) counterparts. During the development phase, the validation set will be released. The participants can evaluate their models on the validation set by submitting their results to the validation server. Specifically, super-resolved images are rendered under given camera intrinsics and extrinsics and then submitted to the server for online evaluation. During the test phase, the test set will be released, which includes LR 3D content only. Challenge participants should apply their trained models to the LR 3D content to generate super-resolved results. Then, these results are submitted to the server and evaluated by the organizers.
This challenge has two tracks:
  • Track 1: Bicubic Degradation
    • The aim of this track is to obtain super-resolved 3D content under bicubic degradation (Matlab $imresize$ function in bicubic mode).
  • Track 2: Realistic Degradation
    • The aim of this track is to obtain super-resolved 3D content under realistic degradation.

Organizers

  • Longguang Wang (wanglg9@mail.sysu.edu.cn), Sun Yat-sen University
  • Yulan Guo (guoyulan@sysu.edu.cn), Sun Yat-sen University
  • Yingqian Wang (wangyingqian16@nudt.edu.cn), National University of Defense Technology
  • Juncheng Li (cvjunchengli@gmail.com), East China Normal University
  • Sida Peng (pengsida@zju.edu.cn), Zhejiang University
  • Ye Zhang (zhangy2658@mail.sysu.edu.cn), Sun Yat-sen University
  • Radu Timofte (radu.timofte@uni-wuerzburg.de), University of Würzburg

Official Repository

NTIRE 2026: 3D Content Super-Resolution Challenge
pages.content_zh:
NTIRE 2026: 3D内容超分辨率挑战赛

重要时间节点

  • 2026-01-15: 发布训练和验证数据；
  • 2026-01-30: 验证服务器上线；
  • 2026-03-10: 发布最终测试数据，验证服务器关闭；
  • 2026-03-17: 测试结果提交截止时间；
  • 2026-03-17: 信息表/代码/模型提交截止时间；
  • 2026-03-19: 向参赛者发布测试初步成绩；
  • 2026-03-22: 挑战赛论文提交截止时间；
  • 2026-03-24: 论文审稿决议通知；
  • 2026-04-08: 相机就绪版本；
  • 2026-06-18 (待确认): 研讨会日期；

介绍

除了传统的图像和视频外，3D内容因其能提供场景的外观和几何信息而获得了广泛的欢迎。近年来，由于3D生成和重建技术的进步，3D内容呈现出指数级增长。然而，现有的3D生成模型仍然存在质量相对较低的问题。对于3D重建，由于现实世界中存在的多种约束，观察到的多视图图像通常分辨率较低，这最终会降低重建的3D内容的质量。在AR/VR等应用中，提高这些3D内容的分辨率是高度需要的，以提供沉浸式体验并帮助解析真实世界。
近年来，图像超分辨率(SR)在改进图像视觉质量方面取得了显著进展。为了增强3D内容的质量，一个直接的方法是使用现成的图像SR模型来超分辨渲染的图像。然而，这种方法忽略了几何和外观信息之间紧密耦合的关系，导致多视图不一致和闪烁伪影。
在此挑战赛中，我们旨在建立3D内容超分辨率的基准。我们希望突出3D内容超分辨率所面临的具体挑战和研究问题。我们期望此挑战能激励社区探索低级视觉和3D视觉的交叉领域，最终推动AR/VR等新兴应用中的技术进步。

挑战赛描述

第8届NTIRE: 图像恢复与增强新趋势研讨会将于2026年6月与CVPR 2026一起举办。
本挑战赛的目标是从低分辨率(LR) 3D内容重建高分辨率(HR) 3D内容。在开发阶段，将发布验证集。参赛者可以通过将结果提交到验证服务器来评估其模型在验证集上的性能。具体而言，超分辨率图像在给定的相机内参和外参下渲染，然后提交到服务器进行在线评估。在测试阶段，将发布测试集，其中仅包含LR 3D内容。挑战赛参赛者应将其训练的模型应用于LR 3D内容以生成超分辨率结果。然后，这些结果提交到服务器并由组织者进行评估。
本挑战赛有两个赛道：
  • 赛道1：双立方退化
    • 该赛道的目标是在双立方退化(Matlab $imresize$双立方模式函数)下获得超分辨率3D内容。
  • 赛道2：真实退化
    • 该赛道的目标是在真实退化下获得超分辨率3D内容。

组织者

  • 王龙光 (wanglg9@mail.sysu.edu.cn)，中山大学
  • 郭玉兰 (guoyulan@sysu.edu.cn)，中山大学

pages.title: Data
pages.content:
NTIRE 2026: 3D Content Super-Resolution Challenge

Track 1: Bicubic Degradation

(1) Training Set
This challenge does not provide a training set and encourages the participants to employ diverse external datasets, including but not limited to image SR datasets, depth map SR datasets, multi-view datasets, and 3D reconstruction datasets, to improve the quality of a 3D content.

(2) Validation Set
The validation set consists of LR 3D contents (trained using images with a resolution of 342x228, part 1 and part 2) and HR images/depth maps (1368x912) with their corresponding camera poses. The participants can download the validation set to evaluate the performance of their developed models by comparing their super-resolved images/depth maps with the HR images/depth maps. The participants are encouraged to write papers to describe their methods and use the released validation set for performance evaluation.

(3) Test Set
To rank the submitted models, a test set consisting of two 3D scenes is provided. Unlike the validation sets, only LR 3D contents (trained using images with a resolution of 342x228) and a set of camera poses will be released for the test set. The participants must apply their models to the released LR 3D contents and submit their super-resolved images/depth maps (1368x912) at given camera poses to the server.

Track 2: Realistic Degradation

(1) Training Set
This challenge does not provide a training set and encourages the participants to employ diverse external datasets, including but not limited to image SR datasets, depth map SR datasets, multi-view datasets, and 3D reconstruction datasets, to improve the quality of a 3D content.

(2) Validation Set
The validation set consists of LR 3D contents (trained using images with a resolution of 342x228) and HR images/depth maps (1368x912) with their corresponding camera poses. The participants can download the validation set to evaluate the performance of their developed models by comparing their super-resolved images/depth maps with the HR images/depth maps. The participants are encouraged to write papers to describe their methods and use the released validation set for performance evaluation.

(3) Test Set
To rank the submitted models, a test set consisting of two 3D scenes is provided. Unlike the validation sets, only LR 3D contents (trained using images with a resolution of 342x228) and a set of camera poses will be released for the test set. The participants must apply their models to the released LR 3D contents and submit their super-resolved images/depth maps (1368x912) at given camera poses to the server.
pages.content_zh:
NTIRE 2026：3D内容超分辨率挑战赛

赛道1：双三次插值退化

（1）训练集
本挑战赛不提供训练集，鼓励参与者使用多种外部数据集，包括但不限于图像SR数据集、深度图SR数据集、多视图数据集和3D重建数据集，以改进3D内容的质量。

（2）验证集
验证集包含LR 3D内容（使用分辨率为342x228的图像训练，第1部分和第2部分）、HR图像/深度图（1368x912）及其对应的相机姿态。参与者可下载验证集，通过将其超分辨率图像/深度图与HR图像/深度图进行比较来评估所开发模型的性能。鼓励参与者撰写论文来描述其方法，并使用发布的验证集进行性能评估。

（3）测试集
为了对提交的模型进行排名，提供了包含两个3D场景的测试集。与验证集不同，测试集仅发布LR 3D内容（使用分辨率为342x228的图像训练）和一组相机姿态。参与者必须将其模型应用于发布的LR 3D内容，并将超分辨率图像/深度图（1368x912）在给定相机姿态处提交到服务器。

赛道2：真实退化

（1）训练集
本挑战赛不提供训练集，鼓励参与者使用多种外部数据集，包括但不限于图像SR数据集、深度图SR数据集、多视图数据集和3D重建数据集，以改进3D内容的质量。

（2）验证集
验证集包含LR 3D内容（使用分辨率为342x228的图像训练）、HR图像/深度图（1368x912）及其对应的相机姿态。参与者可下载验证集，通过将其超分辨率图像/深度图与HR图像/深度图进行比较来评估所开发模型的性能。鼓励参与者撰写论文来描述其方法，并使用发布的验证集进行性能评估。

（3）测试集
为了对提交的模型进行排名，提供了包含两个3D场景的测试集。与验证集不同，测试集仅发布LR 3D内容（使用分辨率为342x228的图像训练）和一组相机姿态。参与者必须将其模型应用于发布的LR 3D内容，并将超分辨率图像/深度图（1368x912）在给定相机姿态处提交到服务器。

pages.title: Evaluation
pages.content:
NTIRE 2026: 3D Content Super-Resolution Challenge

Evaluation

The objective of this challenge is to reconstruct high-resolution (HR) 3D contents from their low-resolution (LR) counterparts. During the development and testing phase, challenge participants will submit the super-resolved results. Example codes to calculate evaluation metrics can be found here.
  • Track 1: Bicubic Degradation
    • Given multi-view images of a scene, bicubic degradation (Matlab $imresize$ function in bicubic mode) is used to generate LR images.
  • Track 2: Realistic Degradation
    • Given multi-view images of a scene, a realistic degradation model consisting of random blur, downsampling, noise, and compression is adopted to synthesize LR images.
For quantitative evaluation, we select M camera poses for each test 3D content to render M images and corresponding depth maps. Then, PSNR is used to evaluate the visual quality, while EPE is employed to assess geometry accuracy. The final score for ranking is calculated as:

Submission

During the development phase, participants can submit their results on the validation set to get feedback from the Codabench server. During the test phase, participants will submit the whole results of the test set. This should match the last submission to Codabench.
The submitted results should contain images and depth maps (1368x912) rendered from the super-resolved 3D content under given camera intrinsics and extrinsics. Note that, the intrinsics and extrinsics file can be found here (validation poses and test poses), and the camera indices in the following example code should be used for rendering.

When submitting the results, create a ZIP archive containing all the resulting images and depth maps. The terminology of the rendered images and depth maps should be identical to that of the camera intrinsics and extrinsics in the validation and test sets. A valid example dummy submission file can be found here.
After the test phase, the participants will 1) fill in an online form and 2) submit a zip file (containing fact sheet, source code, and final results, an example of fact sheet can be found here) to the official submission account (ntire.3dsr@outlook.com) by email. The final submission should be made by the following rules:
  • The submitted results must be from the same method that generated the last submission to the Codabench. We will check the consistency. Otherwise, the submission is invalid.
  • Both the testing source code (or executable) and the training code must be submitted. Reproducibility is a necessary condition.
  • Factsheet describing the method details should be submitted together. The factsheet format is provided with data. Participants must submit a compiled pdf file and the tex source. We encourage participants to provide details and enclose figure image sources together. This helps writing the challenge summary report.
Each participating team in the final testing phase should use the provided factsheet template to describe their solution(s).

Email submission format

Please use the following format to submit your final results, fact sheet, code, and model (with trained parameters). We will run the test code to reproduce the results. The code and the model will be posted on the NTIRE 2026 website.

to: ntire.3dsr@outlook.com;
cc: your_team_members
title: [NTIRE 2026 3D Content Super-Resolution Challenge (Track *)] - [Team_name]
body should include:
1) the challenge name (including track id)
2) team name
3) team leader's name, affiliation, and email address
4) team members' names, affiliations, and email addresses
5) user name on the NTIRE 2026 Codabench leaderboard (if any)
6) executable/source code attached or download links.
7) fact sheet attached
8) download link to the results

It should be noted that, the top ranking participants should publicly release their code (or executables) under a license of their choice, taken among popular OSI-approved licenses (http://opensource.org/licenses) and make their code (or executables) online accessible for not less than one year following the end of the challenge (applies only for top three ranked participants of the competition).

Group number policy

Each group cannot have more than six group members (i.e., 1 to 6 group members is OK), and each paricipant can only join one group. Each group can only submit one algorithm for final ranking.
pages.content_zh:
NTIRE 2026：3D内容超分辨率挑战赛

评估

本挑战赛的目标是从低分辨率（LR）的3D内容重建高分辨率（HR）的3D内容。在开发和测试阶段，挑战赛参与者需要提交超分辨率处理后的结果。计算评估指标的示例代码可以在此处找到。
  • 赛道1：双三次插值降质
    • 给定场景的多视图图像，使用双三次插值降质（Matlab中$imresize$函数的双三次模式）生成LR图像。
  • 赛道2：真实降质
    • 给定场景的多视图图像，采用包含随机模糊、下采样、噪声和压缩的真实降质模型来合成LR图像。
对于定量评估，我们为每个测试3D内容选择M个相机位姿来渲染M张图像和对应的深度图。然后，使用PSNR评估视觉质量，而EPE用于评估几何精度。最终排名分数计算方式如下：

提交

在开发阶段，参与者可以在验证集上提交结果以获得Codabench服务器的反馈。在测试阶段，参与者将提交测试集的全部结果。这应该与最后一次向Codabench的提交相匹配。
提交的结果应包含在给定的相机内参和外参下从超分辨率3D内容渲染的图像和深度图（1368x912）。注意，内参和外参文件可以在此处找到（验证位姿和测试位姿），以下示例代码中的相机索引应用于渲染。

提交结果时，创建一个ZIP压缩包，包含所有生成的图像和深度图。渲染图像和深度图的命名应与验证集和测试集中的相机内参和外参命名相同。有效的虚拟提交示例文件可以在此处找到。
测试阶段结束后，参与者需要1）填写在线表格，以及2）向官方提交账户（ntire.3dsr@outlook.com）通过电子邮件提交一个ZIP压缩包（包含事实表、源代码和最终结果，事实表示例可以在此处找到）。最终提交应遵循以下规则：
  • 提交的结果必须来自生成Codabench最后一次提交的同一方法。我们将检查一致性。否则提交无效。
  • 必须提交测试源代码（或可执行文件）和训练代码。可重现性是必要条件。
  • 应一起提交描述方法细节的事实表。事实表格式随数据提供。参与者必须提交已编译的PDF文件和TeX源文件。我们鼓励参与者提供详细信息并附带图像源文件。这有助于撰写挑战赛总结报告。
最终测试阶段的每个参与团队应使用提供的事实表模板来描述其解决方案。

电子邮件

pages.title: Overview
pages.content:
NTIRE 2026: 3D Content Super-Resolution Challenge

Important Dates

  • 2026-01-15: Release of training and validation data;
  • 2026-01-30: Validation server online;
  • 2026-03-10: Final test data release, validation server closed;
  • 2026-03-17: Test result submission deadline;
  • 2026-03-17: Fact sheet / code / model submission deadline;
  • 2026-03-19: Test preliminary score release to the participants;
  • 2026-03-22: Challenge paper submission deadline;
  • 2026-03-24: Paper decision notification;
  • 2026-04-08: Camera-ready;
  • 2026-06-18 (TBU): Workshop day;

Introduction

Beyond traditional images and videos, 3D content has gained immense popularity to provide both appearance and geometry information of the scene. Recent years have witnessed exponential surge of 3D contents due to the advances in 3D generation and reconstruction. Nevertheless, current 3D generation models still suffer relatively low quality. For 3D reconstruction, due to diverse constraints in the real world, the observed multi-view images are usually of low resolution, which ultimately degrades the quality of reconstructed 3D content. In applications like AR/VR, increasing the resolution of these 3D contents is highly demanded to provide immersive experience and help to parse the real world.
Recently, remarkable progress has been achieved in image super-resolution (SR) to improve the visual quality of images. To enhance the quality of a 3D content, a straightforward approach is to employ an off-the-shelf image SR model to super-resolve the rendered images. However, this approach ignores the tightly coupled relationship between geometry and appearance information, resulting in multi-view inconsistency and flicker artifacts.
In this challenge, we aim at establishing a benchmark for 3D content SR. We aspire to highlight the specific challenges and research problems faced by 3D content SR. We hope that this challenge could inspire the community to explore the cross area of low-level vision and 3D vision, and ultimately drive technological advancement in emerging applications such as AR/VR.

Challenge Description

The 8th edition of NTIRE: New Trends in Image Restoration and Enhancement workshop will be held on June, 2026 in conjunction with CVPR 2026.
The objective of this challenge is to reconstruct high-resolution (HR) 3D contents from their low-resolution (LR) counterparts. During the development phase, the validation set will be released. The participants can evaluate their models on the validation set by submitting their results to the validation server. Specifically, super-resolved images are rendered under given camera intrinsics and extrinsics and then submitted to the server for online evaluation. During the test phase, the test set will be released, which includes LR 3D content only. Challenge participants should apply their trained models to the LR 3D content to generate super-resolved results. Then, these results are submitted to the server and evaluated by the organizers.
This challenge has two tracks:
  • Track 1: Bicubic Degradation
    • The aim of this track is to obtain super-resolved 3D content under bicubic degradation (Matlab $imresize$ function in bicubic mode).
  • Track 2: Realistic Degradation
    • The aim of this track is to obtain super-resolved 3D content under realistic degradation.

Organizers

  • Longguang Wang (wanglg9@mail.sysu.edu.cn), Sun Yat-sen University
  • Yulan Guo (guoyulan@sysu.edu.cn), Sun Yat-sen University
  • Yingqian Wang (wangyingqian16@nudt.edu.cn), National University of Defense Technology
  • Juncheng Li (cvjunchengli@gmail.com), East China Normal University
  • Sida Peng (pengsida@zju.edu.cn), Zhejiang University
  • Ye Zhang (zhangy2658@mail.sysu.edu.cn), Sun Yat-sen University
  • Radu Timofte (radu.timofte@uni-wuerzburg.de), University of Würzburg

Official Repository

验证集包含低分辨率3D内容（使用分辨率为342x228的图像训练，第1部分和第2部分）以及高分辨率图像/深度图（1368x912）和对应的相机位姿。参赛者可以下载验证集，通过将其超分辨率图像/深度图与高分辨率图像/深度图进行对比来评估所开发模型的性能。鼓励参赛者撰写论文来描述其方法，并使用发布的验证集进行性能评估。

（3）测试集
为了对提交的模型进行排名，提供了包含两个3D场景的测试集。与验证集不同，测试集仅发布低分辨率3D内容（使用分辨率为342x228的图像训练）和一组相机位姿。参赛者必须将其模型应用于发布的低分辨率3D内容，并将在给定相机位姿下超分辨率后的图像/深度图（1368x912）提交到服务器。

赛道2：真实图像降质

（1）训练集
本挑战赛不提供训练集，鼓励参赛者使用多样化的外部数据集，包括但不限于图像超分辨率数据集、深度图超分辨率数据集、多视图数据集和3D重建数据集，以改进3D内容的质量。

（2）验证集
验证集包含低分辨率3D内容（使用分辨率为342x228的图像训练）以及高分辨率图像/深度图（1368x912）和对应的相机位姿。参赛者可以下载验证集，通过将其超分辨率图像/深度图与高分辨率图像/深度图进行对比来评估所开发模型的性能。鼓励参赛者撰写论文来描述其方法，并使用发布的验证集进行性能评估。

（3）测试集
为了对提交的模型进行排名，提供了包含两个3D场景的测试集。与验证集不同，测试集仅发布低分辨率3D内容（使用分辨率为342x228的图像训练）和一组相机位姿。参赛者必须将其模型应用于发布的低分辨率3D内容，并将在给定相机位姿下超分辨率后的图像/深度图（1368x912）提交到服务器。

pages.title: Evaluation
pages.content:
NTIRE 2026: 3D Content Super-Resolution Challenge

Evaluation

The objective of this challenge is to reconstruct high-resolution (HR) 3D contents from their low-resolution (LR) counterparts. During the development and testing phase, challenge participants will submit the super-resolved results. Example codes to calculate evaluation metrics can be found here.
  • Track 1: Bicubic Degradation
    • Given multi-view images of a scene, bicubic degradation (Matlab $imresize$ function in bicubic mode) is used to generate LR images.
  • Track 2: Realistic Degradation
    • Given multi-view images of a scene, a realistic degradation model consisting of random blur, downsampling, noise, and compression is adopted to synthesize LR images.
For quantitative evaluation, we select M camera poses for each test 3D content to render M images and corresponding depth maps. Then, PSNR is used to evaluate the visual quality, while EPE is employed to assess geometry accuracy. The final score for ranking is calculated as:

Submission

During the development phase, participants can submit their results on the validation set to get feedback from the Codabench server. During the test phase, participants will submit the whole results of the test set. This should match the last submission to Codabench.
The submitted results should contain images and depth maps (1368x912) rendered from the super-resolved 3D content under given camera intrinsics and extrinsics. Note that, the intrinsics and extrinsics file can be found here (validation poses and test poses), and the camera indices in the following example code should be used for rendering.

When submitting the results, create a ZIP archive containing all the resulting images and depth maps. The terminology of the rendered images and depth maps should be identical to that of the camera intrinsics and extrinsics in the validation and test sets. A valid example dummy submission file can be found here.
After the test phase, the participants will 1) fill in an online form and 2) submit a zip file (containing fact sheet, source code, and final results, an example of fact sheet can be found here) to the official submission account (ntire.3dsr@outlook.com) by email. The final submission should be made by the following rules:
  • The submitted results must be from the same method that generated the last submission to the Codabench. We will check the consistency. Otherwise, the submission is invalid.
  • Both the testing source code (or executable) and the training code must be submitted. Reproducibility is a necessary condition.
  • Factsheet describing the method details should be submitted together. The factsheet format is provided with data. Participants must submit a compiled pdf file and the tex source. We encourage participants to provide details and enclose figure image sources together. This helps writing the challenge summary report.
Each participating team in the final testing phase should use the provided factsheet template to describe their solution(s).

Email submission format

Please use the following format to submit your final results, fact sheet, code, and model (with trained parameters). We will run the test code to reproduce the results. The code and the model will be posted on the NTIRE 2026 website.

to: ntire.3dsr@outlook.com;
cc: your_team_members
title: [NTIRE 2026 3D Content Super-Resolution Challenge (Track *)] - [Team_name]
body should include:
1) the challenge name (including track id)
2) team name
3) team leader's name, affiliation, and email address
4) team members' names, affiliations, and email addresses
5) user name on the NTIRE 2026 Codabench leaderboard (if any)
6) executable/source code attached or download links.
7) fact sheet attached
8) download link to the results

It should be noted that, the top ranking participants should publicly release their code (or executables) under a license of their choice, taken among popular OSI-approved licenses (http://opensource.org/licenses) and make their code (or executables) online accessible for not less than one year following the end of the challenge (applies only for top three ranked participants of the competition).

Group number policy

Each group cannot have more than six group members (i.e., 1 to 6 group members is OK), and each paricipant can only join one group. Each group can only submit one algorithm for final ranking.
pages.content_zh:
NTIRE 2026：3D内容超分辨率挑战赛

评估

本挑战赛的目标是从低分辨率(LR)的3D内容重建高分辨率(HR)的3D内容。在开发和测试阶段，挑战赛参与者需要提交超分辨率处理的结果。计算评估指标的示例代码可以在此处找到。
  • 赛道1：双三次插值降质
    • 给定场景的多视图图像，使用双三次插值降质(Matlab中的$imresize$函数的双三次模式)生成低分辨率图像。
  • 赛道2：真实降质
    • 给定场景的多视图图像，采用由随机模糊、下采样、噪声和压缩组成的真实降质模型合成低分辨率图像。
对于定量评估，我们为每个测试3D内容选择M个相机姿态以渲染M张图像及对应的深度图。随后，PSNR用于评估视觉质量，而EPE用于评估几何精度。最终排名得分计算如下：

提交

在开发阶段，参与者可以在验证集上提交结果以获得Codabench服务器的反馈。在测试阶段，参与者将提交整个测试集的结果。这应与最后一次向Codabench的提交一致。
提交的结果应包含从超分辨率3D内容在给定的相机内参和外参下渲染的图像和深度图(1368x912)。注意，内参和外参文件可在此处找到(验证集姿态和测试集姿态)，以下示例代码中的相机索引应用于渲染。

提交结果时，请创建一个ZIP压缩包，包含所有生成的图像和深度图。渲染图像和深度图的命名应与验证集和测试集中的相机内参和外参命名一致。可以在此处找到一个有效的示例虚拟提交文件。
测试阶段后，参与者需要1)填写在线表格，2)向官方提交账户(ntire.3dsr@outlook.com)发送电子邮件并提交一个ZIP文件(包含说明书、源代码和最终结果，说明书示例可在此处找到)。最终提交应按照以下规则进行：
  • 提交的结果必须来自生成最后一次向Codabench提交的同一方法。我们将检查一致性。否则，提交无效。
  • 必须同时提交测试源代码(或可执行文件)和训练代码。可重复性是必要条件。
  • 应同时提交描述方法细节的说明书。说明书格式随数据提供。参与者必须提交编译后的PDF文件和tex源文件。我们鼓励参与者提供详细信息并附上图像源文件。这将有助于编写挑战赛总结报告。
参加最终测试阶段的每个参与团队应使用提供的说明书模板来描述其解决方案。

电子邮件

pages.title: Overview
pages.content:
## MICCAI STSR 2026 Pre-Task 2D Images Segmentation This task is a prerequisite for STS26. To prevent dataset misuse, upon completion, you will be eligible to access the STS26 main competition dataset. Please submit the model's inference results('data/test/masks.zip') on the 'data/test/imgs/' data to the platform as a proof.(just takes about 1 hour) If there's any question, please contact directly at SemiTeethSegChallenge@outlook.com ## 📖 Project Overview 👌A simple 2D image segmentation task using [U-Net](https://arxiv.org/abs/1505). - **Step1: Download the Pre-Task data and training modal**
To avoid the misuse of our data, you may just finish this simple (very simple) pre-task and fill out the Data Access Agreement at first to get access to the data. Data site:
-[Huggingface](https://huggingface.co/datasets/Ricoooo/MICCAI-STS26-Challenge-Pre-Task)
-[Modelscope](https://modelscope.cn/datasets/lizhii/MICCAI-STS26-Challenge-Pre-Task/summary)
-[Baidu Netdisk(extracting code = bm2u)](https://pan.baidu.com/s/1U090bZnMGEJQaD3jwqaQuA)
-[Google Drive](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1lER9eIavr99g28aTO0kuxIcos_k9FBSx?usp=sharing)
Or you could use the [STS-Skill](https://github.com/ricoleehduu/sts-skill) as when you use Claude Code, like:
"Use STS-Skill https://github.com/ricoleehduu/sts-skill to help me finishing the Pre-Task." - **Step2:** Submit result to the online submission system. - **Step3:** Fill out the registration form and submit the agreements to WPS Form or Google Form:
- [For domestic participants (WPS Form)](https://f.wps.cn/g/VV1UmM0O/)
- [For international participants (Google Form)](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSf4hxOwxQdkIHkSICzlD2xnlAuPWr3EvUeB7oeuRKRPUimAKQ/viewform?usp=publish-editor) ## 💻 Data Structure ``` data/ ├── imgs/ # Training images ├── masks/ # Training masks └── test/ ├── imgs/ # Test images (50 samples) └── masks/ # Where you put your references (50 samples) ``` ## ☑️ Quick Start Code is available at [U-Net](https://arxiv.org/abs/1505) ### Environment Requirements - Python 3.6+ - PyTorch 1.13+ - CUDA (recommended) ### Install Dependencies ```bash pip install -r requirements.txt ``` ### Train Model ```bash python train.py --amp ``` ## 🛠️ Usage ### Training Parameters ```console > python train.py -h usage: train.py [-h] [--epochs E] [--batch-size B] [--learning-rate LR] [--load LOAD] [--scale SCALE] [--validation VAL] [--amp] Train the UNet on images and target masks optional arguments: -h, --help show this help message and exit --epochs E, -e E Number of epochs --batch-size B, -b B Batch size --learning-rate LR, -l LR Learning rate --load LOAD, -f LOAD Load model from a .pth file --scale SCALE, -s SCALE Downscaling factor of the images --validation VAL, -v VAL Percent of the data that is used as validation (0-100) --amp Use mixed precision ``` ### Prediction #### Batch Processing ```bash python predict.py -m checkpoints/checkpoint_epoch1.pth -i data/test/imgs python predict.py -m checkpoints/checkpoint_epoch1.pth -i data/test/imgs -o data/test/masks python predict.py -m checkpoints/checkpoint_epoch1.pth -i data/test/imgs -o data/test/masks -t 0.3 ``` #### Single File ```bash python predict.py -m checkpoints/checkpoint_epoch1.pth -i input.png -o output.png ``` #### Prediction Parameters ```console > python predict.py -h usage: predict.py [-h] --model FILE --input INPUT [INPUT ...] [--output INPUT [INPUT ...]] [--viz] [--no-save] [--mask-threshold MASK_THRESHOLD] [--scale SCALE] [--bilinear] [--classes CLASSES] Predict masks from input images optional arguments: -h, --help show this help message and exit --model FILE, -m FILE Model checkpoint path --input INPUT, -i INPUT Input image(s) or directory --output INPUT, -o INPUT Output image(s) or directory --viz, -v Visualize results --no-save, -n Do not save output masks --mask-threshold, -t Threshold for mask binarization (default: 0.5) --scale, -s Image scale factor (default: 0.5) --bilinear Use bilinear upsampling --classes, -c Number of classes (default: 1) ``` ## ‼️ Submission Format Please make sure there's no extra folder in your ZIP file and the size of the image you predict need to be the same as the original image!! Please keep the pixel value in the range of (0, 255) ``` ─mask.zip ├──A-150.png ├──... └──C-198.png ``` ## ✅ Reference Original paper by Olaf Ronneberger, Philipp Fischer, Thomas Brox: [U-Net: Convolutional Networks for Biomedical Image Segmentation](https://arxiv.org/abs/1505.04597) Original code by milesial: [Pytorch-UNet](https://github.com/milesial/Pytorch-UNet?tab=readme-ov-file#with-docker)
pages.content_zh:
## MICCAI STSR 2026 预赛2D图像分割 该任务是STS26的前置任务。为防止数据滥用，完成后，你将有资格获得STS26主竞赛数据集的访问权限。请将模型在'data/test/imgs/'数据上的推理结果('data/test/masks.zip')提交到平台作为证明。(仅需约1小时) 如有任何问题，请直接联系：SemiTeethSegChallenge@outlook.com ## 📖 项目概述 👌一个使用[U-Net](https://arxiv.org/abs/1505)的简单2D图像分割任务。 - **步骤1：下载预赛数据和训练模型**
为了防止我们数据的滥用，你可以先完成这个简单(非常简单)的预赛任务，并首先填写《数据访问协议》来获得数据访问权限。 数据网站：
-[Huggingface](https://huggingface.co/datasets/Ricoooo/MICCAI-STS26-Challenge-Pre-Task)
-[Modelscope](https://modelscope.cn/datasets/lizhii/MICCAI-STS26-Challenge-Pre-Task/summary)
-[百度网盘(提取码 = bm2u)](https://pan.baidu.com/s/1U090bZnMGEJQaD3jwqaQuA)
-[谷歌云盘](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1lER9eIavr99g28aTO0kuxIcos_k9FBSx?usp=sharing)
或者你可以使用[STS-Skill](https://github.com/ricoleehduu/sts-skill)，就像你使用Claude Code一样，例如：
"使用STS-Skill https://github.com/ricoleehduu/sts-skill 帮我完成预赛。" - **步骤2：** 向在线提交系统提交结果。 - **步骤3：** 填写注册表并将协议提交到WPS表单或谷歌表单：
- [国内参赛者(WPS表单)](https://f.wps.cn/g/VV1UmM0O/)
- [国际参赛者(谷歌表单)](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSf4hxOwxQdkIHkSICzlD2xnlAuPWr3EvUeB7oeuRKRPUimAKQ/viewform?usp=publish-editor) ## 💻 数据结构 ``` data/ ├── imgs/ # 训练图像 ├── masks/ # 训练掩码 └── test/ ├── imgs/ # 测试图像(50个样本) └── masks/ # 放置你的预测结果的位置(50个样本) ``` ## ☑️ 快速开始 代码可在[U-Net](https://arxiv.org/abs/1505)获取 ### 环境要求 - Python 3.6+ - PyTorch 1.13+ - CUDA(推荐) ### 安装依赖 ```bash pip install -r requirements.txt ``` ### 训练模型 ```bash python train.py --amp ``` ## 🛠️ 使用方法 ### 训练参数 ```console > python train.py -h usage: train.py [-h] [--epochs E] [--batch-size B] [--learning-rate LR] [--load LOAD] [--scale SCALE] [--validation VAL] [--amp] 在图像和目标掩码上训练UNet 可选参数： -h, --help 显示此帮助信息并退出 --epochs E, -e E 训练轮数 --batch-size B, -b B 批处理大小 --learning-rate LR, -l LR 学习率 --load LOAD, -f LOAD 从.pth文件加载模型 --scale SCALE, -s SCALE 图像的缩放因子 --validation VAL, -v VAL 用作验证的数据百分比(0-100) --amp 使用混合精度 ``` ### 预测 #### 批处理 ```bash python predict.py -m checkpoints/checkpoint_epoch1.pth -i data/test/imgs python predict.py -m checkpoints/checkpoint_epoch1.pth -i data/test/imgs -o data/test/masks python predict.py -m checkpoints/checkpoint_epoch1.pth -i data/test/imgs -o data/test/masks -t 0.3 ``` #### 单个文件 ```bash python predict.py -m checkpoints/checkpoint_epoch1.pth -i input.png -o output.png ``` #### 预测参数 ```console > python predict.py -h usage: predict.py [-h] --model FILE --input INPUT [INPUT ...] [--output INPUT [INPUT ...]] [--viz] [--no-save] [--mask-threshold MASK_THRESHOLD] [--scale SCALE] [--bilinear] [--classes CLASSES] 从输入图像预测掩码 可选参数： -h, --help 显示此帮助信息并退出 --model FILE, -m FILE 模型检查点
